6. Beat yolks of two eggs, add a little cream, mix with the sauce.
7. Pour the sauce over the veal.
8. Garnish with chopped parsley.

title: Fricassee vasikanlihasta (Veal Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds veal backstrap
- boiling water
- 2 stalks celery
- 1 small carrot
- salt
- onion slices
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- veal broth
- pepper
- lemon juice
- 2 egg yolks
- cream
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wipe and cut 2 pounds veal backstrap into pieces and pour boiling water over them into a kettle.
2. Add 2 stalks celery, 1 small carrot, salt and an onion slice.
3. Simmer gently covered until the meat is tender.
4. Make a sauce from 3 tablespoons butter, 2 tablespoons flour, strained veal broth, salt, pepper and lemon juice.
5. Beat 2 egg yolks, add some cream and add last to the sauce.
6. Pour the sauce over the meat and garnish with fine parsley.

title: Fried Bananas
body:
## Ingredients

- bananas
- salt
- bread crumbs
- egg
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Peel bananas and split in two, sprinkle with a little salt.
2. Dip in bread crumbs, egg and again in bread crumbs.
3. Fry in butter or in lard.
4. Serve very hot.

title: Fried Celery
body:
## Ingredients

- celery roots
- salted water
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs
- butter
- lard
- melted butter
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cook roots of celery in salted water until tender.
2. Cut in thin slices, dip in beaten egg and bread crumbs.
3. Fry with butter in a frying pan or cook in lard.
4. Pour melted butter over and sprinkle with chopped parsley.

title: Fried Chestnuts
body:
## Ingredients

- skinned chestnuts
- butter
- fine salt
- chicken stock

## Directions

1. Cut the skinned chestnuts in two or four pieces
2. Fry quickly in best butter
3. Sprinkle with fine salt
4. Pour chicken stock on
5. Let stand in pan, covered, two minutes

title: Fried Chops
body:
## Ingredients

- chops
- egg
- bread crumbs
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- meat stock
- tomato

## Directions

1. Trim as for frying.
2. Dip in egg and bread crumbs.
3. Fry in butter until brown on both sides.
4. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
5. Put the pan in oven and bake covered for a few minutes.
6. Prepare sauce of the drippings in pan by adding a little flour, meat stock, pepper, salt and tomato.
7. Strain and serve with chops.

title: Fried Chops
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton chops
- egg
- bread crumbs
- butter
- flour
- meat stock
- pepper
- salt
- tomato

## Directions

1. Trim chops
2. Dip in egg and bread crumbs
3. Fry in butter until brown on both sides
4. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
5. Put the pan in oven, bake covered for a few minutes
6. Prepare sauce of the drippings in pan by adding a little flour, meat stock, pepper, salt and tomato
7. Strain and serve with chops

title: Fried Eggplant
body:
## Ingredients

- eggplant
- salt
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs
- fat
- cheese

## Directions

1. Peel eggplant and cut into thin slices.
2. Place slices overlapping, sprinkle a little salt between and let stand under light weight 1/2 to 1 hour.
3. Wipe slices, dip in beaten egg and bread crumbs; fry in hot fat.
4. Cheese may be sprinkled on top if desired.

title: Fried Eggplant
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 eggplant
- salt
- 1 egg
- bread crumbs
- hot fat
- cheese crumbs (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel an eggplant and cut in thin slices
2. Lay the slices on a dish and sprinkle with salt between the slices
3. Press with a slight weight for 1/2 to 1 hour
4. Wipe the slices
5. Dip in egg and bread crumbs
6. Fry in hot fat
7. Sprinkle with cheese crumbs if desired

title: Fried Fish
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- 1 cupful of flour
- 1 1/2 tablespoonfuls of salt
- butter
- cream

## Directions

1. Scale the fish, wash, wipe dry and cut in pieces.
2. Mix 1 cupful of flour with 1 1/2 tablespoonfuls of salt.
3. Roll the pieces of fish in it.
4. Brown the butter in pan and fry the fishes until lightly browned.
5. Cover and put the pan into oven.
6. Bake until done.
7. Pour a little cream into the pan, let boil up and pour on the fish.
8. For fish fried in lard, roll in cracker dust, in eggs and again in cracker-dust.
9. Take care that the fish is completely covered with egg and crackerdust, so that no opening is left for the lard to enter.

title: Fried Hominy
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled hominy
- egg
- bread crumbs
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut the boiled hominy in pieces 1/2 inch thick.
2. Dip in egg and bread crumbs.
3. Fry in lard.

title: Fried Lobster
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled lobster
- cracker crumbs
- egg
- lard
- Tartar sauce

## Directions

1. Shell and clean boiled lobster.
2. Cut the tailmeat in quarters and the meat of claws in halves.
3. Roll in cracker crumbs, in egg and again in cracker crumbs.
4. Cook in lard.
5. Serve with Tartar sauce.

title: Fried Lobster
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled lobster
- cracker crumbs
- egg
- lard

## Directions

1. Shell and clean boiled lobster.
2. Cut the tailmeat in quarters and the meat of claws in halves.
3. Roll in cracker crumbs, in egg and again in cracker crumbs.
4. Cook in lard.
5. Serve with Tartar sauce.

title: Fried Onions
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- salted water
- butter
- salt
- sugar
- meat stock
- water
- beaten egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Peel onions and cook in salted water until half done.
2. Drain off the water, brown in butter, sprinkled with salt and sugar.
3. Mix with one cupful of meat stock or water and let simmer, covered, until tender.

title: Fried Pork
body:
## Ingredients

- ham or bacon
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut ham or bacon into thin slices.
2. Heat a frying-pan, put a bit of butter into the pan, and fry the ham quickly on both sides.
3. Serve with fried eggs or potatoes.

title: Fried Tongue
body:
## Ingredients

- Tongue
- Salt
- Water
- 3 tablespoonfuls butter
- Sliced onion
- Flour
- Carrots
- Celery
- Chopped parsley
- 3 cupfuls tongue liquor
- Chopped mushrooms
- Tomato
- Pepper
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape the tongue carefully.
2. Put in hot salted water and let boil about one hour.
3. Remove skin and roots.
4. Cook 3 tablespoonfuls of butter with sliced onion in a frying pan.
5. Place the tongue in it and fry on both sides.
6. Sprinkle with flour and lay around it carrots and celery cut in pieces and chopped parsley.
7. Pour over it 3 cupfuls of liquor of tongue.
8. Cover tightly and bake 2-3 hours.
9. Lift the tongue on a platter and prepare sauce of liquor by adding flour, stock and 1/2 cupful of tomato.
10. Strain and mix with chopped mushrooms.
11. Return to the fire and cook 5 minutes.
12. Season with salt, pepper and lemon.
13. Place the tongue on a serving dish and pour the sauce over.

title: Fried Tripe
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled tripe
- melted butter
- lemon juice
- egg
- bread crumbs
- butter or lard
- Bearnaise or hot Mayonnaise sauce

## Directions

1. Take pieces of cold boiled tripe
2. Pour over them melted butter mixed with a little lemon juice
3. Let stand for an hour
4. Dip in egg and bread crumbs
5. Fry in butter or lard to a light brown
6. Serve with Bearnaise or hot Mayonnaise sauce

title: Frozen Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of rich cream
- 1/2 cupful of sugar
- stewed fruit
- lady fingers

## Directions

1. Whip cream and sugar to a froth
2. Mix with fruit and macaroons
3. Pour into an individual mold
4. Put to freeze in cracked ice and salt for a few hours
5. Unmold and serve garnished with fruit and macaroons

title: Fruit Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- Oranges
- Bananas
- Grapefruit
- Strawberries
- Cherries
- Melons
- Currants
- Pineapples
- Figs
- Walnuts
- Apples
- Salad oil
- Lemon juice
- Salt
- Paprika

## Directions

1. Use one or several kinds of fruit to prepare a salad
2. Oranges, bananas, grapefruit, strawberries and cherries may be mixed together for salad
3. A fancy salad may be made of melons, currants, strawberries, pineapples, cherries and figs
4. Oranges, bananas, currants and walnuts mixed together; oranges mixed with bananas and apples make a delicious fruit salad
5. Prepare a sauce of 3 tablespoonfuls of salad oil and 1 tablespoonful of lemon juice seasoned with salt and paprika

title: Garibaldin Kakkuja (Garibaldi Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cupfuls of flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar
- 1¼ cupfuls of milk
- 1½ teaspoonfuls of baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix butter well with flour in a bowl
2. Add sugar and baking powder
3. Pour milk slowly into it, stirring carefully
4. Knead the batter on a board
5. Form into round cakes
6. Divide into six parts
7. Brush over with egg
8. Bake in oven for 15–20 minutes
9. Serve hot

title: German Lentils
body:
## Ingredients

- lentils
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- cream
- fried chopped onion

## Directions

1. Cook lentils until soft.
2. Force through a sieve, season with salt, pepper and butter.
3. If the mass is too thick add a little cream.
4. Heap upon a platter.
5. Make a little cavity on top and fill it with 3 tablespoonfuls of fried chopped onion.

title: Ginger Bread (Inkivääri Leipä)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of molasses
- 1/2 cupful of sour cream or milk
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of flour
- 1/2 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 teaspoonful of ginger
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Melt molasses and butter, work to a froth.
2. Add seasonings, cream and flour mixed with baking soda.
3. Bake about 1/2 hour.

title: Ginger Bread (Inkivääri Leipä)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1 cupful of molasses
- 1 cupful of milk
- 1 egg
- 1 heaping tablespoonful of butter
- 2 teaspoonfuls of baking soda
- 1 teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- 3/4 teaspoonful of ginger
- flour enough to make a thin batter, a little thicker than for cakes

## Directions

1. Warm molasses, butter and sugar, rub to a froth.
2. Add beaten egg, milk and flour mixed with cream of tartar and soda.
3. Bake on a shallow pan and cut into squares.

title: Ginger Cake (Inkivääri Kakku)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cupful of butter
- 1 cupful of sugar
- 4 eggs, beaten until light
- 3 cupfuls of flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- 1 1/2 tablespoonfuls of ginger

## Directions

1. Work butter with sugar to a cream.
2. Add beaten eggs.
3. Mix in flour combined with baking powder and ginger.
4. Bake in small molds.

title: Glazed Calf's Head ä la Piquante
body:
## Ingredients

- Cooked calf's head
- Milk sauce
- Toast
- Glazed tomatoes
- Butter
- Flour
- Brown sauce
- Tomato
- Parsley
- Cucumber

## Directions

1. Cut cooked calf's head into pieces
2. Pour a milk sauce over them
3. Place on toast
4. Line with glazed tomatoes
5. Serve with Piquante sauce poured around the meat onto a serving dish
6. Piquante sauce is made of 1 tablespoonful of butter, 1 tablespoonful of flour, 1 cupful of brown sauce, 1 cupful of tomato, chopped parsley, fresh chopped cucumber

title: Glazed Sweet Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- sugar
- lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes, peel and cut lengthwise into halves.
2. Put 2 tablespoonfuls of butter in a frying pan.
3. When the butter is boiling put the potatoes into the pan.
4. Sprinkle with 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar.
5. Fry until brown.
6. Season with lemon juice or vinegar if required.

title: Gold Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cupful of butter
- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1/2 cupful of milk
- yolks of 7 eggs
- 2 cupfuls of flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- juice and grated rind of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Work butter and sugar to a froth
2. Add beaten yolks of eggs, lemon juice and rind, milk and baking powder mixed with flour
3. Bake in a buttered mold in a moderate oven
4. Cover with white frosting

title: Gourd Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- gourd
- vinegar
- sugar (as much as the weight of gourd)
- cloves
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel gourd, split in two and remove seeds.
2. Slice or cut into dice.
3. Cook liquor of vinegar and as much sugar as the weight of gourd.
4. Season with cloves and pepper.
5. Put the gourd in and cook until clear and soft.
6. Place in a glass jar, pour the liquor over, cork and keep in a cold place.

title: Graham Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk or water
- 3 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- 3 tablespoonfuls of molasses
- 2 teaspoonfuls of salt
- 1/2 cake of yeast
- 3 cupfuls of graham flour
- 1 cupful of wheat flour

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough and bake like water bread.

title: Graham Muffensi (Graham Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of graham flour
- 1 cupful of wheat flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 cupful of milk
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- 3 tablespoonfuls of baking powder

## Directions

1. Mix and sift graham flour and wheat flour with baking powder and salt
2. Beat eggs with sugar and milk
3. Pour slowly into the mixture of flour whipping hard
4. Mix with melted butter at the last
5. Put in greased muffin tins
6. Bake nearly 25–30 minutes

title: Graham-leipä (Graham Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint warm milk or water
- 3 tablespoons melted butter
- 4 tablespoons molasses
- 1 1/2 teaspoons salt
- 3/4 or 1 yeast cake
- 3 1/2 cups graham flour
- 1 cup white flour

## Directions

1. Prepare and bake as given for water bread.

title: Graham-muffensia (Graham Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup graham flour
- ½ cup white flour
- ½ teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- ¾ cup milk
- 1 or 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 3 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Sift together the flour, the baking powder and the salt.
2. Beat the eggs with the sugar, add gradually the milk.
3. Last of all mix in the melted butter.
4. Bake for 20 or 30 minutes in buttered muffin tins.

title: Grapefruit (Pompelmus)
body:
## Ingredients

- grapefruit
- sugar
- maraschino cherries

## Directions

1. Peel and skin grapefruit.
2. Sweeten with sugar.
3. Serve ice cold in glasses or in the peels with a maraschino cherry on the top of the fruit.

title: Green Pepper Salad
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 green peppers
- 4 tomatoes
- 4 apples
- lard
- French dressing
- mayonnaise dressing
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Cook green peppers quickly in lard to remove the bitter skin.
2. Skin and cut in very thin, 1 1/2 inch long slices.
3. Mix all together and pour French dressing on.
4. Arrange in a salad bowl.
5. Garnish with lettuce leaves and put mayonnaise dressing with spoon on the leaves.

title: Green Pepper with Sweetbread
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 cupful of chicken stock
- 1 sweetbread
- salt
- pepper
- Worcestershire sauce
- cream
- a few boiled mushrooms
- green peppers
- lard
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare a sauce of butter, flour and chicken stock.
2. Cook 1 sweetbread as usually and cut in pieces, add to the sauce.
3. Season with salt, pepper and Worcestershire sauce.
4. Add a little cream and if wanted, a few boiled mushrooms cut in pieces.
5. Cook green peppers quickly in lard, so as to remove the bitter skin easily.
6. Let cool, fill with the mixture, and sprinkle with bread crumbs.
7. Bake in oven until the surface is browned.
8. Prepare a sauce of butter, flour and chicken stock.
9. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Add lastly one yolk and cream.

title: Green Tomato Pickle
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- 1 quart of small onions
- 1 cupful of salt
- vinegar
- 1 1/2 pounds of brown sugar
- 1/4 pound of mustard seeds
- pepper
- cloves
- allspice
- 1 tablespoonful of each spice

## Directions

1. Cut tomatoes in pieces, peel 1 quart of small onions, sprinkle with 1 cupful of salt, let stand over night.
2. Drain in the morning and put in cold water for a little while.
3. Alternatively, tomatoes may be boiled quickly and strained.
4. Place in boiler and cover with vinegar.
5. Add 1 1/2 pounds of brown sugar, 1/4 pound of mustard seeds, pepper, cloves and allspice, 1 tablespoonful of each.
6. Cook 1/2 hour or until the pickles get soft.
7. Place in jars and preserve as usually.

title: Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of flour
- 1½ cupfuls of milk
- 1-2 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- ½ teaspoonful of baking powder
- 2 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- ½ teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Sift flour with salt and baking powder.
2. Beat eggs with sugar, add milk into it.
3. Pour milk slowly into flour.
4. Lastly mix with melted butter.
5. Drop with spoon on a hot griddle and fry on both sides.
6. Serve with melted butter and maple syrup.

title: Griddle Cakes (Pannukakkuja)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of flour
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of milk
- 1-2 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 1/2 teaspoonful of baking powder
- 2 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Sift flour with salt and baking powder.
2. Beat eggs with sugar, add milk into it.
3. Pour it slowly into flour.
4. Mix with melted butter last.
5. Drop by spoonful into hot buttered pan and bake until brown on both sides.
6. Serve with butter and maple syrup.

title: Griddle-kakkuja (Griddle Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 2 cups milk
- 1 or 2 eggs
- ¼ teaspoon salt
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 1½ teaspoons baking powder
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Sift together all the dry ingredients.
2. Beat the eggs, add the milk and pour gradually in the flour, stirring until smooth.
3. If the batter is too thick add some milk to it.
4. Drop by the spoonful into a hot buttered griddle pan and fry until brown on both sides.
5. Serve with maple syrup or honey.

title: Halibuthyytelö (Halibut à la Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- Halibut
- Aspic jelly

## Directions

1. Prepare the fish and jelly as in the previous recipe.
2. Place a layer of jelly in the mold, place the fish on top of it.

title: Halibuthyytelö (Halibut à la Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish stock
- Egg whites for clarification
- Hard boiled eggs
- Parsley
- Halibut fillets
- Carrots
- Beets
- Mayonnaise
- Whipped cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the fish and the aspic as directed for salmon aspic.
2. Put a layer of aspic on the bottom of the mold.
3. On top of it arrange the fish nicely with carrots and beets cut in cubes.
4. Add a layer of mayonnaise plain or mixed with whipped cream.
5. Continue filling the mold alternately with layers of aspic, fish and mayonnaise.
6. Lay aspic on the top.
7. Let stand for a few hours.
8. Serve garnished with lettuce leaves, tomato and cucumber, etc., having plenty of dressing on.
9. May be prepared without using whites of egg to make the aspic clear.

title: Ham Mousse (Kinkku-muusi)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of ham
- 1/2 lb. of chicken fillet
- whites of 2 eggs
- cream, about 1/2 pint

## Directions

1. Grind the ham with chicken fillet many times.
2. Pound well and add the whites.
3. Pound well again.
4. Force through a sieve.
5. Add gradually about 1/2 pint of cream, or until the mixture is soft enough.
6. Test it by dropping a little dumpling of the mixture in salted boiling water.
7. If too thick, add a little cream; if too thin it may be thickened with white of an egg.

title: Ham Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarts of liquor of ham
- 1 cupful of pearl-barley or barley
- 1 carrot
- 1 small parsnip
- 1/2 root of celery
- 1 onion
- a piece of turnip
- 3 potatoes

## Directions

1. Wash and soak the barley and boil in the liquor of ham until half done.
2. Add the vegetables cut into dice.
3. Then add the potatoes a little later.
4. The soup is done when the vegetables and barley are tender.
5. Add the seasonings and chopped parsley into it and serve with ham or meat cut into cubes in it.
6. If the soup is too salty dilute it with milk.

title: Hampurin haukea (Hamburg Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds pike
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- 2 egg yolks
- cream
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Scale the fish, clean, rinse, wipe and remove the backbone
2. Rub with salt and pepper
3. Moisten with lemon juice, let stand for a while
4. Wipe the fish and spread with melted butter
5. Sprinkle with bread crumbs
6. Bake in a buttered pan to a light brown
7. Remove the fish to a platter
8. Make a gravy of drippings by adding some butter, flour, fish stock, lemon juice
9. Mix with yolks of egg and cream
10. Clean mushrooms and fry in butter, arrange on the fish
11. Or chop the mushrooms, stir into the gravy, pour the gravy over the fish

title: Hampurin haukea (Hamburg Pike)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds pike
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- crackerdust
- 2 egg yolks
- cream
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Scale, clean and rinse the pike and remove the backbone
2. Rub with salt and pepper
3. Moisten with lemon juice and let rest for a while
4. Dry the fish
5. Brush with butter
6. Sprinkle crackerdust on top
7. Fry in a buttered pan in the oven until light brown
8. Place the fish on a serving platter
9. Make sauce from the drippings in the pan by adding butter, flour, fish stock, lemon juice, egg yolks and cream
10. Fry mushrooms in butter in a pan and place on top of the fish or dice them and mix into the sauce
11. Pour the sauce around the fish

title: Hampurin pihvi (Hamburg Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of raw lean meat
- onion juice
- salt and pepper
- 1 egg
- flour

## Directions

1. Chop the meat very finely, removing all membranes as carefully as possible
2. Mix in onion juice, salt, pepper and 1 egg
3. Form the mixture into small flat cakes
4. Dust in flour
5. Fry in butter
6. Make a sauce by adding flour and meat stock to the butter in the pan
7. Season with salt, pepper and lemon juice

title: Hampurin teeleipiä (Hamburg Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 5 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- 1 cup of finely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Beat eggs and sugar well to a froth
2. Mix with almonds, butter and flour
3. Form the batter into strings to resemble fingers
4. Bake on a greased tin sheet until light brown

title: Hampurinpihvi (Hamburg Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound lean beef
- onion juice
- salt
- pepper
- 1 egg
- flour
- butter
- bouillon
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop the beef very fine and remove all the fiber possible.
2. Mix with onion juice, salt, pepper and egg.
3. Form into small patties and roll in flour.
4. Fry in butter in a pan.
5. Prepare a gravy by adding flour and bouillon to the drippings in the pan.
6. Season with salt and pepper and flavor with lemon juice.
7. The patties may also be baked in the oven.

title: Hanhen-Paisti (Roast Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose
- 1 lemon
- 2 tablespoonfuls of salt
- ½ teaspoonful of pepper
- ½ lb. of prunes
- 2 quarts of apples
- butter or lard
- meat stock
- cream

## Directions

1. Clean the goose, wash and wipe with a cloth.
2. Rub inside and outside with lemon.
3. Rub a little salt inside.
4. Wash the fruit and stuff the goose with them.
5. Truss and tie the wing tips and legs together at the back.
6. Spread a piece of butter or lard on the goose, place in a pan and roast in a hot oven, basting frequently with the fat in the pan and adding meat stock to the pan as needed.
7. When the goose is browned, sprinkle salt on top and cover with buttered paper.
8. Allow 2-3 hours for roasting.
9. Place on a platter, arrange the prunes and apples around it.
10. Strain the broth, skim off fat, and add a little cream.

title: Hanhipaisti (Roast Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose
- 1 lemon
- 2 tablespoons salt
- ¼ teaspoon pepper
- ½ pound prunes
- 2 quarts apples
- butter or bacon
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Clean the goose, rub with lemon inside and out.
2. Rub inside with salt.
3. Rinse the fruit and stuff the goose with the fruit.
4. Truss.
5. Spread with butter or line with bacon.
6. Roast in a hot oven basting often.
7. Add some water to the drippings, if necessary.
8. When brown sprinkle with salt and cover with buttered paper.
9. Allow 2 or 3 hours for roasting a goose.
10. Slice onto a platter, arrange the apples and prunes around.
11. Strain the drippings, skim off the fat and add some flour and cream.

title: Hapanimelää-kastiketta (Sweet Sour Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brown sauce
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Mix brown sauce with sugar and vinegar.
2. Season with salt and pepper.

title: Hapankaali (Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- middlesized cabbages
- salt
- small apples
- sprigs of dill

## Directions

1. Cut cabbages into strips
2. Pound well
3. Lay into a wooden vessel, having salt, small apples and sprigs of dill between the layers
4. Press tightly with cover, having a weight on top of it
5. Keep in a warm room for a week, carefully skimming off the mildew
6. Keep in a cool place
7. Allow 1 pound of salt to 50 heads of cabbage

title: Hapankaalia (Sauer Kraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- Medium-sized heads of white cabbage
- Salt
- Small apples
- Sprigs of dill

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage in shreds and pound well.
2. Lay into a wooden vessel having salt, small apples and sprigs of dill between the layers.
3. Press tightly with a cover fitting inside the vessel.
4. Place a heavy weight on the top.
5. Keep in a warm room for a week, carefully skimming off the mildew.
6. When ready, keep in a cool place.
7. Allow 1 pound of salt to 50 heads of cabbage.

title: Hapankerma-kakku (Sour Cream Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sour whipping cream
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 1/2 cups flour
- Dash of salt

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar until frothy.
2. Add cream.
3. Add flour sifted with soda.
4. Flavor with yellow rind of a lemon or cinnamon.
5. Last of all fold in the egg whites beaten until firm.
6. Bake in a shallow tin.
7. May be mixed with melted chocolate.

title: Hashe Cutlets
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 1/2 cupfuls of boiled meat
- 1/2 cupful of bacon
- 2 cupfuls of boiled potatoes
- 1 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 red onion
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Force beef, bacon and potatoes through a meat chopper.
2. Cook chopped onion in butter, mix with meat.
3. Add seasonings, stirring well.
4. Form into cutlets, fry in butter until lightly browned.
5. For sauce: Cook 1 chopped onion in butter, add flour, bouillon, cream, 2 tablespoonfuls of tomato stew, salt and pepper, color with Kitchen Bouquet, and strain.

title: Hashe-kotletteja (Hashe Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups boiled meat
- 1/2 cup bacon
- 2 cups boiled potatoes
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 red onion
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Force meat, bacon and potatoes through a meat chopper.
2. Cook onion, chopped, in butter, add to the meat.
3. Season and beat well.
4. Form into cutlets, fry in butter until light brown.
5. For gravy: Cook 1 onion, chopped, in butter, add flour, bouillon, cream, 2 tablespoons stewed tomato, salt and pepper.
6. Color with Kitchen Bouquet; strain.

title: Hashed Brown Potatoes
body:
## Ingredients

- cold potatoes
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut cold potatoes into small cubes.
2. Fry in butter until thoroughly heated, sprinkle with salt and pepper.
3. Brown underneath, double on a dish like omelet.

title: Haudottu Eggplant (Scalloped Eggplant)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eggplant
- Water
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1/2 onion
- 1 green pepper
- 1/2 cup bread crumbs
- 1 egg
- 1 cup tomato

## Directions

1. Peel the eggplant and cut in cubes 2/3 inch thick.
2. Cook in scanty water until tender.
3. Drain.
4. Cook 2 tablespoons butter with 1/2 onion and 1 green pepper chopped.
5. Add the eggplant, 1/2 cup bread crumbs and 1 egg.
6. Put the mixture into a buttered mold.
7. Fill the mold with a cup of tomato.
8. Bake from 1/2 to 1 hour.

title: Haudottuja tomaatteja (Scalloped Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 can of tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- sugar
- chopped onion
- green pepper (optional)
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Take 1 can of tomatoes, drain off the juice.
2. Season the tomato with salt and pepper.
3. Add butter, sugar and chopped onion (green pepper chopped may be added, if desired).
4. Put into a buttered mold, sprinkle with bread crumbs mixed with butter.
5. Bake for 15 minutes.
6. Serve in the mold.

title: Haukea piparuutin kanssa (Pike with Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- butter
- parsley
- fish stock
- flour
- horseradish
- cream

## Directions

1. Boil the fish as directed
2. Remove carefully onto a platter
3. Pour some melted butter over and sprinkle with parsley
4. Make a sauce of fish stock, butter, and flour, mixed with grated horseradish when done
5. Serve sauce in a separate dish
6. Or make a sauce of whipped cream and grated horseradish and serve cold

title: Hedelmä Hyydyke (Fruit Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh or canned fruit
- 1 ounce of gelatine to 2 cupfuls of fruit
- sugar
- lemon juice
- orange juice
- wine
- 2 1/2 cupfuls of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Pass the fruit through a sieve or cut them in pieces.
2. Dissolve gelatine in fruit juice.
3. Season the mixture of fruit with sugar, lemon juice, orange juice and wine.
4. Stir the mixture until cool.
5. Add gradually 2 1/2 cupfuls of whipped cream.
6. Put in a wet mold to harden.

title: Hedelmä Kakku (Fruit Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 10 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of almonds, blanched and chopped
- 2 lbs. of currants
- 2 lbs. of seeded and chopped raisins
- 1/2 teaspoonful of cinnamon
- 1/2 teaspoonful of mace
- 1/2 teaspoonful of nutmeg
- 1/2 cupful of brandy
- 1 teaspoonful of soda

## Directions

1. Rub butter and sugar to a cream, mix with yolks of eggs, spices, fruit, dredged with flour, soda and flour.
2. Then whites of eggs, beaten until stiff.
3. Put in a buttered mold and bake in a moderate oven 1 1/2 — 2 hours.
4. If it begins to get too brown, cover with paper.

title: Hedelmä-Kermajäätelö (Fruit Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Vanilla ice cream
- Fruit such as cherries, strawberries, apples or apricots
- Preserved fruit for garnish

## Directions

1. Prepare as directed for vanilla ice cream.
2. When half frozen pack in a freezer alternately with fruit such as cherries, strawberries, apples or apricots.
3. Freeze completely.
4. Garnish around with preserved fruit.

title: Hedelmä-laserrus (Fruit Frosting)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup confectioner's sugar
- 2 tablespoons fruit juice
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Mix slowly 1 cup confectioner's sugar with 2 tablespoons fruit juice and some lemon juice
2. Stir until smooth

title: Hedelmä-vesijäätelö (Fruit Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups water
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 cups fruit juice
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook the sugar and the water 15 minutes.
2. When cool add the fruit juice.
3. Freeze.

title: Hedelmäkakku (Fruit Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 4 cups flour
- 10 eggs
- 1/4 pound almonds blanched and chopped
- 2 pounds currants
- 2 pounds raisins cut in pieces
- Cinnamon, mace and nutmeg, 1/2 teaspoon each
- 1/2 cup brandy
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Rub until creamy the butter with the sugar
2. Work in the egg yolks
3. Add spices, raisins and currants rolled in flour and soda sifted with flour
4. Add last the egg whites beaten until firm
5. Bake in a buttered tin 2 or 3 hours in a moderate oven
6. When risen reduce the heat and cover the cake with paper

title: Hedelmäputinki (Fruit Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1/2 cup chopped suet
- 1/4 cup syrup or sugar
- 1/4 cup milk
- 4 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1/2 cup currants
- 1/2 cup raisins

## Directions

1. Mix the dry ingredients
2. Add the spices and the milk
3. Stir well
4. Steam in a buttered mold for 2 1/2 hours

title: Hedelmäsalaattia (Fruit Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- White grapes
- Peaches
- Pineapples
- Cantaloupes
- Bananas
- Strawberries
- Cherries
- Figs
- Oranges
- Walnuts
- Grapefruit
- Celery
- Apples
- Lettuce
- French dressing
- Mayonnaise
- Cream
- Dates

## Directions

1. All kinds of fruit plain or mixed may be used for salad
2. Have 1 cup of each: white grapes split and seeded, peaches and pineapples cut in cubes
3. Cantaloupes, grapes, peaches, bananas, strawberries, pineapples, cherries and figs mixed make a delicious salad
4. Oranges, bananas, grapes and walnuts may be mixed together
5. Equal parts of oranges, grapefruit and celery mixed make a nice salad
6. For pineapple, celery and apples: Take 2 cups apple, 1 cup celery and 1 cup pineapple
7. Or pile slices of pineapple on lettuce leaves, fill the center with fruit moistened with French dressing
8. Cover the whole with mayonnaise mixed with cream
9. Sprinkle with chopped walnuts
10. Mix together 1 grapefruit, 2 oranges, 1 cup grapes split and seeded and 2 bananas
11. Mix with cream dressing and serve in cups made of orange peels
12. Grapefruit, apples, bananas, celery, figs and dates cut in pieces make a nice fruit salad
13. Serve in mayonnaise mixed with cream

title: Hedelmätipat (Fruit Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh apricots, pears and peaches
- Fritter batter
- Lard for frying

## Directions

1. Pare the fruit into sections
2. Cut in pieces
3. Mix in the batter
4. When made of canned fruit, drain the liquid and cut the fruit in pieces
5. Drop into boiling lard by the spoonful
6. Cook until brown

title: Hedelmätäyte No. 1 (Fruit Filling No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 1 orange
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- chopped nuts or almonds

## Directions

1. Stir over the fire until thick the juices of an orange and a lemon with 1 cup sugar and 2 egg yolks
2. Mix with chopped nuts and almonds, if preferred

title: Hedelmätäyte No. 2 (Fruit Filling No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups crushed berries
- grated apple, peach or pineapple
- some lemon juice or rind
- 1½ cups sugar
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Take 2 cups crushed berries, grated apple, peach or pineapple, some lemon juice or rind, 1½ cups sugar and 3 egg whites.
2. Beat together all the ingredients until firm.

title: Hedelmätäyte No. 2 (Fruit Filling No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups crushed berries
- grated apple, peach or pineapple
- lemon juice or rind
- 1½ cups sugar
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Take 2 cups crushed berries, grated apple, peach or pineapple, lemon juice or rind, 1½ cups sugar and 3 egg whites
2. Beat all ingredients together until stiff froth

title: Herneitä maitokastikkeen kanssa (Creamed Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled peas
- milk sauce, thin
- parsley

## Directions

1. Mix the boiled peas with thin milk sauce
2. Add a sprinkling of parsley

title: Hernekakkuja (Pea Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup boiled mashed peas
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup milk or cream
- 5 tablespoons flour
- 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Mix the mashed peas with butter, milk, eggs beaten and flour
2. Add salt and pepper to taste
3. Form into cakes
4. Fry in butter
5. Serve with meat or fish

title: Hernekeitto (Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cups dried peas
- 1½ quarts bone stock or bouillon
- 2½ quarts cold water
- 1 small onion
- 2 stalks celery
- ½ pound pork
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 tablespoon flour

## Directions

1. Rinse the peas, soak in lukewarm water for a few hours.
2. Drain, put into cold water with the ham.
3. Cook on a slow fire.
4. Cook the onion in butter, add flour, combine with the soup.
5. Strain, rubbing the peas through the strainer, or serve unstrained.
6. Cut the pork in pieces, serve in the soup.
7. Instead of meat stock, milk or water may be used.

title: Hernepalleroita (Pea Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups dried peas
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 1 cup milk
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1 onion
- 2 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- minced parsley

## Directions

1. Boil the peas until tender
2. Drain and let cool
3. Force through a meat grinder
4. Add the chopped onion fried in butter
5. Add bread crumbs soaked in milk
6. Add salt, pepper and minced parsley
7. Mix well
8. Form into croquettes
9. Dip in egg and roll in bread crumbs
10. Fry in butter or deep fat

title: Hernevanukas (Pea Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup canned peas or fresh ones
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- pepper
- a few drops of onion juice

## Directions

1. Rinse the peas
2. Rub through a sieve
3. Add eggs, melted butter, salt, pepper and a few drops of onion juice
4. Place mixture in a buttered mold
5. Bake in a pan of water covered with buttered paper until it feels firm

title: Herring Balls
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 herrings
- 1 cupful of chopped potato
- 2 eggs
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of breadcrumbs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cream

## Directions

1. Soak the herrings, skin and bone.
2. Force through the meatchopper.
3. Chop the onion, fry in butter.
4. Soak the breadcrumbs in cream, add the beaten eggs into it, mix with the fried onion.
5. Form into cakes or balls, roll in egg and breadcrumbs, cook in fat.
6. Serve with any kind of sour sauce.

title: Hiekkakakku (Sand Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of corn starch
- 3 eggs
- vanilla to flavor

## Directions

1. Cream butter with one-half of the sugar
2. Beat yolks of eggs with the remaining sugar
3. Add vanilla and corn starch
4. Last add the whites whipped to a froth
5. Turn in a buttered mold and bake in a moderate oven

title: Hiivakakku (Coffee Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of milk
- 1 whole egg
- 1 yolk of egg
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1/2 teaspoonful of lemon juice
- a dash of mace
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 cake of yeast
- 1 cupful of flour
- chopped almonds
- granulated sugar
- pink colored granulated sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Scald milk, mix with yeast and 1 cupful of flour, cover it and set to rise.
2. Then add eggs beaten with sugar, butter rubbed to a cream, salt, lemon juice, mace and flour enough to make a moderately thick batter.
3. Knead the mixture well and set to rise.
4. Roll the dough into 1/4 inch thick sheet, spread with melted butter, sprinkle with chopped almonds and granulated sugar.
5. Triple and shape into round cakes.
6. Place the cakes on a buttered baking sheet to rise.
7. Brush over with eggs and drop with a spoon in the centre of the cakes following mixture: 1 yolk of egg, 1 tablespoonful of sugar, 2 tablespoonfuls of chopped almonds and few drops of vanilla beaten together.
8. Sprinkle with pink colored granulated sugar and bake in a moderate oven.

title: Hillo-omeletti (Jam Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- sugar
- 3 tablespoons milk or cream
- jam
- fruit sauce

## Directions

1. Beat separately whites and yolks of 3 eggs.
2. Mix the yolks with some sugar, 3 tablespoons of milk or cream.
3. Add the whites beaten stiff.
4. Bake as usually.
5. Cover with any kind of jam.
6. Fold once.
7. Serve hot with fruit sauce.

title: Hilloleipiä (Jam Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- rich batter
- jam

## Directions

1. Roll rich batter into thin round cookies
2. Put jam in center of each
3. Fold edges together
4. Form into balls
5. Let rise
6. Cook in fat
7. Dip in sugar

title: Hillopannukakkuja (Finnish Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- batter from sweet pancakes recipe
- jam
- cream
- sugar
- vanilla
- cooked fruit

## Directions

1. Fry thin pancakes and pile them nicely on a serving dish, putting jam between the cakes.
2. Whip cream until stiff, mix with a little sugar and vanilla, place on the pancakes.
3. Garnish with cooked fruit.
4. Serve cold.

title: Hillopannukakkuja (Jam Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- batter as for sweet pancakes
- jam
- cream
- sugar
- vanilla
- preserved fruit

## Directions

1. Prepare the batter as directed for sweet pancakes
2. Fry thin large cakes
3. Pile on a serving platter having jam between the cakes
4. Whip cream, add sugar and vanilla
5. Cover the top of the pile with the cream
6. Garnish around with preserved fruit
7. Serve cold

title: Hillotippoja (Jam Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons butter
- ½ cup milk or water
- ½ cup flour
- Salt
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- Lard for frying
- Jam

## Directions

1. Bring to boiling the milk with the butter
2. Add the flour beating well
3. Cook until it loosens from the edges of the saucepan
4. Remove from the fire
5. Add 2 eggs, one at a time, 2 tablespoons sugar and beat well
6. Drop by the spoonful into boiling lard
7. When browned and risen, remove from the lard
8. Make a little cavity in the center
9. Fill with jam

title: Hirvensarvia (Elk Horns)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 6 tablespoons sugar
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 3 tablespoons cream
- Yellow rind of 1 lemon
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 4 cups flour

## Directions

1. Beat until frothy eggs with sugar
2. Add flavoring, cream, butter and flour mixed with baking powder
3. Form into cookies in the shape of an elk horn
4. Bake on a buttered baking sheet in a moderately hot oven

title: Hirvensarvia (Elk Horns)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 6 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 3 tablespoonfuls of cream
- yellow rind of 1 lemon
- 2 teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- 4 cupfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Whip eggs with sugar to a froth
2. Add spices, cream, butter and baking powder mixed with flour
3. Form into cookies to look like elk horns
4. Bake on a buttered baking sheet in a slow oven

title: Hollannin-kastike (Hollandaise Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 heaping tablespoons butter
- 4 yolks of egg
- juice of 1 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- pepper
- 2 2/3 cups boiling water

## Directions

1. Rub until foamy 2 heaping tablespoons of butter in a saucepan
2. Add 4 yolks one by one, whipping well between each
3. Mix with juice of 1 1/2 lemon, 1/2 teaspoon of salt and some pepper
4. Let stand for a while
5. Add 2 2/3 cups of boiling water and beat the sauce in a pan of boiling water until it becomes thick
6. Remove from the fire and continue to stir for a while
7. Serve with fish, asparagus, cauliflower, etc.

title: Hominy Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of boiled hominy
- 1 tablespoonful of melted butter
- salt and pepper
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Season hominy with salt and pepper
2. Add well beaten egg
3. Rub until smooth
4. Form into croquettes
5. Dip in egg and bread crumbs
6. Fry in lard

title: Hominy Croquettes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of boiled hominy
- 1 tablespoonful of melted butter
- salt and pepper
- 1 egg
- bread crumbs
- lard

## Directions

1. Combine 2 cupfuls of boiled hominy and 1 tablespoonful of melted butter.
2. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Add an egg well beaten.
4. Rub until smooth.
5. Form into croquettes, dip in egg and bread crumbs and fry in lard.

title: Hominy Pohja (Hominy Foundation)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup hominy
- 3 cups water
- 1 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Boil hominy with water and salt for 5 to 10 minutes.
2. Pack into a buttered shallow mold.
3. Turn onto a suitable platter.
4. Use as a foundation for various dishes.

title: Hominy pohja (Hominy Foundation)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup hominy
- 3 cups water
- 1 teaspoon salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Boil hominy with water and salt for 5 to 10 minutes
2. Pack into a buttered shallow mold
3. Turn onto a suitable platter
4. Use as a foundation for various dishes

title: Hominypalleroita (Hominy Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups boiled hominy
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- salt and pepper
- 1 egg, well beaten
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Rub the hominy, butter, salt, pepper, and egg until all lumps are smooth.
2. Form into croquettes.
3. Roll in egg and bread crumbs.
4. Fry in butter or deep lard.

title: Hominypalleroita (Hominy Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups cooked hominy
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mix cooked hominy with melted butter
2. Season with salt and pepper

title: Honey Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1 cupful of honey
- 6 ozs. of sugar
- 8 ozs. of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of cinnamon
- 1/2 teaspoonful of ginger
- 1/2 teaspoonful of baking soda

## Directions

1. Beat yolks with sugar
2. Mix with warm honey beaten to a froth
3. Continue rubbing for a while
4. Stir into it spices and flour mixed with baking soda
5. Add last whites of eggs beaten to a froth
6. Bake in a quick oven 1 to 1 1/2 hours

title: Hot Mayonnaise Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoonfuls of salad oil
- 1 tablespoonful of vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful of lemon juice
- 1/4 cupful of hot water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Mix the oil drop by drop with the yolks, stirring well
2. Add vinegar and water gradually into it
3. Cook in a pot of boiling water until thick
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Add minced parsley, if you like

title: Hot Pineapple Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 3/4 lb. of sugar
- 2 ozs. of flour
- 2 ozs. of corn starch
- 2 1/2 cupfuls of thin cream
- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of pineapple

## Directions

1. Cook cream and flour to a paste.
2. Add butter and whipped yolks gradually.
3. Then stir pineapple, cut in dice, into it.
4. Lastly whites, beaten stiff.
5. Pour in a buttered mold and bake in a moderate oven 1 hour.
6. Prepare a sauce of pineapple liquor, a little white wine, corn starch and sugar.

title: Hot Sponge Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 whole eggs
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 6-8 ozs. of flour
- 4-8 ozs. of butter

## Directions

1. Whip eggs and sugar in a pan of hot water on the fire until the mixture is thick and light, about 20-30 minutes
2. Add melted butter and sifted flour
3. Pour in a buttered mold and bake in a moderate oven about 3/4 hour

title: Hummeri hyydyke (Lobster Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat of boiled lobster
- mayonnaise sauce
- gelatine
- caper
- pickled onions
- hard-boiled eggs
- truffle
- slices of bread

## Directions

1. Cut meat of boiled lobster into dice.
2. Mix with mayonnaise sauce diluted with gelatine.
3. Season with caper and a few finely chopped pickled onions.
4. Line individual molds with liquid jelly, garnish with egg and truffle.
5. Turn the mixture of lobster into the molds, put aside to set.
6. Unmold on slices of bread.

title: Hummeri Kastike (Lobster Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 2 cupfuls of fish stock
- 1 can (1/2 quart) or 1 pound of fresh lobster
- 1-2 yolks of eggs
- salt and pepper
- a little lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare a sauce of butter, flour and fish stock.
2. Add the lobster cut in pieces, then salt and pepper.
3. Lastly add beaten yolks and a little lemon juice.

title: Hummeri Sienien Kanssa (Lobster with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups boiled lobster, diced
- 2 cups fried mushrooms, cut in pieces
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 slice of onion
- 1 cup milk or cream

## Directions

1. Cook the chopped slice of onion in butter
2. Add flour, mushrooms, lobster, spices and milk or cream hot, bring to boiling
3. Remove from fire, add some lemon juice or sherry
4. Serve on a platter having toast around, or fill lobster shells with the mixture, sprinkle with bread crumbs, dot with butter
5. Brown in oven

title: Hummeri Voin Kanssa (Buttered Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- Lobster
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon juice
- Melted butter

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from the shells taking care so as not to break the shells
2. Cut the meat in small cubes, season with salt, pepper and lemon juice
3. Fill the shells, pour some melted butter over
4. Heat in oven
5. Serve garnished with lobster claws

title: Hummeri à la Mayonnaise (Lobster à la Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- lobster shells
- lobster meat
- mayonnaise
- red pepper
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse lobster shells.
2. Fill with lobster meat mixed with mayonnaise.
3. Garnish the top with red pepper.
4. Serve with lettuce leaves on a napkin.

title: Hummeri à la Newburg (Lobster à la Newburg)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups boiled lobster
- 2 tablespoons butter
- salt
- pepper
- some sherry or lemon juice
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cup hot cream

## Directions

1. Cut 2 cups of boiled lobster in dices 1 inch thick.
2. Put 2 tablespoons of butter in a saucepan and add the lobster pieces.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Cover and let simmer about 5 minutes.
5. Add some sherry or lemon juice.
6. Beat 2 egg yolks and mix with 1 cup of hot cream, then add to the pan.
7. Shake the pan over the fire until the mixture becomes thick.
8. Alternatively, boiled egg yolks rubbed through a sieve may be used instead of raw yolks to prevent curdling.

title: Hummeri ä la Charlotte (Lobster ä la Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled lobsters
- 1/4 cup milk
- 4 tablespoons gelatine
- 2 tablespoons mayonnaise
- 4 tablespoons cream
- truffle

## Directions

1. Shell boiled lobsters and cut the meat in cubes
2. Stir gelatine with milk
3. When it is cool add the mayonnaise and the whipped cream
4. Decorate small molds with truffle
5. Cover the bottom of molds with the mixture
6. When firm, lay lobster meat on it, and a layer of the mixture on top
7. When firm turn on lettuce leaves

title: Hummeri-hyytelö (Lobster à la Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh boiled lobsters or canned lobsters
- fish stock

## Directions

1. Prepare fresh boiled lobsters or canned lobsters.
2. Make jelly of fish stock in the same way as for Salmon à la Aspic.
3. Place the lobsters cut in pieces into an individual mold, laying the nicest pieces on the bottom and on the sides.
4. Pour the jelly over and let get firm.

title: Hummeri-Kalopsi (Scalloped Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 boiled lobster, cut in cubes
- milk sauce
- hard boiled egg yolk
- mace
- pepper
- salt
- lemon juice
- bread crumbs
- butter
- fish stock
- cream

## Directions

1. Cut the boiled lobster meat in cubes.
2. Mix with some milk sauce, yolk of a hard boiled egg, some mace, pepper, salt and lemon juice.
3. Fill the lobster shell with this mixture.
4. Dredge with bread crumbs and dot with butter.
5. Brown in oven.
6. Serve with sauce made of fish stock and cream.
7. Alternatively, the mixture may be baked in a mold and made thinner by using less milk sauce.
8. Instead of milk sauce, bread crumbs may be used.
9. Fill the mold with layers of lobster having bread crumbs, salt and pepper between the layers.
10. Cover with milk mixed with 2 eggs and dot with butter.
11. Bake.

title: Hummeri-kastike (Lobster Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 cups fish stock
- 1 can (1/4 quart) or 1 pound of fresh lobster
- 1 or 2 egg yolks
- Salt and pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Make a gravy of butter, flour and fish stock
2. Add lobster cut in pieces, salt and pepper
3. Last of all mix with yolks of egg beaten
4. Flavor with lemon juice
5. Lobster sauce may be made of Hollandaise sauce by adding lobster to it

title: Hummeri-keitto (Lobster Bisque)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of fish stock
- 1 lobster or 1 small can of lobster
- 1 tablespoonful of butter or lobster-butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- yolks of 3 eggs
- 1 cupful of cream
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the tail meat and cook it in fish stock about half an hour and rub through a sieve.
2. Cook the shells separately in a small quantity of water and add then into the soup.
3. Thicken with butter and flour and season with salt and pepper.
4. Beat the yolks, add cream into it, pour the soup slowly in and stir on the fire for a little while.
5. Serve with the meat of claws in it or with lobster balls and tips of asparagus cooked in fish stock until tender.

title: Hummeri-Kotletteja (Lobster Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups lobster, cut in fine pieces
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- chopped parsley
- 1 cup milk sauce
- 2 hard boiled egg yolks or 1 raw egg yolk
- bread crumbs
- egg
- lard or butter

## Directions

1. Season 2 cups of lobster, cut in fine pieces, with salt, pepper, some lemon juice and chopped parsley.
2. Mix with 1 cup of milk sauce and 2 hard boiled egg yolks or one raw egg yolk.
3. Place on ice for a while.
4. Form into cutlets.
5. Roll in bread crumbs, dip in egg and roll in bread crumbs again.
6. Fry in deep lard.
7. Serve with tartare or tomato sauce.
8. Alternatively, the cutlets may be rolled in flour and fried in butter.

title: Hummeri-kvenellejä (Lobster Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls chopped lobster meat
- 4 tablespoonfuls bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoonfuls lobster butter or butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls cream
- 2 eggs
- hard boiled egg yolk
- anchovy paste
- seasonings

## Directions

1. Chop or grind lobster meat fine with hard boiled yolk of an egg and anchovy paste
2. Add seasonings, butter, raw yolk of an egg and cream, stirring well
3. Mix with white of an egg beaten stiff
4. Form into small balls
5. Roll in flour
6. Boil in salt water

title: Hummeri-kvenellejä (Lobster Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoonfuls of finely chopped lobster
- 4 tablespoonfuls of bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of crab butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 2 eggs
- a little anchovy paste
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Boil one egg until hard
2. Chop or pound the lobster, hard-boiled egg yolk and anchovy paste fine
3. Add seasonings and crab butter (melted butter may be used instead)
4. Mix in raw egg yolk and cream
5. Mix thoroughly
6. Beat the egg whites to a froth and stir into the mixture
7. Form into small balls
8. Dip in flour
9. Cook in salted water

title: Hummeri-voi (Lobster Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- shells, fat and all small claws of the lobster
- 1/4 pound of butter
- 2 quarts of boiling water

## Directions

1. Pound shells, fat and all small claws of the lobster and mix with 1/4 pound of butter.
2. Cook it on a slow fire 15 minutes.
3. Pour 2 quarts of boiling water into it and let boil half an hour.
4. Set to cool.
5. Next day skim the fat.
6. Melt it again, pour in a small jar for future use.

title: Hummeria à la Newburg (Lobster à la Newburg)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups cooked lobster
- 2 tablespoons butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sherry wine or lemon juice
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cup heated cream

## Directions

1. Cut the lobster into cubes about an inch in size.
2. Put the butter in a saucepan and add the lobster pieces; season with salt and pepper.
3. Cover and let simmer gently about 5 minutes.
4. Add a little Sherry wine or lemon juice, two egg yolks beaten and mixed with one cup of heated cream.
5. Shake the pan over the fire until the mixture is blended.
6. Alternatively, cooked egg yolks pressed through a sieve may be used instead of raw yolks, which prevents the mixture from curdling when heated.

title: Hummerihyydyke (Lobster Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled lobster meat
- mayonnaise
- gelatine
- capers
- pickles
- egg
- truffle
- bread

## Directions

1. Cut meat of boiled lobsters in cubes.
2. Stir into mayonnaise mixed with gelatine.
3. Flavor with finely chopped capers and pickles.
4. Fill small molds and let cool.
5. Serve on thin slices of bread.

title: Hummerihyytelö (Lobster à la Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh boiled lobsters or canned lobsters
- Fish stock
- Gelatine

## Directions

1. Make an aspic of fish stock, same as directed for salmon.
2. Lay the lobster cut in pieces in a mold, having the nicest pieces on the bottom and on the sides.
3. Fill the mold with the aspic.
4. Let set.

title: Hummerikeitto (Lobster Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts fish stock
- 1 lobster or 1 can of lobsters
- 1 tablespoon butter or lobster butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cup cream
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the tail meat fine.
2. Cook in fish stock for about half an hour.
3. Force through a sieve.
4. Boil the shells separately in small quantity of water and add to the broth if desired.
5. Thicken with butter and flour.
6. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Beat the egg yolks, add the cream.
8. Gradually add the broth to the egg yolk and cream mixture and stir over heat for a short time.
9. Serve with pieces of lobster meat or with lobster balls and asparagus tips cooked in fish stock until tender.

title: Hummerimuhennos (Stewed Lobster)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups boiled lobster, cut in cubes
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 slice of onion
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 cup rich milk
- salt
- paprika
- some sherry or lemon juice
- 2 egg yolks
- 1/2 cup cream

## Directions

1. Cook 1 tablespoon of butter with a slice of onion.
2. Add 1 tablespoon of flour, 1 cup of rich milk and the lobster cubes.
3. Season with salt and paprika.
4. Bring to boiling.
5. Before serving, add some sherry or lemon juice.
6. Beat 2 egg yolks with 1/2 cup of cream and add to the mixture.
7. Shake over the fire for a minute.

title: Hummerivatkuli (Lobster Chowder)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups lobster cut in pieces
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 small onion
- 4 cups milk
- 2 tablespoons cracker crumbs
- Salt
- Paprika

## Directions

1. Cook the butter with onion (chopped) in a saucepan for 5 minutes.
2. Strain the butter, add flour and cracker crumbs, milk, lobster meat and season.
3. Boil quickly and serve.

title: Hummerivoi (Lobster Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- Shells, fat and small claws of boiled lobster
- 1.5 pounds butter
- 2 quarts boiling water

## Directions

1. Pound shells, fat and small claws of boiled lobster
2. Mix with 1.5 pounds of butter
3. Cook on a slow fire for 1 hour
4. Add 2 quarts of boiling water to the butter
5. Continue to cook for 1/2 hour
6. Place to cool
7. Next day skin the fat
8. Melt it again and pour into a small jar for future use

title: Hummersalaatti (Lobster Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled lobster
- mayonnaise
- lettuce
- lobster claws
- hard boiled eggs
- celery

## Directions

1. Cut boiled lobster in small cubes.
2. Let stand in a cold place until served.
3. Mix with mayonnaise.
4. Place on a platter on a bed of lettuce.
5. Garnish around with lobster claws and hard boiled eggs.

title: Hyydyke No. 1 (Aspic No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups of meat or fish stock well seasoned
- 4 tablespoons tomato
- 1/2 onion
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 1/2 package or 12 leaves of gelatine
- 2 eggs, whites and shells, lightly beaten
- Kitchen Bouquet (optional)

## Directions

1. Place all the ingredients into a saucepan with the lightly beaten whites and shells of 2 eggs.
2. Put on the fire, stir until it begins to boil.
3. Let simmer for 5 minutes.
4. Strain through a fine cloth.
5. If not clear, strain once more without pressing the cloth.
6. If darker color is preferred, add a few drops of Kitchen Bouquet.

title: Hyydyke No. 2 (Aspic No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons of chopped carrot and celery
- 2 slices of onion
- Some parsley
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 cloves
- 2 tablespoons tomato
- 2 teaspoons beef extract
- 2 1/2 cups water
- 1 package of gelatine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 1 egg white for clarification

## Directions

1. Place into a saucepan 2 tablespoons of chopped carrot and celery, 2 slices of onion, some parsley, 1 bay leaf, 2 cloves, 2 tablespoons tomato, 2 teaspoons beef extract and 2 1/2 cups water.
2. Cover let cook for 1/2 hour.
3. Add 1 package of gelatine, stir until dissolved.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Flavor with the juice of 2 lemons.
6. Clarify with an egg white.

title: Hyydytetty peltopyy (Jellied Squab)
body:
## Ingredients

- squab
- stuffing for stuffed chicken
- thin cloth
- aspic jelly

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the squab as directed.
2. Stuff the same as stuffed chicken.
3. Sew up and wrap into a thin cloth.
4. Cook for 3/4 hour or until tender.
5. Remove from the stock and place under a small weight, until cold and even.
6. Make an aspic jelly of the stock.
7. Lay some of the aspic on the bottom of the mold.
8. Slice the squab crosswise and arrange nicely into the mold.
9. Fill the mold with aspic and place aside to set.

title: Hyytelö Kastike (Jelly Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of brown sauce
- 3 tablespoonfuls of currant jelly
- a little sherry wine

## Directions

1. Prepare 1 cupful of brown sauce
2. Add currant jelly and a little sherry wine
3. Serve with game and boiled mutton

title: Hyytelökastike (Jelly Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 3/4 cup boiling water
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3/4 cup jelly

## Directions

1. Mix sugar with cornstarch
2. Add slowly the boiling water
3. Let boil for 5 minutes
4. Remove from the fire, add butter and jelly
5. Keep in a pan of hot water until served

title: Hyytelökastike (Jelly Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup brown sauce
- 3 tablespoons currant jelly
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare a cupful of brown sauce
2. Add 3 tablespoons of currant jelly and some lemon juice
3. Serve with game or mutton

title: Häränhäntä-Liemi (Ox-tail Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 carrots
- 2 red onions
- White part of celery
- 1 quart of Jerusalem artichokes
- 1 cup fresh or dried mushrooms
- Salt and pepper
- Butter and flour for thickening
- Lemon
- Cayenne pepper
- Kitchen Bouquet for coloring
- Hardboiled egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the vegetables.
2. Cut in pieces and brown in butter.
3. When brown cover with boiling water.
4. Add the artichokes, the mushrooms, the green part of leek, a sprig of parsley, a bay leaf, salt and pepper.
5. Let simmer covered until the vegetables are tender.
6. Strain through a fine cloth.
7. Thicken with butter and flour.
8. Season with salt and cayenne pepper.
9. Flavor with lemon.
10. Color with Kitchen Bouquet.
11. Serve with hardboiled egg yolks cut in pieces or with egg balls.
12. May be made without artichokes and mushrooms, if desired.

title: Häränhäntäliemi (Ox-tail Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 ox-tails
- 1/4 cup onion
- 1/4 cup carrot
- 1/4 cup celery
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 sprig parsley
- 5 peppercorns
- 2 cloves
- Some cayenne pepper
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Some wine and lemon
- 2 tablespoons butter or 1 tablespoon pork fat
- 5 quarts brown stock or 3 pounds lean beef

## Directions

1. Scrape the vegetables and cut in small cubes
2. Cut the ox-tails at the joints
3. Brown the butter in a soup kettle
4. Add the meat, the vegetables and the flour, continue frying until all is browned
5. Add the brown stock or water and the meat, let simmer covered for 3 hours
6. Remove the pieces of ox-tail when the meat is tender
7. Strain the stock
8. Add cayenne, white wine or Sherry
9. Serve with hard boiled egg yolks or tiny cubes of meat and a few slices of lemon, or with croutons flavored with lemon or plain

title: Hää Kakku (Wedding Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of butter
- 2 cupfuls of sugar
- 3 1/2 cupfuls of flour
- 10 eggs
- 2 pounds of currants
- 1/4 pound of blanched and chopped almonds
- 1/4 pound of grated lemon rind
- 1/4 pound of grated orange peel
- 1 pound of raisins, cut in pieces
- 2 teaspoonfuls of cinnamon
- 2 teaspoonfuls of spices
- 1/2 grated nutmeg
- 1/2 teaspoonful of mace
- 1 cupful of brandy
- 1/4 teaspoonful of soda

## Directions

1. Work butter and sugar to a cream, add well beaten yolks of eggs, spices, fruits dipped in flour, brandy, soda, flour and hard beaten whites.
2. Bake in oven about 3 — 4 hours.
3. If it becomes brown cover with paper.

title: Hääkakku (Wedding Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups butter
- 2 cups sugar
- 4 cups flour
- 12 eggs
- 2 or 4 pounds currants
- 2 or 4 pounds raisins cut in pieces
- 1 pound almonds blanched and chopped
- 1/2 pound each candied lemon and orange peel
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon cloves and cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1 cup brandy

## Directions

1. Rub the butter with the sugar until creamy
2. Add the yolks of the eggs beaten to a froth
3. Add spices, raisins, currants and candied peel chopped and all rolled in flour
4. Add brandy, soda and flour
5. Fold in the egg whites beaten until firm
6. Bake for 3 or 4 hours
7. If the surface is becoming too brown, cover with paper

title: Höyryssä keitetty turska (Steamed Codfish)
body:
## Ingredients

- codfish
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- Hollandaise sauce
- parsley
- truffels

## Directions

1. Clean the fish and rub with salt and pepper
2. Put in a buttered pan with cover
3. Bake for half an hour
4. Lift onto a platter and remove the skin carefully
5. Cover with Hollandaise sauce
6. Garnish with chopped parsley and truffels

title: Iced Coffee (Jää-kahvi)
body:
## Ingredients

- black coffee
- cracked ice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Brew black coffee, strain and let cool
2. Half fill glasses with cracked ice, fill with coffee and flavor with sugar

title: Iced Tea (Jää-tee)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 teaspoonfuls of tea
- 2 cupfuls of water
- cracked ice
- lemon slices
- granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare tea as directed for regular tea but allow 4 teaspoonfuls of tea to 2 cupfuls of water
2. Half fill glasses with cracked ice, put a slice of lemon in
3. Strain the tea on it, filling the glasses
4. Add granulated sugar to taste

title: Imperial Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of butter
- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1½ cupfuls of flour
- 5 eggs
- ½ lb. of raisins
- ¼ lb. of peeled and chopped almonds
- ¼ lb. of chopped lemon rind
- ¼ cupful of brandy
- 1 teaspoonful of soda

## Directions

1. Work butter and sugar to a froth
2. Add beaten yolks of eggs
3. Add spices and fruit
4. Mix soda with flour
5. Fold in yolks of eggs beaten to a stiff froth

title: Imperial-kakku (Imperial Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 5 eggs
- 1/2 pound raisins
- 1/4 pound lemon rind cut in pieces
- 1/4 pound chopped almonds
- 1/4 cup brandy or lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Rub the butter with the sugar until creamy
2. Add the yolks of the eggs beaten to a froth
3. Add raisins, lemon rind, almonds, brandy
4. Add soda and flour sifted together
5. Last of all fold in the whites of the eggs beaten stiff
6. Bake in a tub tin

title: Indian-putinkia (Indian Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups milk
- 1/4 cup corn meal
- 1/2 cup syrup
- 3/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 teaspoon soda
- Lemon rind or ginger

## Directions

1. Cook a mush of corn meal and milk for about 15 minutes
2. Add the rest of the ingredients
3. Bake in a slow oven for 2 or 3 hours
4. Serve with hard sauce or cream

title: Inkiväärikakku No. 3 (Ginger Cake No. 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 cup milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon ginger
- Some salt

## Directions

1. Rub butter with sugar, add eggs beaten well.
2. Add milk, baking powder mixed with flour, ginger and some salt.
3. Bake in individual tins.

title: Inkiväärikakkuja (Ginger Snaps)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup molasses
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon ginger
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1 1/2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Rub butter, sugar and molasses to a froth
2. Add spices and eggs beaten until frothy
3. Mix in flour sifted with soda
4. Roll thin on a floured baking board
5. Cut in small cakes with a cutter
6. Bake in a hot oven

title: Inkiväärileipä No. 1 (Ginger Bread No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup molasses (or sugar syrup)
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 egg
- 1 large tablespoon butter
- 1/2 teaspoon ginger
- Some cinnamon and cloves
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 cup raisins
- 3 cups flour

## Directions

1. Warm molasses, rub to a froth with sugar and butter.
2. Add soda dissolved in sour milk, egg beaten until frothy, spices and flour.
3. Bake in a shallow tin in a moderately hot oven.

title: Inkiväärileipä No. 2 (Ginger Bread No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1 egg
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1/4 teaspoon soda
- 1/2 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 1/2 teaspoon ginger
- Cinnamon and mace

## Directions

1. Rub until creamy the butter with the sugar, add egg, milk and the dry ingredients sifted with flour.
2. Bake in a shallow tin in a moderate oven.

title: Inklväärikakkuja (Ginger Snaps)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cupfuls of molasses
- 3/4 cupful of butter
- 4 1/2 cupfuls of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 teaspoonful of ginger
- 1 teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Work molasses and butter to a froth
2. Add spices and flour
3. Rub the mixture to a smooth paste
4. Roll thin
5. Shape small cookies
6. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Irish Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. shoulder of mutton
- boiling water
- carrots
- 4 onions
- 2 stalks of celery
- salt
- pepper
- potatoes
- flour
- cold water
- butter
- dumplings

## Directions

1. Wipe and cut the mutton in pieces.
2. Put into a kettle and pour boiling water over.
3. Let quickly come to boil and skim.
4. Add carrots cut in nice pieces, onions, celery cut in pieces, salt and pepper.
5. Let simmer, covered, until tender.
6. About half an hour before taking the meat out add potatoes into it.
7. Thicken with flour mixed with cold water.
8. Serve with dumplings.
9. Meat may be browned with a little butter before putting water in.

title: Irish Stew
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds of shoulder of mutton
- boiling water
- carrots
- onions
- celery
- salt
- pepper
- potatoes
- flour
- dumplings

## Directions

1. Wipe and cut in pieces 3 pounds of shoulder of mutton
2. Put into a kettle and pour over it boiling water
3. Let quickly come to boil, then skim off the foam
4. Add carrots cut into nice pieces, onions, celery cut into pieces, salt and pepper
5. Let simmer slowly covered until the meat is tender
6. About half an hour before serving add potatoes
7. Thicken with flour mixed in cold water and stir in
8. Serve with dumplings
9. The meat may also be browned in a little butter before adding the water

title: Irlantilainen Muhennos (Irish Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds shoulder of mutton
- carrots
- 4 onions
- celery
- 1 tomato
- salt
- pepper
- potatoes
- flour
- cold water
- butter

## Directions

1. Wipe and cut the mutton into pieces.
2. Put in a kettle in cold water and bring quickly to boiling.
3. Skim off the foam.
4. Add carrots cut in pieces, onions, celery cut in pieces, tomato, salt and pepper.
5. Let simmer covered until the meat is tender.
6. Half an hour before the stew is done, add potatoes to it.
7. Thicken with flour diluted in cold water.
8. Serve with dumplings.

title: Jam Omelet (Hillo-omeletti)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- sugar
- 3 tablespoonfuls of milk or cream
- jam
- fruit-sauce

## Directions

1. Beat separately the whites and yolks of three eggs.
2. Mix the yolks with sugar, 3 tablespoonfuls of milk or cream and the beaten whites.
3. Fry as directed; spread with jam, fold once, and serve at once with fruit-sauce.

title: Jellied Chicken
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- celery stalks
- 1 quart of chicken stock
- ½ cupful of tomato
- 2 teaspoonfuls of meat extract
- 7 tablespoonfuls of gelatine dissolved in water or 10 leaves of gelatine
- 2 egg whites
- hard-boiled eggs
- beets
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the chicken, put on the fire to cook with salt, pepper, onion and two stalks of celery.
2. Let simmer until tender.
3. Skin, remove the bones and cut the meat into neat pieces.
4. Take 1 quart of chicken stock, ½ cupful of tomato, 2 teaspoonfuls of meat extract, 7 tablespoonfuls of gelatine dissolved in water or 10 leaves of gelatine and whites of 2 eggs.
5. Whip over the fire until boiling; let simmer 5 minutes.
6. Strain through a fine cloth.
7. Line an individual mold with cleared liquid jelly.
8. Let thicken.
9. Garnish the mold with hard-boiled eggs, beets and parsley.
10. Lay the pieces of chicken into the mold, and pour over them the jelly.
11. Set in a cold place to stiffen.

title: Jellied Squab
body:
## Ingredients

- Squab
- Stuffing for stuffed chicken
- Thin cloth

## Directions

1. Remove the bones as directed.
2. Fill with the same stuffing as directed for stuffed chicken.
3. Sew up and wrap in a thin cloth.
4. Cook about 3/4 hour, or until done.
5. Remove from the liquor and put under a tiny weight until cold and even.
6. Prepare an aspic jelly of the liquor.
7. Line with it the bottom of a mold.
8. Cut the squab crosswise into slices.
9. Arrange nicely into the mold.
10. Pour the jelly over it and set aside to get firm.

title: Jelly Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of brown sauce
- 3 tablespoonfuls of currant jelly
- a little sherry

## Directions

1. Make a cupful of brown sauce.
2. Add 3 tablespoonfuls of currant jelly and a little sherry.
3. Serve with game and mutton.

title: Jelly Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of corn starch
- 1 cupful of boiling water
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 3/4 cupful of jelly

## Directions

1. Mix sugar with corn starch.
2. Add slowly 1 cupful of boiling water and let boil 5 minutes.
3. Remove from fire and add butter and jelly.
4. Keep in double-boiler until it has to be served.

title: Jenny Lind's Soup (Jenny Lind's Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of bone- or meat-stock
- 3 tablespoonfuls of tapioca
- salt and pepper
- 2 tablespoonfuls of chopped parsley
- 2 yolks
- 1 cupful of cream

## Directions

1. Wash the tapioca and cook in stock until tender.
2. Beat the yolks and add the cream into it.
3. Pour the soup slowly in.
4. Place on the fire for a little while; stir, not allowing it to boil.
5. Serve with cheese-balls, nicely cut artichokes or small potatoes.

title: Jenny Lindin keitto (Soup à la Jenny Lind)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts bone stock or bouillon
- 3 tablespoons tapioca
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cup cream
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Rinse the tapioca, cook in bouillon until tender.
2. Beat the yolks of eggs with the cream, add gradually the soup to it, heat but not to boiling.
3. Serve with tiny potatoes or cheese balls.

title: Julienne Soup
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of rich bouillon
- 1 small celery
- 1 carrot
- 1 leek
- 1 head of lettuce
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of string beans
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the vegetables and cut them into long strips.
2. Brown in butter.
3. Add the hot bouillon and the string beans into the vegetables and let it boil for a while.
4. When the vegetables are half cooked add the head of lettuce cut into shreds.
5. When all the vegetables are tender season the soup and serve while very hot.

title: Juliennekeitto (Julienne Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts meat stock
- 1 carrot
- 1 onion
- 2 stalks celery
- 1 head lettuce
- 1½ cups string-beans
- 2 tablespoons butter
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Wash the vegetables, cut in fine strips and brown in butter.
2. Add to the vegetables the hot meat stock and the string-beans, let boil.
3. When the vegetables are half done, add the head of lettuce cut in strips.
4. When the vegetables are tender, season the soup.
5. Serve very hot.

title: Juliennekeittoa (Julienne Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts meat stock
- 1 carrot, 1 onion
- 2 stalks celery, 1 head lettuce
- 1 1/2 cups string beans
- 2 tablespoons butter
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the vegetables and cut into long thin strips and brown in butter.
2. Add the hot meat stock and string beans.

title: Juuri-artisokka (Jerusalem Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Jerusalem artichokes
- cold water
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Let Jerusalem artichokes stand in cold water for 1 or 2 hours and then peel.
2. Put on the fire in salted water mixed with lemon juice.
3. When tender, drain quickly, otherwise they will become tough.
4. Serve hot with milk sauce or cold as salad prepared the same as potato salad.

title: Juusto liuskoja (Cheese Straws)
body:
## Ingredients

- Puff paste
- Grated cheese
- Cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Roll puff paste into a sheet, ¾ inch thick
2. Sprinkle one half with grated cheese and a little cayenne
3. Fold in half and press lightly so the layers stick together
4. Cut with knife into strips 4 inches long and ½ inch wide
5. Bake 10 minutes in a hot oven
6. Serve with salad

title: Juusto-omeletti (Cheese Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup cream or milk
- 3 tablespoons grated cheese
- Salt and pepper
- Butter for the pan

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks and whites separately.
2. Mix the whipped cream or milk, cheese and salt with the yolks.
3. Add the whites.
4. Turn into a buttered omelet mold.
5. Bake.
6. Serve hot.

title: Juusto-pyöryjä No. 1 (Cheese Balls No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup grated cheese
- 1/2 tablespoon flour
- salt
- pepper
- 1 egg white, beaten firm

## Directions

1. Mix grated cheese with flour, salt, pepper and the beaten egg white.
2. Cook in deep lard.

title: Juusto-pyöryjä No. 2 (Cheese Balls No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons flour
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1 1/2 cups milk
- 9 tablespoons grated cheese
- 3 or 6 tablespoons bread crumbs
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook butter and flour in a saucepan.
2. Add milk and cheese, stirring until smooth, and let boil for a few minutes.
3. Place to cool.
4. Mix with yolks of egg and bread crumbs so as to make a batter thick enough to be formed into small balls.
5. Roll the balls in flour.
6. Cook in milk or lard or fry in butter.

title: Juusto-soufflé No. 1 (Cheese Soufflé No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping tablespoon butter
- 3 heaping tablespoons American cheese
- 2 tablespoons whipped cream
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 egg whites
- some paprika
- some mustard

## Directions

1. Stir the yolks of egg with the butter on the fire until thick.
2. Add cheese and spices.
3. Last of all mix with the whites beaten stiff.

title: Juusto-soufflé No. 2 (Cheese Soufflé No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup thick milk sauce
- 1 cup grated American cheese
- 3 eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Stir the cheese into the milk sauce.
2. Add gradually the yolks of egg, salt and pepper.
3. At the last mix with the whites of egg beaten to a firm froth.
4. Butter individual molds and fill with the mixture.
5. Bake in oven in a pan of water for 15 minutes.
6. Serve in the mold.

title: Juusto-voileipiä (Cheese Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread slices
- grated cheese
- white pepper or cayenne pepper
- American cheese
- butter
- anchovy paste
- lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- Swiss cheese
- lettuce leaves
- cottage cheese
- green pepper
- nuts
- olives
- cream

## Directions

1. Fry slices of bread light brown on one side.
2. Spread the fried side with grated cheese.
3. Sprinkle with white pepper or cayenne.
4. Lay the slices in a pan and place in hot oven.
5. When the cheese starts to melt, place the sandwiches on a napkin and serve hot.
6. Alternatively, place a thin slice of American cheese between buttered slices of bread and toast in a hot oven.
7. Or mix grated American cheese with a little butter, anchovy paste, lemon juice, salt, pepper and mustard, rub well, and spread between thin slices of bread.
8. For another variation, cut American or Swiss cheese into thin slices, place between buttered slices of bread, and use lettuce leaves if desired.
9. For cottage cheese variation, mash the cottage cheese and mix with chopped green pepper, nuts or olives, dilute with cream, and season with salt and pepper.

title: Juustoa crackersien päällä (Cheese Crackers)
body:
## Ingredients

- grated cheese
- cream
- salt
- pepper
- buttered crackers

## Directions

1. Moisten grated cheese with some cream, add salt and pepper.
2. Spread on buttered crackers.
3. Heat in oven until the cheese melts a little.

title: Juustokokkareita (Cheese Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1/2 cup milk
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons grated cheese
- Salt and pepper

## Directions
