1. Make a sauce of butter, flour and milk.
2. Stir until smooth and cook for a few minutes.
3. Remove from the fire.
4. Add seasonings, cheese and yolks of egg.
5. Drop by the spoonful into boiling bouillon or boil in salted water about 5 minutes, or until done.
6. If too soft, mix with beaten white of an egg.

title: Juustokokkareita (Cheese Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1/2 cupful of milk
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 3 tablespoonfuls of cheese crumbs
- yolks of 2 eggs
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cook a sauce of butter, flour and milk, stirring well to make a smooth paste
2. Let boil a few minutes
3. Remove the saucepan from the fire
4. Stir the seasonings, cheese and egg yolks into it
5. Drop the mixture with a teaspoon into boiling beef stock
6. Cook slowly about 5 minutes

title: Juustoliuskoja (Cheese Straws)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- grated cheese
- cayenne pepper

## Directions

1. Roll puff paste into a sheet 1/8 inch thick, sprinkle with grated cheese and some cayenne pepper.
2. Double up and press lightly together.
3. Cut with knife in straws 1/2 inch wide and 4 inches long.
4. Bake in a hot oven for 10 minutes.
5. Serve with salad.

title: Juustopiirakka No. 1 (Cheese Pie No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- Grated yellow rind of a lemon
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup whipped cream or plain
- 3 tablespoons currants
- 3 tablespoons chopped almonds
- cheese
- sugar
- spices

## Directions

1. Mix the cornstarch and flour with sugar and cheese
2. Add the cream, the spices and the eggs beaten separately
3. Bake in an open crust
4. May be made without currants, almonds and lemon and instead of cream milk may be used, if plain cheese pie is preferred

title: Juustopiirakka No. 1 (Cheese Pie No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups grated cheese
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- Grated yellow rind of a lemon
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup whipped cream or plain milk
- 3 tablespoons currants
- 3 tablespoons chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Mix the cornstarch and sugar together with the cheese.
2. Add cream or milk.
3. Add the seasonings and whipped eggs separately.
4. Bake in an open crust.

title: Juustopiirakka No. 2 (Cheese Pie No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups cottage cheese
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3 tablespoons milk or cream
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 3 eggs
- Vanilla and currants if desired
- Salt

## Directions

1. Force the cheese through a sieve
2. Add the seasonings, flour and cream
3. Beat the yolks with sugar
4. Add to the cheese
5. Mix with the beaten whites
6. Bake in an open crust until firm and the crust is brown on the bottom

title: Jälkiruoka kuivasta leivästä (French Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- Stale bread
- 1 egg
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 cup milk
- Butter
- Jam or jelly

## Directions

1. Cut stale bread in slices.
2. Beat an egg with 1 tablespoon of sugar, add 1 cup milk.
3. Dip the bread into the milk, fry in butter until brown on both sides.
4. Spread with jam or jelly.

title: Jänis-fricassee (Rabbit Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- Rabbit
- Water
- Vinegar or lemon juice
- Salt
- Flour
- Lard or butter
- Cream
- Jelly

## Directions

1. Clean the rabbit, rinse and remove the fine skin.
2. Soak in water mixed with salt and vinegar or lemon juice for 2 hours.
3. Wipe the pieces of meat, dip in flour.
4. Cook in butter or lard covered until tender.
5. Skim off the fat, add some water or bouillon.
6. Let boil quickly.
7. Season, add some cream and serve with jelly.

title: Jänis-keitto (Rabbit Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- remains of roast rabbits
- 3 quarts of water
- 1 onion
- 2 stalks of celery
- 2 sprigs of parsley
- 1 small parsnip
- 2 tomatoes
- 10 peppercorns
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 3 tablespoonfuls of sherry or lemon juice
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the bones and put them in boiling water on fire.
2. Scrape the vegetables, cut into pieces and brown in butter.
3. Then put them to boil with the bones.
4. Add the seasonings parsley and the tomatoes.
5. Let boil slowly for a few hours.
6. Thicken with butter and flour; season and strain.
7. Serve with the meat cut into cubes.
8. The soup may also be served with small potatoes or rice in it.

title: Jäniskeitto (Rabbit Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Remains of roast rabbit
- 3 quarts water
- 1 onion
- 2 stalks celery
- 1 small parsnip
- 2 sprigs parsley
- ¼ cup tomato
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 10 peppercorns
- Salt and lemon juice

## Directions

1. Crack the bones, put on the fire in boiling water.
2. Peel the vegetables, cut in pieces and brown in butter.
3. Add to the bone stock, season; add the parsley and the tomato.
4. Simmer for a few hours.
5. Thicken with butter and flour.
6. Serve with tiny potatoes or rice.

title: Jää-kahvi (Iced Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- black coffee
- cracked ice
- sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. Prepare black coffee
2. Strain and cool
3. Half fill glasses with cracked ice
4. Fill with coffee
5. Sweeten with sugar
6. Add cream if desired

title: Jää-kahvi (Iced Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- ground coffee
- water
- cracked ice
- sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare black coffee
2. Strain and cool
3. Half fill glasses with cracked ice
4. Fill with coffee
5. Sweeten with sugar

title: Jää-tee (Iced Tea)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 teaspoons tea
- 1 cup water
- cracked ice
- 1 slice of lemon
- granulated sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Prepare tea allowing 2 teaspoons tea to 1 cup water
2. Half fill glasses with cracked ice
3. Put a slice of lemon in each glass
4. Strain the tea and fill the glasses with it
5. Add granulated sugar according to taste

title: Jäädytettyjä tomaattia (Frozen Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tomatoes of equal size
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onion juice
- Chopped cucumber (if preferred)
- Mayonnaise
- Cream
- Dissolved gelatine (optional)
- Lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Stick tomatoes into boiling water so as to remove the skin easier.
2. Cut off the tops and scoop the insides.
3. Place the tomatoes top down on ice.
4. Mix tomato with salt, pepper and onion juice.
5. Add chopped cucumber if preferred.
6. Mix with a dressing made of equal parts of mayonnaise and cream.
7. Put into a mold, pack in ice and salt.
8. Fill the tomatoes with the frozen mixture, arrange on a bed of lettuce.
9. If some dissolved gelatine is used in the mixture, it will freeze quicker.
10. Can be served on a bed of lettuce having the tomatoes chopped and added to the mixture.

title: Jäätelö Marengin Kanssa (Baked Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Frozen ice cream
- 5 egg whites
- 5 tablespoons sifted powdered sugar
- Sponge cake slices

## Directions

1. Fill individual molds with ice cream and pack tight in cracked ice and salt
2. Beat egg whites until stiff, add sifted powdered sugar and continue beating
3. Cut thin slices of sponge cake and place on dessert plates with wet cloth strips on the edges
4. Unmold ice cream onto cake slices
5. Cover with beaten egg whites, taking care not to leave any openings
6. Place plates in a pan and brown meringue quickly in a very hot oven

title: Jäätynyttä Kermiakiisseliä (Frozen Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups whipped cream
- 1/2 cup macaroons cut in pieces
- Stewed fruit cut in pieces
- Sugar
- Vanilla and maraschino

## Directions

1. Mix together all the ingredients
2. Pour mixture into ice cream mold
3. Pack in a pail of cracked ice and salt
4. Let stand for 4-5 hours
5. Serve unmolded garnished with fruit and macaroons

title: Kaakao (Cocoa)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 cups milk
- 3 1/2 tablespoons cocoa
- 3/4 cup sugar
- a pinch of salt
- 1 cup boiling water
- whipped cream

## Directions

1. Scald 5 cups milk
2. Dissolve 3 1/2 tablespoons cocoa in water
3. Add 3/4 cup sugar and a pinch of salt
4. Pour 1 cup boiling water over
5. Bring to boil
6. Pour in the scalded milk
7. Serve with whipped cream or plain

title: Kaakao (Cocoa)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 cupfuls of milk
- 3½ tablespoonfuls of cocoa
- ¼ cupful of sugar
- a dash of salt
- 1 cupful of boiling water

## Directions

1. Scald 5 cupfuls of milk
2. Mix with 3½ tablespoonfuls of cocoa, ¼ cupful of sugar and a dash of salt
3. Add 1 cupful of boiling water into it
4. Cook for 1 minute
5. Cocoa may be also whipped in double boiler for 5 minutes
6. Serve with whipped cream or as it is

title: Kaali laatikko (Cabbage Casserole)
body:
## Ingredients

- kaalia
- voita
- sokeria
- sienipallerotaikina
- rasvaa
- kaalinlientä

## Directions

1. Kaali keitetään suolatussa vedessä pehmeäksi, paloitellaan ja paistetaan voissa ruskeaksi, 1 teelus. sokeria seassa.
2. Sienipallerotaikina valmistetaan ja siitä tehdään pieniä palleroita, jotka keitetään rasvassa, tai paistetaan voissa.
3. Pannaan kerroksittain voideltuun vormuun kaalia ja palleroita, kaalikerros viimeisenä.
4. Vähän kaalinlientä kaadetaan päälle ja paistetaan uunissa 1 tunti.

title: Kaali Laatikko (Molded Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salted water
- butter
- sugar, 1 teaspoon
- mushroom croquette paste
- cabbage broth

## Directions

1. Cook cabbage in salted water until soft
2. Cut into pieces and fry in butter until browned, adding 1 teaspoon sugar
3. Prepare mushroom croquette paste and form into small balls
4. Cook the balls in deep lard or fry in butter
5. Layer cabbage and mushroom balls in a buttered mold, ending with a cabbage layer
6. Pour some cabbage broth over the top
7. Bake in oven for 1 hour

title: Kaali- ja Riisi-Vanukas (Pudding of Cabbage with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cabbage
- 1 cupful of rice
- 2 cupfuls of water
- 2 cupfuls of milk
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 eggs
- salt, pepper, a dash of sugar

## Directions

1. Cook rice in water mixed with milk until tender
2. Boil cabbage in salted water
3. Chop the cabbage
4. Put into a kettle to cook with butter until thoroughly done
5. Mix all above ingredients with the batter of rice
6. Add eggs at the last
7. Pour in a buttered mold
8. Bake about 1 hour

title: Kaali- ja riisivanukas (Cabbage and Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup rice
- water
- milk
- cabbage
- salt
- butter
- pepper
- sugar
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil 1/2 cup of rice in water and milk to a batter.
2. Parboil the cabbage in salt water, drain, chop, and cook with butter in a saucepan until tender.
3. Mix the cabbage with rice and season with salt and pepper, sweeten with some sugar.
4. Add 2 eggs beaten at the last.
5. Turn into a buttered mold and bake about an hour.

title: Kaali-keitto (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarts of meat stock
- 1/2 head of cabbage
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- pepper and salt
- meatballs

## Directions

1. Cook the head of cabbage in salted water.
2. Drain well.
3. Cut into narrow strips and fry in butter in a pan until brown, turning often.
4. Pour hot meat stock into it and boil until the cabbage is soft.
5. Season with salt and pepper and serve with small meatballs.

title: Kaali-kääryjä (Finnish Cabbage Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chopped meat, bread crumbs, milk, eggs, onion, pepper, salt and butter mixed together
- 1 cupful of rice
- one cabbage
- 3-4 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 4 teaspoonfuls of sugar
- 4 teaspoonfuls of salt

## Directions

1. Cook one cabbage and take off outside leaves
2. Remove tough stalk carefully
3. Boil rice until tender in a large quantity of water
4. Drain off the water
5. Mix rice with the meat and chopped and fried small inside leaves
6. Put this mixture by spoonfuls on the leaves
7. Roll together
8. Brown on both sides
9. Sprinkle with salt, sugar and pepper
10. Pour bouillon over and put in oven
11. Bake 1-1½ hours, turning often
12. Place on a serving dish
13. Prepare sauce of liquor in pan by adding flour and cream
14. Strain sauce over the rolls

title: Kaali-muhennus (Stewed Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 (2 1/2 lbs.) cabbage
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 3 tablespoonfuls of vinegar
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1/2 quart of boiling water
- 1 cupful of water

## Directions

1. Cut cabbage into pieces
2. Parboil in salted water
3. Pour the water out
4. Stir butter, sugar, salt and 1/2 quart of boiling water into it
5. Let the cabbage simmer covered until tender (about 1 hour)
6. Mix one cupful of water with flour
7. Thicken the cabbage with it
8. Pour vinegar in
9. Let the stew boil 10 minutes
10. Serve with mutton or tongue

title: Kaali-sienipallerot (Cabbage and Mushroom Ball Casserole)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Salted water
- Sugar
- 1 teaspoon
- Butter
- Mushroom ball paste
- Cabbage broth

## Directions

1. Cook the cabbage in salted water until tender
2. Cut the cabbage in pieces
3. Brown in butter with 1 teaspoon sugar
4. Prepare a mushroom ball paste
5. Form the paste into tiny balls
6. Fry the balls in butter
7. Put into a buttered mold alternate layers of cabbage and mushroom balls, having a layer of cabbage on top
8. Surround with cabbage broth
9. Bake for 1 hour

title: Kaali-Timboleja (Cabbage Timbale)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cabbage
- 3/4 pound butter
- 5 tablespoonfuls cooked rice
- 1/2 pound wheat flour
- 3 cupfuls milk or vegetable broth
- 4 eggs
- pepper
- nutmeg
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook cabbage in salted water and grind in a meat grinder with the rice
2. Prepare a sauce of butter, flour and milk
3. Mix the ground cabbage with the sauce, add seasonings and beaten egg yolks
4. Finally fold in egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
5. Bake either in small timbale molds or in a mold with a hole in the center, placed in a pan of water
6. May also be baked as balls
7. Serve with tomato or milk sauce

title: Kaali-timboleja (Cabbage Timbales)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cabbage
- 1/2 pound butter
- 5 tablespoons boiled rice
- 3/4 cup flour
- 3 cups milk or vegetable stock
- 4 eggs
- Pepper
- Mace
- Salt
- Tomato sauce or milk sauce for serving

## Directions

1. Cook the cabbage in salted water
2. Force through a meat grinder with the boiled rice
3. Make a sauce of butter, flour and milk
4. Add the cabbage and rice to the sauce
5. Add spices and the whipped egg yolks
6. Mix in the whites of the eggs beaten firm
7. Bake in timbale molds or in a dome-shaped mold
8. Serve with tomato or milk sauce
9. The cabbage may be mixed with tomato, if preferred

title: Kaalia omenain kanssa (Cabbage with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of cabbage
- 4 or 6 apples
- 4 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- 1/2 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage in strips, put in a saucepan.
2. Add apples pared and cut in pieces, butter, lemon juice and sugar.
3. Cook slowly tightly covered until tender, about 2 hours.

title: Kaalikeitto (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarts bouillon
- 1 small head of cabbage
- 1 carrot
- 1 onion
- 1 stalk of celery
- salt and pepper
- meat balls

## Directions

1. Parboil the cabbage in salted water, drain well.
2. Cut the cabbage in fine strips and fry in butter constantly stirring.
3. Add the hot meat stock and continue cooking until the cabbage is tender.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Serve with small meat balls or plain.
6. The soup may be made without browning the cabbage and using water boiled with lean meat instead of bouillon.

title: Kaalikääryjä (Cabbage Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ground meat
- Bread crumbs
- Milk
- Eggs
- Onion
- Pepper
- Salt
- Butter
- 1/2 cup rice
- 1 head of cabbage
- 3 or 4 tablespoons butter
- 4 teaspoons sugar
- 4 teaspoons salt
- Bouillon
- Flour
- Cream

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of ground meat, bread crumbs, milk, egg, onion, pepper, salt and butter
2. Boil the cabbage whole and remove the outside leaves and tough stalk
3. Boil the rice in plenty of water and strain; mix with the meat
4. Add the small leaves of cabbage chopped and fried in butter
5. Place this mixture on leaves, one spoonful on each, and roll together
6. Brown on both sides
7. Add a sprinkling of sugar, salt and pepper
8. Pour some bouillon into the pan and place the pan into oven
9. Bake from 1 to 1 1/2 hours, turning often
10. Arrange on a platter
11. Make a gravy of the stock in the pan by adding flour and cream
12. Strain over the rolls

title: Kaalilaatikko (Cabbage Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salted water
- butter
- sugar
- mushroom ball mixture
- cabbage broth

## Directions

1. Cook the cabbage in salted water until tender
2. Cut in pieces and brown in butter with 1 teaspoon sugar
3. Prepare mushroom ball paste and form into tiny balls
4. Fry the balls in butter
5. Put into a buttered mold in alternate layers of cabbage and mushroom balls with cabbage layer last
6. Pour some cabbage broth on top
7. Bake in oven for 1 hour

title: Kaalimuhennus (Stewed Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 or 2 tablespoons sugar
- Salt
- 2 cups boiling water
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Juice of 1 or 2 lemons or 3 tablespoons vinegar
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage in strips, parboil in salted water and drain.
2. Mix with 1 tablespoon of butter, 1 or 2 tablespoons of sugar, salt and 2 cups of boiling water.
3. Let simmer covered until tender.
4. Thicken with 1 tablespoon of flour mixed in cold water.
5. Add the juice of 1 or 2 lemons or 3 tablespoons of vinegar, season and continue boiling for 10 minutes.

title: Kaalipalleroita (Cabbage Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Rich milk sauce
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onion
- 3 eggs
- Flour
- Butter

## Directions

1. Boil the cabbage, force through a meat chopper.
2. Add rich milk sauce until it becomes a thick porridge.
3. Season with salt and pepper, flavor with chopped fried onion.
4. Mix in 3 beaten eggs.
5. Form into croquettes or patties, roll in flour, brown in butter.

title: Kaalisalaatti (Cabbage Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small head of cabbage
- French dressing
- celery
- Spanish onion
- peanuts
- boiled salad dressing
- olives
- apples

## Directions

1. Shred or cut the cabbage into cubes
2. Serve mixed with French dressing
3. Alternatively, take equal parts of cabbage and celery, add a Spanish onion chopped and some peanuts
4. Mix with French dressing or boiled salad dressing
5. Sprinkle the top with chopped olives
6. Instead of celery, apples may be used, omitting the onions

title: Kaalisalaatti (Cold Slaw)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- ice water
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/2 cup salad oil
- 1/2 cup vinegar or juice of one lemon
- salt
- pepper
- mustard

## Directions

1. Shred the cabbage, rinse and let stand in ice water.
2. Drain and wrap into a towel.
3. Make a dressing of 3 egg yolks mixed with 1/2 cup of salad oil, 1/2 cup vinegar or the juice of a lemon.
4. Season with salt, pepper and mustard.
5. Add dressing to the cabbage.

title: Kaalisalaatti (Cold Slaw)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Ice water
- Egg yolks
- Salad oil
- Vinegar or lemon juice
- Salt
- Pepper
- Mustard
- Cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut cabbage into shreds
2. Wash and let stand in ice water
3. Drain and wrap in a cloth to draw out the moisture
4. Pour over it a sauce made of 3 yolks of eggs mixed gradually with ½ cupful of salad oil, ½ cupful of vinegar or juice of one lemon
5. Season with salt, pepper and mustard
6. Dressing for salad may be made also of cream
7. For hot slaw: Cook cabbage until tender and pour over it Bearnaise sauce or chill the cabbage and serve cold with French dressing

title: Kahvi (Coffee)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup ground coffee
- 6 cups water
- egg white or egg shells
- cold water

## Directions

1. Put into a coffee pot the ground coffee with the white or the shells of an egg
2. Mix with cold water
3. Pour boiling water over
4. Boil for 5 minutes
5. Place on the side of the range and let stand for 10 minutes to set

title: Kahvi Kakkuja (Coffee Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of milk
- 1 whole egg
- 1 egg yolk
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- ½ teaspoonful of lemon juice
- nutmeg
- ½ teaspoonfuls of salt
- 1 cake of yeast
- flour
- finely chopped almonds
- crystal sugar

## Directions

1. Heat milk, add yeast and 1 cupful of flour, cover and let rise.
2. Add eggs beaten with sugar, butter rubbed into foam, salt, lemon juice and nutmeg and enough flour to make ordinary thick dough.
3. Stir mixture well, cover and let rise.
4. Roll dough ¾ inch thick, brush with melted butter, sprinkle with finely chopped almonds and crystal sugar.
5. Fold dough in three layers and cut cakes with round form.
6. Place cakes on buttered pan.

title: Kahvi munahyydyke (Coffee Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of milk
- 3 eggs
- 3/4 cupful of sugar
- a pinch of salt
- a pinch of nutmeg
- 2 tablespoonfuls of ground coffee

## Directions

1. Boil the milk with 2 tablespoonfuls of ground coffee
2. Strain the milk
3. Beat eggs, add sugar, salt and nutmeg
4. Pour the scalded milk gradually into it
5. Put in a buttered mold and steam in oven until firm

title: Kahvi täyte (Coffee Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of butter
- 2 cupfuls of sugar
- 4 tablespoonfuls of coffee
- 4 tablespoonfuls of cocoa
- 1 teaspoonful of vanilla

## Directions

1. Cream butter with sugar
2. Add cocoa, coffee and vanilla
3. Rub to a smooth paste

title: Kahvi-Kermajäätelö (Coffee Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cream
- ½ cup black coffee
- 1½ cups sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- Vanilla
- 1 cup ground coffee
- 2½ cups water

## Directions

1. Make black coffee by boiling 1 cup coffee in 2½ cups water for 10 minutes.
2. Strain through a fine cloth and let cool.
3. Make a mixture of one half of the cream, yolks and sugar as directed.
4. When cool, add the rest of the cream, vanilla and coffee.
5. Freeze.

title: Kahvihyytelö (Coffee Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cups water
- 1/2 or 3/4 cup black coffee
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons or 1/2 package gelatine
- whipped cream for serving

## Directions

1. Prepare as given for lemon jelly, put into a mold to set
2. Serve with whipped cream

title: Kahvihyytelö (Coffee Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2½ cupfuls of water
- 1 oz. of gelatine
- 1 cupful of sugar
- ½–¾ cupful of strong coffee

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same manner as lemon jelly.
2. Pour into a mold to get firm.
3. Serve with whipped cream.

title: Kahvikakku (Coffee Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 cup brown sugar
- 1 cup molasses
- 5 eggs
- 1 cup coffee
- 1/2 cup raisins
- 1/2 cup currants
- 1/2 cup lemon rind chopped
- 4 cups flour
- 4 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/4 teaspoon cloves
- 1/4 teaspoon mace
- 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg

## Directions

1. Rub until creamy the butter with the sugar
2. Add the beaten yolks of the eggs
3. Add molasses, coffee, spices
4. Add raisins and currants rolled in flour, baking powder and flour sifted together
5. Last of all add the whites beaten to a firm froth
6. Bake in a tub tin

title: Kahvikakku I (Coffee Cake I)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 3 cupfuls of brown sugar
- 1 cupful of melted butter
- 4 cupfuls of flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls of soda
- 1 cupful of sour cream
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of currants
- 1 cupful of sweet almonds
- 1 yellow lemon rind
- 10 cardamoms

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks with sugar until light
2. Mix with butter, cream and soda dissolved in cream, currants, almonds, lemon rind, cardamom and flour
3. Fold in the stiffly beaten whites
4. Bake 1 hour

title: Kahvikakku II (Coffee Cake II)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of butter
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of sugar
- 1 cupful of molasses
- yolks of 5 eggs
- 1 cupful of coffee
- 1/2 cupful of raisins, cut in pieces
- 1/2 cupful of currants
- 1/4 cupful of lemon rind, finely chopped
- 3 teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- 3 cupfuls of flour
- 3/4 teaspoonful of cinnamon
- 3/4 teaspoonful of cloves
- 3/4 teaspoonful of mace
- 3/4 teaspoonful of nutmeg

## Directions

1. Work sugar and butter to a froth
2. Add beaten yolks of eggs, molasses, coffee, spices, floured fruit and baking powder mixed with flour
3. Bake in a deep mold

title: Kahvikakkuja (Coffee Buns)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk
- 1 whole egg and 1 yolk
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1½ teaspoon lemon juice
- ½ teaspoon salt
- Dash of mace
- 1 yeast cake
- Flour
- Sugar
- Chopped almonds
- Vanilla

## Directions

1. Warm the milk, dissolve the yeast in it, add 1 cup flour; cover, let rise.
2. Work in the eggs beaten with sugar, the butter rubbed until creamy, salt, lemon juice, mace and flour enough to make a moderately thick dough.
3. Knead well, cover, let rise.
4. Roll the dough in a 1/4 inch thick sheet, brush with melted butter, sprinkle with chopped almonds and granulated sugar.
5. Triple up and cut with a round cutter in cakes.
6. Place the cakes to rise on a buttered baking sheet.
7. Brush over with egg and drop with a spoon in the center of each bun the following mixture: 1 egg yolk, 1 tablespoon sugar, 2 tablespoons chopped almonds and some vanilla beaten together.
8. Sprinkle with granulated sugar.
9. Bake in a moderate oven.

title: Kahvimunahyydyke (Coffee Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups milk
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- Salt and nutmeg
- 2 tablespoons ground coffee

## Directions

1. Add 2 tablespoons of ground coffee to the milk
2. Bring to a boil and strain
3. Prepare the same way as baked custard, using the coffee-infused milk instead of plain milk
4. Beat the eggs and add sugar, salt and nutmeg
5. Pour gradually the milk mixture into the eggs
6. Bake in a buttered mold in a pan of water until firm

title: Kahvimunahyydyke (Coffee Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons ground coffee
- Milk
- Eggs
- Sugar
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Prepare as given for baked custard
2. Boil 2 tablespoons of ground coffee in milk
3. Strain the milk
4. Use the coffee-infused milk to prepare custard
5. Alternatively, use 1 cup of black coffee and 1 cup of cream instead of milk

title: Kakkuja makeista perunoista (Sweet Potato Patties)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mashed sweet potatoes
- Butter
- Salt
- Cream

## Directions

1. Mix mashed sweet potatoes with salt, butter and some cream
2. Form into patties
3. Fry or bake until brown

title: Kala pihviä (Fish Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- crackerdust
- onion
- cream

## Directions

1. Scale the fish and remove the backbone
2. Scrape the flesh from the skin and remove all small bones
3. Grind the flesh through a meat grinder several times
4. Mix butter pieces into the flesh while grinding
5. Season with salt and pepper
6. Form into round, flat cakes
7. Dip in egg and roll in crackerdust
8. Fry in butter or lard
9. Place the fish cakes on a serving dish
10. Line with fried slices of onion
11. Add a little cream to the drippings
12. Pour the sauce over the fish

title: Kala-hakkelusta (Fish Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold prepared fish
- cold boiled potatoes
- salt and pepper
- lard or butter
- 1 onion

## Directions

1. Take cold, prepared fish and the same amount of cold boiled potatoes and chop them separately into fine pieces.
2. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Melt a piece of lard or put a little butter in a pan, cook one finely chopped onion in it.
4. Add the fish and potato hash to it and stir until it is heated.
5. Then fry it brown on the bottom.
6. Turn like an omelette and place on a serving-dish.

title: Kala-kotletteja (Fish Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of cold boiled or fried fish (salmon, haddock, halibut, etc.)
- 1 cupful of mashed potatoes
- yolks of 2 eggs
- a little cream
- salt and pepper
- breadcrumbs
- lard

## Directions

1. Mix cold boiled or fried fish with mashed potatoes.
2. Mix with yolks of 2 eggs and a little cream, season with salt and pepper.
3. Form into cakes or balls.
4. Dip in breadcrumbs, egg and again in breadcrumbs.
5. Cook in boiling lard.
6. Serve with Bearnaise, Hollandaise or Tartar sauce.

title: Kala-kvenellejä (Fish Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound of boned fish
- 2 tablespoonfuls butter
- 1 tablespoonful cream
- 1 egg
- flour

## Directions

1. Grind the boned fish
2. Pound well with the butter
3. Force through a sieve
4. Add cream and egg, working the mixture to a smooth paste
5. Add flour enough to make a firm batter
6. Form into small quenelles
7. Cook in salted water or in fish stock
8. Serve in fish or crab soup

title: Kala-kvenellejä (Fish Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 lb. of fish meat
- 2 ounces of butter
- 1 tablespoon of cream
- 1 egg
- flour

## Directions

1. Remove bones from fish and grind the meat in a meat grinder
2. Mix well with butter
3. Press through a sieve
4. Add the other ingredients and blend to a smooth paste
5. Add just enough flour to hold the mixture together
6. Form into small balls
7. Cook in salted water or fish stock
8. Serve in fish or crab soup

title: Kala-liemi (Fish Stock)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lbs. small fishes
- 3 quarts of water
- 1 carrot
- 1 root of celery
- 2 sprigs of parsley
- 1 leek
- 6 peppercorns
- salt
- bit of bay leaf
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the fishes but do not scale them.
2. Cut into pieces and put in cold salted water.
3. Place on the fire.
4. When coming to a boil add the vegetables into it.
5. Season and add a little bit of butter.
6. Let cook covered for an hour.
7. Strain through a fine cloth.
8. Fish stock may be used for different kinds of fish soups prepared with vegetables (spinach, cauliflower, asparagus, potatoes, rice etc.) in the same way as meat stock.
9. If brown fish stock is wanted, brown the vegetables in a small quantity of butter before putting them into the soup.
10. Fish stock may be made richer by adding eggs and cream.

title: Kala-palloja kuivasta kalasta (Fish Balls of Dried Fish Flakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- salt dried fish
- cold boiled fish
- hot mashed potatoes
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1-2 eggs
- flour

## Directions

1. If kala is too salty, soak it in water.
2. Cook and let cool.
3. Make a mixture of equal quantities of boiled fish and hot mashed potatoes.
4. Add 2 tablespoonfuls of butter and stir well.
5. Add 1-2 eggs.
6. Form into balls or cakes.
7. Dip in flour and place in refrigerator.
8. Can be kept overnight.
9. Fry either in lard or butter.
10. For a nicer appearance, dip in egg and breadcrumbs.

title: Kala-putinki (Fish Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of remains of fish
- 1½ cupful of rich milk
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of crackercrumbs
- 1½ teaspoonful of salt
- ½ onion
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon juice
- chopped parsley
- pepper

## Directions

1. Bone the fish, chop with the meat chopper.
2. Fry onion in butter, add crackercrumbs and milk, add gently to the fish, mixing thoroughly.
3. Mix with beaten yolks, rub through a sieve.
4. Add salt, pepper, lemon juice and chopped parsley.
5. Put in a buttered mold.
6. Bake in oven.

title: Kala-timboleja (Fish Timbales)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fish, boned
- 6 ounces butter
- 2 ounces bread
- 1.5 cups cream
- 4 eggs
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Force the fish through a meat chopper several times.
2. Pound well in a wooden bowl.
3. Add gradually the creamed butter, yolks of egg one by one and the bread soaked in cream.
4. Continue rubbing until the mixture becomes very fluffy.
5. Last of all add the whites of egg beaten stiff and seasonings.
6. Bake in timbale molds in a pan of water covered with buttered paper.
7. Unmold and serve with any kind of sauce given for fish.

title: Kala-Timboleja (Mock Fish Timbale)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 onions
- 3/4 pound butter
- 1/2 pound wheat flour
- 3 cupfuls boiling milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoonful chopped dill
- pepper
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Chop 2 onions and cook in 3/4 pound butter without browning
2. Add 1/2 pound wheat flour and stir in 3 cupfuls boiling milk, whisking well until it pulls away from the sides of the pan
3. Allow to cool
4. Add 5 egg yolks one at a time, along with 1 tablespoonful chopped dill and pepper
5. Finally fold in egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
6. Bake in a fish mold or in timbale molds placed in a pan of water, covered with buttered paper
7. Serve with lemon or hollandaise sauce and boiled rice

title: Kala-timboleja (Mock Fish Timbales)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 onions
- 1/2 pound butter
- 1/4 pound boiled rice
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Scalded milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon chopped dill
- Salt
- Pepper
- Egg whites
- Hollandaise or lemon sauce for serving

## Directions

1. Chop 2 onions
2. Cook with 1/2 pound of butter
3. Add 1/4 pound of boiled ground rice and 1 tablespoon flour
4. Pour slowly scalded milk into the mixture until it becomes a thick porridge
5. Stir briskly
6. Remove from the fire and let cool
7. Add 5 egg yolks one by one and 1 tablespoon of chopped dill
8. Season with salt and pepper
9. Mix in the whites beaten until firm
10. Bake in timbale molds in a pan of water covered with buttered paper
11. Serve with Hollandaise or lemon sauce

title: Kala-vatkuli (Fish Chowder)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 lbs. of fresh fish
- 4 cupfuls of potatoes
- 1 onion
- 1/2 lb. of pork
- 3 cupfuls of milk
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- pepper and salt
- crackers

## Directions

1. Bone the fish and cut in pieces 1 inch long.
2. Fry onion in butter, strain and put the butter again into the frying pan to fry the fish in.
3. Cut pork in cubes, roll in flour, and fry with a lump of butter, taking care not to burn it.
4. Drain and fry the boiled potatoes in the drippings, cooking them for a little while.
5. Put into the kettle layers of potatoes, fish, pork and onion.
6. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
7. Pour on it a little fish stock, let boil 10 min.
8. Add scalded milk to it, mix with a little butter, chopped parsley and crackers soaked in milk, and let come to a boil.
9. Turn the fish chowder on a serving dish, cover with soaked crackers and pour the sauce over.

title: Kalaa maitosoosin kanssa (Creamed Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- onion juice
- egg yolks
- fish
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Make a milk sauce of 1 tablespoon of butter, 1 tablespoon flour and 1½ cups of milk.
2. Season with salt, pepper and onion juice.
3. Add 2 egg yolks last of all.
4. Mix with 2 cups of fish, cut finely with bones removed.
5. Bake in a buttered mold, sprinkled with bread crumbs in a moderate oven until brown.

title: Kalaa maitosoosin kanssa (Creamed Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- eggs
- remains of fish

## Directions

1. Make the milk sauce of 1 tablespoonful of butter, 1 tablespoonful of flour and 1½ cupful of milk; season with salt, pepper and lemon juice.
2. Add yolks of 2 eggs to it.
3. Mix with 2 cupfuls of remains of fish boned and flaked into bits.
4. Put in a buttered mold, dredge with breadcrumbs.
5. Bake in a moderate oven until brown.

title: Kalahakkelusta (Fish Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boned fish
- cold boiled potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- butter or lard
- onion

## Directions

1. Chop separately cold boned fish and equal amount of cold boiled potato, season with salt and pepper.
2. Melt some butter or lard in a frying pan, fry one chopped onion in it.
3. Put the fish and potato in the pan, stirring until hot.
4. Let fry until brown underneath.
5. Fold as an omelet and turn onto a platter.

title: Kalakotletteja (Fish Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups cold boiled or fried fish (salmon, haddock, sole, or similar firm-fleshed fish)
- 1 cup mashed potatoes
- 2 egg yolks
- cream or milk
- salt
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- egg
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Mix cold boiled or fried fish with mashed potatoes.
2. Add two egg yolks, some cream or milk, season with salt and pepper.
3. Form into cakes or balls.
4. Roll in bread crumbs, dip in egg, and roll in bread crumbs again.
5. Fry with butter or cook in lard, or if frying in a pan, roll in flour only.
6. Serve with Bearnaise, Hollandaise, or tartar sauce.

title: Kalaliemi (Fish Stock)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds small fish
- 3 quarts water
- 1 carrot
- 1 stalk celery
- 2 sprigs parsley
- 1 leek
- 5 peppercorns
- Salt
- Butter

## Directions

1. Clean the fish leaving the scales on.
2. Place on the fire in cold salted water, having the fish cut in pieces.
3. When coming to boil add the vegetables, spices and a small lump of butter.
4. Cook covered for an hour.
5. Strain through a fine cloth.

title: Kalamiireketta (Fish Farce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ pounds fish, pike or bass
- 3 cups cream
- 5 eggs
- ½ pound butter
- ¾ pound white bread
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Scrape the fish off the skin, remove bones, force through a meat chopper several times
2. Place the fish into a wooden bowl or mortar and pound well, adding gradually foamed whipped butter and egg yolks one at a time
3. Soak bread in cream and add gradually to the fish mixture, continue pounding until the mixture looks fluffy
4. Last of all add foamed whipped egg whites and seasonings
5. Place the mixture in a buttered mold, cover with lid in a pan of water in the oven and bake 1-1½ hours
6. Serve with mushroom, crayfish, caper or lemon sauce

title: Kalan täyte I (Fish Stuffing I)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1 small onion, minced
- parsley, minced
- pickles
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Put butter in frying pan and cook the onion in it
2. Add bread soaked in a little water or fish broth
3. Add parsley, salt and pepper
4. Fry on gentle heat for a while
5. If desired, soft oyster meat can also be used in the stuffing, mixed in last

title: Kalan täyte II (Fish Stuffing II)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup rice
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 hard-cooked eggs, minced finely
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 8 whole cooked prunes

## Directions

1. Cook rice in salted water
2. Strain off the broth
3. Mix melted butter, chopped eggs, prunes and salt with the rice
4. Use this mixture to stuff the fish

title: Kalantäyte No. 1 (Fish Stuffing No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1 small onion, chopped
- Chopped parsley
- Pickles
- Salt
- Pepper
- Water or fish stock
- Egg (optional)
- Oyster soft parts (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the onion in butter in a frying-pan.
2. Add the bread crumbs soaked in water or fish stock, parsley, salt and pepper.
3. Fry on a slow fire.
4. Add an egg if desired.
5. Oyster may be used for stuffing also.

title: Kalantäyte No. 2 (Fish Stuffing No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup rice
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 hard boiled eggs, chopped
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 8 plums

## Directions

1. Boil the rice in salt water.
2. Drain.
3. Mix with melted butter, chopped eggs, plums and salt.
4. Use for stuffing. Leave out the plums if desired.

title: Kalantäyte No. 2 (Stuffing No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup rice
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 hard boiled chopped eggs
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 8 plums

## Directions

1. Boil the rice in salt water and drain.
2. Mix with melted butter, chopped eggs, plums and salt.
3. Use for stuffing. Leave out the plums if preferred.

title: Kalapalleroita kuivasta kalasta (Fish Balls of Fish Flakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- salt fish or fresh fish
- water
- mashed potatoes
- butter
- eggs
- flour
- bread crumbs
- lard

## Directions

1. If very salty, soak the fish in water.
2. Boil, let cool.
3. Mix equal parts of fish and mashed potatoes.
4. Add two tablespoons of butter and 1 or 2 eggs, rubbing well.
5. Form into balls or patties.
6. Sprinkle with flour, let stand in a cold place.
7. Fry in butter or lard.
8. If dipped in egg and rolled in bread crumbs, they will look nicer.
9. Potatoes and fish may be boiled together if preferred.

title: Kalapihviä (Fish Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- bread crumbs
- onions, sliced and fried
- cream

## Directions

1. Scale the fish, clean, rinse and wipe.
2. Remove the backbone.
3. Run through a meat chopper several times, mixing it while grinding with lumps of butter.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Form into round flat cakes.
6. Dip in egg and bread crumbs.
7. Fry in butter or lard.
8. Place on a platter.
9. Line with a few sliced fried onions.
10. Add a little cream into the drippings and pour on the fish.

title: Kalaputinki (Fish Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups remains of fish
- 1½ cups rich milk
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1 tablespoon bread crumbs
- 1½ teaspoons salt
- ⅔ onion
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- 3 eggs
- Chopped parsley and pepper

## Directions

1. Remove the bones and force the fish through a meat grinder
2. Fry the onion in butter, add bread crumbs and milk, mix with fish rubbing well
3. Add the eggs beaten to a froth
4. Force the mixture through a strainer, add salt, pepper, lemon juice and some chopped parsley
5. Pour into a buttered mold
6. Bake in oven in a pan of water

title: Kalasalaattia No. 1 (Fish Salad No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mayonnaise
- Cold fish, any kind, cut in pieces
- Hard boiled eggs, chopped
- Pickles or cucumbers, chopped and cut in pieces
- Lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Mix any kind of cold fish cut in pieces with mayonnaise.
2. Add hard boiled eggs, chopped cucumber cut in pieces and chopped pickles.
3. Serve on lettuce leaves.

title: Kalasalaattia No. 2 (Fish Salad No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons gelatine
- 0.5 cup water
- 1 cup fish stock
- 0.5 cup mayonnaise
- 1.5 cups whipped cream
- 2 cups cold fish, cut in cubes
- Lettuce leaves
- Cucumbers
- French dressing

## Directions

1. Melt 2 tablespoons of gelatine in 0.5 cup of water.
2. Add 1 cup of fish stock, heat until the gelatine is melted, let cool.
3. When the mixture begins to thicken, add 0.5 cup of mayonnaise, whipping it until firm.
4. Mix with 1.5 cups of whipped cream and last of all add 2 cups of cold fish cut in cubes.
5. Moisten the fish mold with water mixed with gelatine, put the mixture in, let thicken.
6. Serve on a platter having lettuce leaves and cucumber around the fish.
7. Pour French dressing over.

title: Kalavatkuli (Fish Chowder)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds fresh fish
- 4 cups potatoes
- 1 onion
- 1½ pounds salt pork
- 3 cups milk
- 3 tablespoons butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Crackers

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from the fish and cut in pieces 1 inch thick.
2. Fry onion in butter and strain.
3. Put the butter back in the frying pan to fry the fish in.
4. Cut the pork in cubes, roll in flour, and fry in a little butter taking care not to brown the pork.
5. Drain the pork.
6. Put the boiled potatoes in the drippings and fry for a while.
7. Place layers of potato, fish and pork alternately into a kettle.
8. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
9. Add a little fish stock into the kettle.
10. Cook for 10 minutes.
11. Add heated milk, some butter, chopped parsley and crackers soaked in milk.
12. Boil quickly.
13. Put the chowder on a serving dish, cover with soaked crackers, and pour the sauce over.
14. Instead of milk, stewed strained tomatoes may be used.

title: Kalkkiinapaisti (Roast Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey
- ¼ pound bacon
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 3 tablespoons butter
- water
- flour
- cream

## Directions

1. Clean the turkey, rub with salt inside and out.
2. Line with slices of bacon and place in a buttered pan.
3. Put in a hot oven.
4. When brown sprinkle with salt, add hot water to the drippings.
5. Baste often.
6. If the oven is too hot, cover the turkey with a buttered paper.
7. Allow 2 or 3 hours for roasting.
8. Make a gravy of the drippings by adding some flour and cream.
9. Skim off the fat carefully.

title: Kalkkuna-paisti (Roast Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey
- 3/4 lb. of pork
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- water

## Directions

1. Clean the turkey, rub with salt inside and out.
2. Line with thin slices of pork and place in a buttered pan.
3. Roast in a hot oven.
4. When browned, sprinkle with salt, and pour hot water in the pan.
5. Baste often.
6. If the oven is too hot, cover the turkey with buttered paper.
7. Allow 2-3 hours for roasting.
8. Thicken the gravy with a little flour and cream.
9. Skim off the fat carefully.

title: Kampela kyljykset pinaatin kanssa (Fillets of Sole with Spinach)
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Thick milk sauce
- Salt
- Pepper
- Sole fillets
- Fish stock or water
- Butter
- Grated cheese
- Bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Boil spinach, grind or force through a sieve, add some thick milk sauce, salt and pepper.
2. Butter a baking dish, place the spinach in the center of dish.
3. Raise the fillets of a sole, lay in a pan, sprinkle with salt and pepper, add 1/2 cup of fish stock or water and small dots of butter.
4. Bake covered with buttered paper for 10 minutes.
5. Arrange the fillets over the spinach, cover the whole with milk sauce mixed with grated cheese.
6. Sprinkle with bread crumbs, bake until brown.
7. Serve in the baking dish.

title: Kampela à la Marquery (Sole à la Marquery)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two soles
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon juice
- Fish stock or water
- Butter
- Normandy sauce
- Bread, toasted or fried tomato slices
- Parsley or truffle, chopped
- Crab tails
- Oysters

## Directions

1. Raise the fillets of two soles, skin with a sharp knife.
2. Cut nicely in pieces; if too thin, fold in two.
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, flavor with lemon juice.
4. Let stand for 10 minutes.
5. Lay the fillets in a pan, pour 1/2 cup of fish stock or water over, add some more lemon juice.
6. Bake in oven for 10-12 minutes covered with buttered paper.
7. Baste with Normandy sauce, lay on toast or fried slices of tomato, arrange on a warm platter.
8. Sprinkle with chopped parsley or truffle, garnish around with crab-tails, oysters, etc.

title: Kampela à la Marquery (Soles à la Marquery)
body:
## Ingredients

- two soles, skinned
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- fish stock
- sherry wine
- Normandi sauce
- toast or fried tomato slices
- truffle or parsley
- crayfish tails
- oysters

## Directions

1. Raise the fillets of two skinned soles
2. Cut nicely in pieces; if too thin fold double
3. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and lemon juice and let stand 10 minutes
4. Place the fish pieces in a pan
5. Pour 1/2 cupful of fish stock and 1/4 cupful of sherry wine over
6. Place in oven with buttered paper on top for 10-12 minutes
7. Coat the fish pieces with Normandi sauce
8. Arrange on toast or fried tomato slices on a warm serving dish
9. Sprinkle with finely chopped truffle or parsley
10. Garnish around with crayfish tails, oysters, etc.

title: Kana Fricasse (Chicken Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- salt
- celery
- onion
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- chicken stock
- 2 egg yolks
- cream
- lemon juice
- rice

## Directions

1. Clean and joint the chicken.
2. Put into a kettle, pour over it boiling water.
3. When boiled about 5 minutes, add salt, a little celery, onion and pepper to season.
4. Prepare a sauce of butter, flour and chicken stock, add yolks of two eggs and a little cream.
5. Arrange on a serving dish having the chicken upon a foundation or molded ring of rice.
6. Or place rice timbales around the chicken.
7. Flavor with a dash of lemon juice.

title: Kana maitosoosin kanssa (Creamed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled chicken
- 1 cup white sauce
- milk or chicken bouillon
- ½ cup chicken pieces
- egg yolk
- cream
- chopped mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut cold boiled chicken into nice pieces.
2. Mix with 1 cup of white sauce, made of milk or chicken bouillon.
3. Heat.
4. Add one yolk of egg and cream.
5. May be mixed with chopped mushrooms, if desired.

title: Kana Maitosoosin Kanssa (Creamed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold boiled chicken
- 1 cupful of white sauce
- chicken stock
- milk
- 1½ cupfuls of chopped chicken
- 1 egg yolk
- cream
- chopped mushrooms

## Directions

1. Cut cold boiled chicken into nice pieces.
2. Mix with 1 cupful of white sauce made of chicken stock and milk.
3. Add 1½ cupfuls of chopped chicken.
4. Heat.
5. At the last add yolk of 1 egg and a little cream.
6. May be mixed also with chopped mushrooms.

title: Kana Palleroita (Chicken Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cupfuls of boiled fowl
- ½ cupful of milk sauce
- ¾ teaspoonful of salt
- lemon juice
- ½ teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- pepper
- egg
- bread crumbs
- lard
- chopped mushrooms

## Directions

1. Grind 1½ cupful of boiled fowl, add ½ cupful of milk sauce, ¾ teaspoonful of salt, a few drops of lemon juice, ½ teaspoonful of chopped parsley and a dash of pepper.
2. Let cool.
3. Form into croquettes, dip in egg and bread crumbs, cook in lard.
4. Chopped mushrooms mixed with the croquettes make them taste better.

title: Kana à la Barlton (Chicken à la Barlton)
body:
## Ingredients

- Breast meats and legs of chicken
- Butter
- Bread for triangular pieces of toast
- Small potatoes
- Green pepper
- Tomato
- Cream

## Directions

1. Fry breast meats and legs in butter covered, taking care so as not to brown the meat.
2. Lay on a platter.
3. Garnish around with triangular pieces of toast and small potatoes.
4. Make a gravy of the drippings by adding green pepper cut in strips and tomato cooked with a lump of butter.
5. Last of all add some cream to the gravy.

title: Kana ä la Neva (Chicken à la Neva)
body:
## Ingredients

- chickens
- vegetable or chicken salad
- goose liver paste or chopped sweetbreads
- white chaudfroid sauce
- lettuce leaves
- chicken jelly

## Directions

1. Boil the chickens whole.
2. Remove carefully the fillets, breastbone and the legs.
3. Stuff with vegetable or chicken salad.
4. Fill the breast space with goose liver paste or chopped sweetbreads.
5. Baste the fillets with white chaudfroid sauce.
6. Arrange nicely on top and around the chicken on the platter.
7. Garnish with lettuce leaves and chicken jelly.

title: Kana-fricassee (Chicken Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- cold water
- salt
- celery
- onion
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- chicken bouillon
- egg yolks
- cream
- rice
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean and joint the chicken. Place on the fire in cold water.
2. Bring quickly to boiling. When boiled about 5 minutes, add salt, some celery, onion and pepper.
3. Cook until tender.
4. Make a gravy of butter, flour and bouillon, add 2 yolks of egg and some cream.
5. Heat up shaking over the fire.
6. Arrange on a platter upon a foundation of molded rice or place the chicken in a ring of rice.
7. Rice timbales may be placed around the chicken.
8. Flavor with a dash of lemon juice, according to taste.

title: Kana-fricassee (Chicken Fricassee)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Water
- Salt
- Celery
- Onion
- Pepper
- Butter
- Flour
- Egg yolks
- Cream

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the chicken in pieces.
2. Place in cold water and bring quickly to a boil.
3. When chicken has boiled for about five minutes, add salt, celery, onion and pepper for seasoning.
4. Cook until chicken is tender.
5. Make sauce of butter, flour and chicken broth.
6. Add a few egg yolks and some cream.
7. Heat over fire until ready to serve.

title: Kana-muussi (Chicken Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound chicken breast
- 2 egg whites
- 1 1/2 cups cream
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Remove all fiber from chicken, pound well.
2. Add egg whites gradually and continue to pound.
3. Force through a sieve.
4. Add cream slowly.
5. Bake in oven in a pan of water covered with buttered paper, or use as stuffing for different kinds of vegetables.

title: Kana-Timboli (Chicken Timbale)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken breast fillets
- 2 scant tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 egg whites
- 1 cup whipped cream
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Remove the fillets from chicken, discard skin and fat
2. Remove fiber with a knife and pound well in a mortar
3. Add gradually the butter and egg whites
4. Rub through a sieve
5. Add slowly the whipped cream to the paste
6. Season with salt and pepper
7. Put into buttered timbale molds
8. Bake in oven in a pan of water covered with buttered paper for 12 to 15 minutes

title: Kanaconsomme (Chicken Consommé)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-2 chickens
- 4 quarts of cold water
- 1/2 root or 2 stalks of celery
- 1 leek or 1/2 onion
- 2 sprigs of parsley
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 6 peppercorns

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken into pieces and place it into the soupkettle in cold water.
2. Let it slowly come to boiling.
3. Add vegetables and seasonings and let simmer slowly for 4 hours.
4. Strain to cool and clarify.
5. Serve in cups for lunch.

title: Kanakeitto (Chicken Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Remains of chicken
- Water enough to cover them
- 1 onion
- 2 stalks celery and a small carrot
- 1 tablespoon salt, pepper
- 2 sprigs parsley

## Directions

1. Put the remains of chicken with the bones into a kettle, add the vegetables.
2. Cover with boiling water.
3. Let simmer for a few hours.
4. Thicken with butter and flour, season with salt and pepper.
5. Flavor with lemon.
6. Strain and serve with pieces of meat and croutons.
7. The remains of duck and turkey may be used for soup same as given above.

title: Kanamuhennos (Chicken Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- Onion
- Celery stalk
- Flour
- Butter or lard

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken in pieces, place on the fire in cold water.
2. Or brown the pieces in butter or lard, add boiling water to the drippings.
3. When coming to boiling add salt, pepper, 1 tablespoon of vinegar, onion and a stalk of celery.
4. Cook until tender.
5. Remove the onion and the celery from the drippings, thicken with flour.

title: Kananlientä (Chicken Consomme)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pound chicken
- 4 quarts water
- 2 stalks celery or 1 1/2 cup celery root
- 1 onion or leek
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 6 peppercorns

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken in pieces, put on the fire in cold water
2. When coming to boil, skim and let simmer for 2 hours
3. Add the vegetables and the spices, continue to boil for 1 hour
4. Strain, let cool
5. The breast and the leg meat may be removed from the kettle when done and used for croquettes, salad, etc.
6. If old chickens are used, the soup will be stronger, but the chicken has to cook longer

title: Kananliha-voileipiä (Chicken Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold chicken
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- mayonnaise
- chicken gravy or cream
- green pepper (optional)
- buttered bread slices

## Directions

1. Force cold chicken through the meat chopper.
2. Season with salt, pepper and lemon juice.
3. Soften with mayonnaise, chicken gravy or cream.
4. Mix with chopped green pepper, if desired.
5. Use for double sandwiches.

title: Kananmaksa-timboli (Chicken Liver Timbale)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cooked chopped chicken liver
- 2 tablespoons bread crumbs soaked in milk or cream
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3 egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- onion juice
- 3 egg whites
- butter for molds

## Directions

1. Have 1 cup of cooked chopped chicken liver.
2. Add 2 tablespoons bread crumbs soaked in milk or cream, 1 tablespoon butter, 3 yolks of egg, salt, pepper and some onion juice.
3. Last of all mix with 3 whites of egg beaten firm.
4. Put into buttered molds.
5. Bake in oven in a pan of water covered with buttered paper for 15 minutes or until firm.

title: Kananmaksa-voileipiä (Chicken Liver Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled chicken livers
- mushrooms
- butter
- onion juice
- salt
- pepper
- lemon juice
- toasted bread slices
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Chop boiled chicken livers and mushrooms.
2. Heat in a pan with a lump of butter.
3. Season with onion juice, salt, pepper and lemon juice.
4. Spread on toasted bread slices.
5. Sprinkle with hard boiled chopped egg on top.

title: Kananmaksaa muusin kanssa (Chicken Liver with Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken livers
- butter
- tomato slices
- salt
- pepper
- chicken mousse
- truffle sauce

## Directions

1. Fry the livers with butter.
2. Place on slices of tomato sprinkled with salt and pepper.
3. Cover with chicken mousse.
4. Bake in oven covered with buttered paper for 10-12 minutes.
5. Serve with truffle sauce.

title: Kananmaksaa omenien kanssa (Chicken Liver with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- butter
- sugar
- chicken livers
- salt
- pepper
- bouillon
- bread for toast points
- fruit sauce

## Directions

1. Pare apples and cut lengthwise in six parts.
2. Cook in butter and sugar until tender.
3. Fry chicken livers in butter, sprinkle with salt and pepper.
4. Add some bouillon and let come to boil.
5. Arrange the apples on a serving dish and place the livers on top.
6. Line the dish around with toast points.
7. Serve with fruit sauce.

title: Kananmaksasalaatti (Chicken Liver Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup fried chicken liver
- 4 hard boiled eggs
- 2-3 pickled onions
- French dressing

## Directions

1. Cut fried chicken liver in pieces.
2. Mix with 4 hard boiled eggs cut in pieces and 2 or 3 pickled onions.
3. Pour French dressing over.

title: Kanapalleroita (Chicken Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cups boiled fowl
- ½ cup rich milk sauce
- ⅜ teaspoon salt
- lemon juice
- ½ teaspoon chopped parsley
- pepper
- egg
- bread crumbs
- lard
- chopped mushrooms

## Directions

1. Grind 1½ cups of boiled fowl, add ½ cup of rich milk sauce, ⅜ teaspoon of salt, a few drops of lemon juice, ½ teaspoon of chopped parsley and some pepper.
2. Let cool.
3. Form into croquettes, dip in egg and bread crumbs.
4. Cook in lard.
5. If chopped mushrooms are added to the croquettes, they will be delicious.

title: Kanapiirakka (Chicken Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- pieces of chicken or remains of chicken
- butter
- 1 onion
- 1 carrot
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1½ cups water or chicken stock
- 1 tablespoon tomato
- pie crust
- 1 egg
- vegetables (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil pieces of chicken or use remains of chicken.
2. Prepare a brown gravy by frying butter with onion and carrot.
3. Add 1 tablespoon of flour, 1½ cups of water or chicken stock and tomato; boil quickly.
4. Lay the pieces of chicken into a mold and strain the gravy over.
5. Cover with pie crust, spread with egg, and perforate with fork.
6. Bake in oven until the crust is brown.
7. Vegetables may be used in the pie if desired.

title: Kanasalaatti (Chicken Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled or roast chicken
- celery
- salt
- pepper
- mayonnaise
- lettuce
- hard boiled eggs
- pickles
- capers
- olives

## Directions

1. Cut boiled or roast chicken in small pieces.
2. Add half as much celery cut in pieces.
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
4. Mix with mayonnaise.
5. Place into a salad bowl on a bed of lettuce.
6. Cover with mayonnaise.
7. Garnish with hard boiled egg, pickles, caper and chopped olives.

title: Kaneelileipä (Cinnamon Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1/2 cupful of sugar
- 4 cupfuls of flour
- 2 cupfuls of lukewarm milk
- 1 cupful of melted butter
- 1 cake of yeast
- cinnamon
- chopped almonds
- egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as for tea-ring.
2. When risen until light, toss in a shallow baking pan.
3. Let rise again.
4. Spread the surface with egg, sprinkle with cinnamon, sugar and chopped almonds.
5. Bake in oven nearly 20 minutes.
6. The dough may be mixed with almonds and spices, if preferred.
7. Shape with knife.

title: Kaneelileipä (Cinnamon Coffee Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 4 cups flour
- 2 cups lukewarm milk
- 1 cup melted butter
- 1 yeast cake
- Cinnamon
- Almonds

## Directions

1. Prepare the dough as given for coffee bread.
2. When risen until light, toss the dough into a shallow baking tin.
3. Let rise some more.
4. Brush the top with egg, sprinkle with cinnamon, sugar and almonds.
5. Bake for 20 or 30 minutes.
6. The dough may be mixed with almonds and spices, according to taste.

title: Kanelirinkilöitä (Cinnamon Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 1 cupful of cream
- 6 ozs. of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of baking powder
- wheat flour as much as needed to make a dough that can be kneaded

## Directions

1. Whip eggs and sugar to a froth
2. Add cream and butter worked to a froth
3. Add baking powder mixed with flour
4. Finally add as much flour as needed so the dough can be kneaded

title: Kanelirinkilöitä (Cinnamon Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 1 cupful of cream
- 6 ozs. of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of baking powder
- flour enough to make a dough easy to handle

## Directions

1. Whip eggs and sugar to a froth
2. Add cream and creamed butter
3. Mix baking powder with flour and add to mixture
4. Add flour enough to make the dough thick enough to handle
5. Form into small rings
6. Dip them in sugar mixed with cinnamon
7. Bake until lightly browned

title: Kannatettu ananas (Canned Pineapple)
body:
## Ingredients

- pineapples
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Pare and core pineapples and remove the black eyes.
2. Cut in slices.
3. Let stand in sugar over night (allow 3 or 4 cups pineapple to 1 cup sugar).
4. Add 1 cup water.
5. Bring slowly to boil.
6. When tender, put into jars.
7. Cover as given for jams.

title: Kannu maissia (Canned Corn)
body:
## Ingredients

- canned corn
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Take canned corn, add a little butter, salt, pepper and a little cream or milk.
2. Heat through and serve.

title: Kannutettu Raparperi (Canned Rhubarb)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups rhubarb
- water

## Directions

1. Wash rhubarb and cut in pieces without peeling if young.
2. Put rhubarb and sugar into a bowl in layers and let stand over night.
3. Reduce the juice by boiling to a thick syrup.
4. Add the rhubarb and simmer until clear.
5. Pour into jars while hot and cover as given for jams.

title: Kannutettu raparperi (Canned Rhubarb)
body:
## Ingredients

- rhubarb
- sugar

## Directions

1. Rinse rhubarb when young and cut in pieces without peeling.
2. Take 1 cup sugar to 2 cups rhubarb.
3. Lay in a kettle in layers and let stand until the next day.
4. Bring the juice to a boil and cook down until thick.
5. Add the rhubarb pieces and cook slowly until they appear clear.
6. Pour into jars while hot and seal.

title: Kannutettuja Mansikoita (Canned Strawberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- strawberries
- 1/2 to 3/4 sugar to weight of strawberries

## Directions

1. Clean strawberries and weigh them.
2. Put the berries with the sugar in layers into a kettle and let stand overnight.
3. Place on low heat, skim carefully, stir slowly or shake the kettle often.
4. Continue to cook until clear and the skum ceases to rise to the surface.
5. Fill the jars immediately and cover airtight with rubber rings and covers.

title: Kannutettuja Omenia, Päärynöitä, Persikoita ja Luumuja (Canned Apples, Pears, Peaches and Plums)
body:
## Ingredients

- apples, pears, peaches or plums
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups water

## Directions

1. Cut the fruit lengthwise in two, pare and core.
2. Pack tight in glass jars having airtight covers.
3. Cook 1 cup sugar in 2 cups water for 10 minutes and pour over fruit while hot.
4. Screw covers on as tight as you can get them with the thumb and the little finger.
5. Place the jars on a foundation in a kettle.
6. Pour lukewarm water into the kettle up to 2/3 of the height of the jars.
7. Steam slowly with the kettle covered.
8. Remove the covers of the jars one at a time and fill the jars with hot syrup made of sugar and water.
9. Replace the covers and let cool.
10. Tighten the covers until airtight.
11. The apples may be flavored with vanilla and cinnamon.

title: Kannutettuja sieniä (Canned Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- rich milk or mushroom liquor and milk
- mushrooms
- onion juice
- parsley
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Prepare a sauce of butter, flour and rich milk or of liquor of the mushrooms and milk equal parts.
2. Cut the mushrooms in pieces, add to the sauce.
3. Heat slowly to boiling.
4. May be mixed with onion juice, if desired.
5. Serve sprinkled with parsley.
6. Or put the mushrooms with the sauce into a mold, sprinkle the top with bread crumbs.
7. Heat in oven in a pan of water so as not to curdle.

title: Kapinetti-putinki (Cabinet Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- Almonds
- Sugar
- Prunes
- Sponge cake
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 3/4 lemon
- 1/2 cup prune juice
- Hard sauce

## Directions

1. Blanch and chop almonds and mix with sugar.
2. Cook prunes, split and fill the halves with almonds.
3. Line a mold with filled prunes.
4. Fill with sponge cake cut in cubes.
5. Cover with the remaining prunes crushed.
6. Prepare a mixture by whipping 2 eggs with 2 tablespoons of sugar, 3/4 lemon and 1/2 cup of prune juice.
7. Cover the top with this mixture.
8. Bake in oven in a pan of water.
9. Serve with hard sauce.

title: Kapris Kastike (Caper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 pint of beef or fish stock
- 1 - 2 tablespoonfuls of caper
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon juice
- a little salt, pepper and sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare a sauce of butter, flour and stock
2. Season with pepper, salt, sugar, lemon juice and caper
3. May be mixed also with two yolks and lastly with a little cream
4. Serve with boiled fish and meat

title: Kapris-kastike (Caper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 pint bouillon or fish stock
- 1 or 2 tablespoons caper
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- salt, pepper and sugar
- 1 egg, finely chopped
- 2 egg yolks (optional)
- cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Prepare a gravy of butter, flour and bouillon.
2. Add finely chopped egg, salt, pepper, sugar, lemon juice and caper.
3. Season to taste.
4. 2 yolks of egg and some cream may be added to the sauce at the last.
5. Serve with mutton or fish.

title: Karamelli kastike (Caramel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1 cupful of water
- a little wine or vanilla

## Directions

1. Put 1 cupful of sugar in a saucepan
2. Stir until it is browned
3. Add 1 cupful of water
4. Let boil 5 minutes
5. When cool, add a little wine or vanilla

title: Karamelli munahyydyke (Caramel Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of water
- 1 pint of milk
- 3 yolks of eggs
- 2 whole eggs
- 1/2 teaspoonful of vanilla
- 1/4 cupful of sugar
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Melt 1 cupful of sugar in a pan, stirring well, to a syrup of light brown color
2. Add 1 tablespoonful of water
3. Pour a part of syrup in a buttered mold, covering the bottom and the sides
4. Add equal part of water to the remaining syrup and set aside
5. Beat the eggs, add sugar, salt, vanilla and scalded milk
6. Pour the mixture into the mold on the hardened syrup
7. Steam in a slow oven in a water bath until firm
8. Serve cold, unmolded, with caramel sauce

title: Karamelli-Kermajäätelö (Caramel Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup water

## Directions

1. Prepare as given for vanilla ice cream.
2. Instead of sugar use caramel made by browning 1 cup of sugar in a pan and 1/2 cup of water added to it.
3. Stir until the sugar is dissolved.
4. The sugar must not be burnt, only delicately browned.

title: Karamelli-munahyydyke (Caramel Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 tablespoon water
- 1 pint milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- Salt

## Directions

1. Brown 1 cup of sugar in a pan stirring constantly
2. Add 1 tablespoon of water
3. Pour a part of the syrup into a buttered mold
4. Turn the mold so as to line the edges and bottom with the syrup
5. Add to the remaining syrup equal part of water to make a sauce
6. Prepare custard: beat eggs with sugar, salt and vanilla
7. Pour the custard into the caramel-lined mold
8. Bake in oven in a pan of water until firm
9. Unmold when cold and serve with the caramel sauce
10. Alternatively, the caramel may be dissolved in milk by stirring over the fire

title: Karamellikastike (Caramel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup water
- wine or vanilla

## Directions

1. Stir in a pan 1 cup of sugar until brown
2. Add 1 cup water, cook for 5 minutes
3. When cool, add some wine or vanilla

title: Karitsaa (Spring Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- Spring lamb
- Water
- Mint sauce
- Butter
- Salt
- Lemon juice
- Stuffing

## Directions

1. Roast lamb in a hot oven with a little water in the pan.
2. Allow 10-18 minutes to a pound.
3. Serve with mint sauce.
4. When using the forequarter, have the bones well cracked or removed.
5. Rub the space with butter, salt and lemon juice.
6. Stuff.

title: Karitsaa (Spring Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- spring lamb
- butter
- salt
- lemon juice
- mint sauce

## Directions

1. Roast in a hot oven, having a little water in dripping-pan
2. Allow 15-18 minutes to a pound
3. When using the fore quarter have the bones well cracked or removed
4. Rub the space with butter, salt and lemon juice
5. Stuff
6. Serve with mint sauce

title: Karpalo Kastike (Cranberry Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of cranberries
- 2 cupfuls of sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Rinse the cranberries
2. Place on fire to boil with 2 cupfuls of sugar and water so that it reaches the level of the berries
3. Cook on a slow fire until the berries break
4. Put in a mold to set either strained or unstrained

title: Karpalo pai (Cranberry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- cranberries
- sugar
- egg
- pastry strips

## Directions

1. Cook cranberries with sugar
2. Let cool down
3. Place on an open pie
4. Lay strips across the top
5. Spread with egg
6. Bake in a moderate oven

title: Karpalohyytelö (Cranberry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups cranberries
- 1 cup water
- 2 cups sugar

## Directions

1. Wash the cranberries, put on the fire in 1 cup of water
2. Let boil for 20 minutes
3. Rub through a sieve
4. Add 2 cups sugar, continue to cook slowly for a few minutes
5. If boiled too fast, it will not jelly
6. Put into a wet mold to set

title: Karpalohyytelö (Cranberry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of cranberries
- ½ cupful of water
- 1 cupful of sugar

## Directions

1. Clean 2 cupfuls of cranberries, put on the fire, mixed with ½ cupful of water.
2. Let boil 20 minutes.
3. Force through a colander, add 1 cupful of sugar and cook a few minutes.
4. Pour into a mold to get firm.

title: Karpalokastike (Cranberry Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cranberries
- 2 cups sugar
- 2 cups water

## Directions

1. Rinse 1 quart of cranberries
2. Boil with 2 cups of sugar and 2 cups of water
3. Cook slowly to a smooth pulp
4. Put into a mold to set strained or unstrained
5. If strained, mix the sugar with the strained pulp and heat until the sugar is dissolved

title: Karpalopiirakka (Cranberry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- cranberries
- sugar
- pastry for one open crust and pastry strips

## Directions

1. Cook cranberries with sugar.
2. Let cool.
3. Cover a pie plate with pastry, fill with cranberries.
4. Lay strips cut of pastry across the top.
5. Brush with egg.
6. Bake in a moderate oven.

title: Karviaismarjahilloke (Gooseberry Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- gooseberries
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Take half ripe gooseberries, wash, pick.
2. Place over the fire in cold water.
3. When coming to boil, strain.
4. Let cool.
5. Put into boiling syrup.
6. Heat slowly.
7. The gooseberries do not crush and are easy to keep.

title: Kastanja Puree (Chestnut Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chestnuts
- Salt
- Cream or milk
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Shell the chestnuts
2. Cook in salted water and drain
3. Mash and dilute with cream or milk
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Stir until smooth

title: Kastanja-putinki (Chestnut Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cupfuls of mashed chestnuts
- 1 cupful of rich milk
- 3 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. Beat eggs with sugar
2. Add milk, flour and chestnuts
3. Bake in oven 30-40 minutes

title: Kastanja-pyree (Chestnut Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of white stock
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of boiled and stewed chestnuts
- 1 slice of onion
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- salt and pepper
- 1 cupful of cream

## Directions

1. Soak the chestnuts about 2 minutes in water to remove the shells.
2. Remove the shells while warm.
3. Cook in stock until soft and rub through a sieve.
4. Cook the onions in butter, add flour and chestnuts into it and stock as much as required.
5. Boil for 5 minutes; season, and at last add two tablespoonfuls of madeira to flavor it.

title: Kastanja-täyte (Chestnut Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of chestnuts
- water or meat stock
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- a dash of pepper
- 1 teaspoonful of parsley
- meat stock if needed

## Directions

1. Soak chestnuts in boiling water to remove the shells
2. Drain from the water and remove the shells
3. Cook the shelled chestnuts in water or meat stock until soft
4. Press through a sieve
5. Add 1 tablespoonful of butter, 1 teaspoonful of salt, a dash of pepper, and 1 teaspoonful of parsley
6. Dilute with meat stock if necessary

title: Kastanjakeitto (Chestnut Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts white stock
- 1½ cups boiled and chopped chestnuts
- 1 slice onion
- salt and pepper
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 cup cream
- Madeira or tomato

## Directions

1. Parboil the chestnuts so as to remove the shells easily.
2. The shells must be removed while the chestnuts are hot.
3. Cook until tender in meat stock, press through a sieve.
4. Cook the onion in butter, add flour, chestnuts and dilute with meat stock.
5. Cook for 5 minutes longer.
6. Season, add two spoonfuls of Madeira or tomato.
7. May be made by using vegetable stock or water instead of meat stock.

title: Kastanjakohokas (Chestnut Souffle)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 cup mashed chestnuts
- 1/2 cup milk
- 4 egg whites
- 2 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Mix together gently the sugar, the flour, the chestnuts and the milk
2. Cook for a few minutes
3. Beat the whites until firm, add to the mixture
4. Bake in a well-buttered mold in a pan of water until firm or about 30 minutes
5. Serve in the mold with vanilla or cream sauce

title: Kastanjaomeletti (Chestnut Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound chestnuts
- 3 eggs
- 3 tablespoons water or milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- cream
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Shell the chestnuts, chop and boil in a small quantity of water until soft.
2. Stir 1 tablespoon of butter with 1 tablespoon of flour in a saucepan.
3. Put the chestnuts in, stirring briskly, let boil quickly.
4. Add some cream and season.
5. Make a French omelet.
6. Pour the mixture on one half and turn the other half over it.

title: Kastanjaomeletti (Chestnut Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ pound chestnuts
- 3 eggs
- 3 tablespoons water or milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Cream

## Directions

1. Peel the chestnuts, chop fine and boil in a small amount of water until soft.
2. Mix 1 tablespoon of butter and 1 tablespoon of flour in a pan.
3. Add the chestnuts, mix well and let simmer.
4. Add a little cream and season.
5. Prepare a French omelet, pour the mixture over one half and fold the other half over.

title: Kastanjaputinki (Chestnut Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cups mashed chestnuts
- 1 cup rich milk
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar
2. Add milk, flour and chestnuts
3. Bake for 30 or 40 minutes

title: Kastanjaputinki - Second Method (Chestnut Pudding - Second Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mashed chestnuts
- ½ cup rice, boiled with milk
- Butter
- Salt
- Sugar or raisins

## Directions

1. Mix the mashed chestnuts with ½ cup rice boiled with milk
2. Add some butter, salt and sugar, or raisins instead of sugar
3. Serve as a substitute for vegetables or as a dessert with fruit sauce

title: Kastanjat (Chestnuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chestnuts
- Salt

## Directions

1. Cut with a knife across or make a little hole in the top of each chestnut
2. Put the chestnuts in a sauce pan with salt and cook without water on high heat, stirring briskly
3. After 30 minutes cooking they are easy to shell
4. Serve in the shells on a folded napkin with cold butter
5. Alternatively, bake chestnuts in oven covered with a thick layer of salt on top; in a moderate oven they will be done in 30 minutes
6. Or boil the chestnuts in salted water; when boiled 20 minutes the shells will be easy to remove

title: Kastanjatäyte (Chestnut Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart chestnuts
- Water or bouillon
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon parsley
- 1 egg
- Bouillon

## Directions

1. Place a quart of chestnuts in boiling water until the skins come off, sieve and peel.
2. Place the peeled chestnuts in water or bouillon and cook until soft.
3. Press through a sieve, add 2 tablespoons butter, 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon parsley, 1 egg, and some bouillon if needed.

title: Kastanjatäyte (Chestnut Stuffing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart chestnuts
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon parsley
- 1 egg
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Have a quart of chestnuts in boiling water so as to remove the shells.
2. Drain and shell.
3. Place the shelled chestnuts in water or bouillon and cook until tender.
4. Rub through a sieve.
5. Add 2 tablespoons of butter, 1 teaspoon of salt, 1 teaspoon of parsley, 1 egg, and some bouillon if necessary.
6. For stuffing turkey, add bread crumbs or mashed sweet potatoes to the stuffing.

title: Kastanjoita kerman kanssa (Chestnut Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chestnuts
- Sugar
- Vanilla
- Salt
- Cream

## Directions

1. Boil chestnuts, peel and rub through a sieve.
2. Season with sugar, vanilla and a little salt, soften with cream.

title: Kastanjoita Kerman Kanssa (Chestnut Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- chestnuts
- sugar
- vanilla
- salt
- cream
- whipped cream
- preserved apples

## Directions

1. Cook chestnuts, shell and rub through a sieve
2. Add sugar, vanilla and salt, dilute with cream
3. Heat the mixture
4. Arrange on a platter in form of a ring or a pyramid
5. Let cool
6. Serve garnished with whipped cream and preserved apples

title: Kastike kylmälle kalalle (Sauce for Cold Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 cups fish stock
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 lemon, juice of
- Salt
- Pepper
- Parsley, chopped finely
- Mayonnaise (optional)
- Olives
- Pickles, finely chopped
- Capers
- Hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Boil 1.5 cups of fish stock.
2. Thicken with 1 tablespoon of cornstarch.
3. Beat 3 yolks of egg, add 1 tablespoon sugar and juice of 1 lemon.
4. Pour gradually mixing well into the fish stock.
5. Heat, taking care not to boil it.
6. Season with salt, pepper and chopped parsley.
7. Pour over the fish.
8. When cold the sauce may be mixed with mayonnaise according to taste.

title: Kasvis-paisti (Vegetable Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of nuts
- 1 cupful of peas
- 1 cupful of boiled potatoes
- 3/4 lb. of butter
- 2 cupfuls of bread crumbs
- 2 cupfuls of cream
- 4 eggs
- salt
- 1 stalk of celery
- 1 onion

## Directions

1. Grind boiled vegetables and nuts in meat mill
2. Add bread crumbs soaked in cream
3. Add beaten eggs and seasonings
4. Rub briskly
5. Form into a loaf and bake in oven, or form into croquettes or fillets
6. Dip formed pieces in egg and roll in bread crumbs
7. Fry in butter
8. Prepare a sauce from the drippings in the pan
9. Serve with vegetables

title: Kasvis-palleroita (Vegetable Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- kaikenlaisia keitettyjä kasviksia
- munaa
- leipäjauhoa
- vähän vehnäjauhoja
- suolaa

## Directions

1. Kasvikset jauhetaan lihamyllyssä.
