2. Lisätään munaa, leipäjauhoa ja vähän vehnäjauhoja, suolaa mausteeksi.
3. Muodostetaan palleroiksi, kastellaan munassa ja leipäjauliossa.
4. Paistetaan voissa tai keitetään rasvassa.
5. Tarjotaan ruskean kastin ja vihannesten kera.

title: Kasviskeittoa maidon kanssa (Vegetable Soup with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carrot
- 1 celery
- 1/4 cauliflower
- 3/4 pound spinach
- 1 potato
- 3/4 cup peas
- 3 quarts milk
- Chopped parsley
- Butter
- Flour

## Directions

1. Cut the vegetables in cubes, boil with butter in a small quantity of water.
2. Cook 2 tablespoonfuls of butter with 2 tablespoonfuls of flour in a saucepan, add the scalded milk and the vegetables.
3. Season, bring to boil.

title: Kasvismureke (Vegetable Farce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups boiled and chopped vegetables
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 1 cup beans
- 2 tablespoons cornstarch
- 2 onions
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 cup cream or tomato juice
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Force through a meat grinder the boiled vegetables such as carrots, potatoes, celery, parsnips, or whatever is available.
2. Brown the chopped onions in butter and add to the vegetables.
3. Add the bread crumbs soaked in cream and mixed with cornstarch and the eggs.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Place into a buttered pan and shape into a loaf.
6. Brush with egg and bake for 1 hour.
7. Serve with brown sauce.
8. May be baked in a mold and served unmoulded.

title: Kasvismureke (Vegetable Farcie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of cooked vegetables
- 2 cupfuls of bread crumbs
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of corn starch
- 3/4 lb. of butter
- 2 onions
- 3/4 cupful of cream

## Directions

1. Grind vegetables in meat mill
2. Brown chopped onions in butter
3. Add browned onions to ground vegetables
4. Soak bread crumbs in cream and mix with beaten eggs
5. Combine with vegetable mixture
6. Season with salt and pepper
7. Turn into a buttered mold and form into a loaf
8. Spread with egg
9. Bake in oven 1 hour
10. Serve with potatoes and brown vegetable sauce

title: Kasvispaisti (Vegetable Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup walnuts
- 1 cup peas
- 1 cup boiled potatoes
- 4 tomatoes
- 1/4 pound butter
- 2 cups bread crumbs
- 4 eggs
- 1 onion
- 1 stalk celery
- salt
- cream

## Directions

1. Boil the potatoes, peel the tomatoes, shell the nuts and clean the celery.
2. Force all the vegetables through a meat grinder.
3. Add the bread crumbs, beaten eggs and seasonings.
4. If the mixture appears too thin, add cornstarch; if too thick, dilute with cream.
5. Form into small cakes or bake in a mold.
6. May be formed into balls, croquettes, etc. and fried in butter or lard.

title: Kasvispaistosta (Vegetable Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- porkkanoita
- herneitä
- perunoita
- selleriä
- kukkakaalia
- nauriita
- voipalasia
- tomaattiviipaleita
- suolaa
- pippuria
- leipäjauhoa

## Directions

1. Kasvikset palotellaan kuutioiksi, keitetään suolatussa vedessä puolipehmeäksi.
2. Siilataan ja asetetaan vormuun pieniä voipalasia päälle.
3. Päällystä tomaattiviipaleilla, ripota suolaa ja pippuria päälle.
4. Peitä astia pais kuorella, tai pane tomaattien päälle leipäjauhoa.
5. Paista uunissa 1 tunti.
6. Tarjotaan joko voisulan tai jonkun kastin kanssa.

title: Kasvispaistosta (Vegetable Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- potatoes
- peas
- cauliflower
- celery
- turnips
- salted water
- butter
- tomato slices
- salt
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- pie-crust (optional)

## Directions

1. Use carrots, potatoes, peas, cauliflower, celery, turnips or whatever you may have.
2. Boil the vegetables in salted water.
3. Drain and place into a mold.
4. Add dots of butter.
5. Cover the top with slices of tomato.
6. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and bread crumbs.
7. May be covered with pie-crust leaving off the bread crumbs.
8. Bake for 1 hour.
9. Serve with melted butter or sauce.

title: Kasvispalleroita (Vegetable Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked vegetables of all kinds
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Grind the vegetables
2. Add eggs, bread crumbs, flour and season with salt
3. Form into croquettes
4. Dip in egg and turn in flour
5. Fry in butter or deep fat
6. Serve with brown sauce and vegetables

title: Kaurakakkuja No. 1 (Oatmeal Cookies No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup oatmeal
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup milk
- 3/4 cup butter
- 1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- Cinnamon and raisins

## Directions

1. Sift together flour, salt and baking powder, work butter in the flour.
2. Beat eggs with sugar, add milk and pour the mixture gradually into the flour.
3. Roll the dough thin, cut in round small cookies.
4. Lay on a buttered baking sheet, bake in a hot oven until light brown.
5. The raisins may be left out, if desired.

title: Kaurakakkuja No. 2 (Oatmeal Cookies No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups rolled oats
- 3/4 cup brown sugar
- 3/4 cup butter
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat separately whites and yolks.
2. Rub butter with sugar until creamy, add salt, vanilla, rolled oats and baking powder; beat well.
3. Fold in the yolks and last of all the whites.
4. Drop by 1/2 teaspoon on a buttered baking sheet leaving room for the cookies to rise.
5. Bake in a moderate oven.

title: Kauraleipää (Oatmeal Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups oatmeal
- 1 quart boiling water
- 1/4 cup molasses
- 2 teaspoons salt
- 2 yeast cakes
- 2 quarts flour

## Directions

1. Bring the water to boil and add the oatmeal
2. When cool, add salt, molasses and the risen sponge of yeast
3. Work in white flour enough to make a dough
4. Keep in a warm place until risen
5. Knead on the table and put into tins to rise
6. Bake for an hour in a hot oven

title: Kauraryyni Muffensit (Oatmeal Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of flour
- 1 cupful of cooked oatmeal
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- ¾ cupful of milk
- 2 eggs
- 3 teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- ½ teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Mix salt, sugar and baking powder with flour
2. Mix milk with the oatmeal
3. Beat eggs until light
4. Add the oatmeal and milk to the eggs
5. Pour this gradually into the flour mixture
6. Mix the batter well
7. Put in buttered molds
8. Bake in a moderately hot oven

title: Kauraryyni-muffins (Oatmeal Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- 1 cup boiled oatmeal
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- ¾ cup milk
- 2 eggs
- ½ teaspoon salt
- 3 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Sift salt, sugar and baking powder with flour.
2. Mix the oatmeal with the milk, beat the eggs until frothy and add to the oatmeal.
3. Add gradually all this to the flour stirring well.
4. Bake in buttered muffin tins in a moderate oven.

title: Kauravohveleita (Oatmeal Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cooked oatmeal
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1 cup milk
- 2 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- Some salt

## Directions

1. Sift together all the dry ingredients
2. Add the milk with beaten eggs
3. Combine with the oatmeal
4. Add the flour
5. Fry in hot greased waffle-iron until brown on both sides
6. Rice waffles may be made in the same way

title: Kauravohveli (Oatmeal Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of oatmeal porridge
- ½ cupful of flour
- 1 cupful of milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 teaspoonful of baking powder
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Beat eggs, add milk, porridge, flour mixed with baking powder and salt.
2. Fry in well buttered waffle iron until brown on both sides.

title: Kaviaarikäärö (Caviare Roll)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh bread
- caviare
- cream
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Cut fresh bread in thin slices.
2. Spread with caviare mixed with cream.
3. Roll together.
4. Lay caviare on the rolls.
5. Decorate with whipped cream and red pepper.

title: Kaviari à la Aspic (Caviare à la Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- small molds with hole in center
- red pepper
- lentils
- jellied caviare
- toast
- caviare
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Cover the bottom of small molds thinly with red pepper and lentils.
2. Fill with jellied caviare.
3. Spread caviare on toast and turn the molds on top of it.
4. Fill the holes with caviare.
5. Serve on lettuce leaves before soup.

title: Kaviari à la Charlotte (Caviare à la Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup whipped cream
- 2 tablespoons caviare
- 2 tablespoons melted gelatine
- red pepper
- sandwiches

## Directions

1. Mix the caviare with gelatine and dilute with cream.
2. Garnish small molds with red pepper.
3. Fill the molds alternately with layers of the mixture and plain caviare having the mixture on the bottom and on top.
4. Serve on sandwiches.

title: Kaviarilla Täytettyjä Munia (Eggs Filled With Caviare)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard boiled eggs
- caviare
- anchovy
- toast
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Cut a thin slice of the sides of hard boiled eggs.
2. Scoop out the yolks, filling the eggs with caviare.
3. Cut off a fine strip near both ends and place a slice of anchovy into the cuts.
4. Make a little hole between the strips and fill the hole with a piece of anchovy.
5. Place the eggs on toast with the sliced side down so as to make the egg look like a barrel.
6. Garnish the toast with mayonnaise.

title: Keitetty Artisokka (Boiled Artichoke)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large artichokes
- water
- salt
- 4 ozs. of butter
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Strip off outer leaves and remove stalks
2. Trim off tops of inner leaves
3. Rub the bottom with lemon
4. Immerse the artichokes with stalk ends uppermost in boiling water mixed with a little salt
5. Cook until tender
6. Take out quickly
7. Arrange on a serving dish with the top up
8. Serve with Hollandaise sauce or melted butter

title: Keitetty Brysselinkaali (Boiled Brussels Sprouts)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- vinegar
- water
- salt
- baking soda
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the sprouts
2. Soak for 1/2 hour in water mixed with vinegar
3. Put on the fire in boiling salt water with a teaspoonful of baking soda
4. Cook until tender
5. Remove immediately from the water
6. Mix with butter, salt and pepper

title: Keitetty Brysselinkaali (Boiled Brussels Sprouts)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 quarts of Brussels sprouts
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter

## Directions

1. Clean Brussels sprouts
2. Soak 1/2 hour in water mixed with vinegar
3. Put in slightly salted boiling water
4. Cook until tender
5. Remove quickly from the water
6. Place on a serving dish
7. Baste with melted butter

title: Keitetty Hedelmäputinki (Plum Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cups bread crumbs
- 1/2 cup flour
- 3/4 cup suet or 1/4 cup butter
- 1/2 cup currants
- 1/2 cup raisins
- Yellow rind of 2 and juice of 4 lemons
- 1 tablespoon chopped almonds
- 1 teaspoon nutmeg
- 2 leaves mace chopped
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- 1/2 cup sugar or syrup
- 1 cup milk or brandy
- 8 eggs or 1 teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Prepare as directed for fruit pudding

title: Keitetty kieli (Boiled Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tongue
- Hot salted water
- Peppercorns
- Vegetables
- Tomato or horseradish sauce
- Tartar sauce

## Directions

1. Rinse the tongue and scrape carefully.
2. Put on fire in boiling salt water with peppercorns and vegetables.
3. Boil until tender.
4. Skin immediately.
5. Serve with tomato or horseradish sauce, having the sauce on the platter and the sliced tongue on the top.
6. If served with tartar sauce have the sauce poured over the tongue.
7. Salted tongue has to be put on the fire in cold water and boiled a longer time.

title: Keitetty kukkakaali (Boiled Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt water
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Remove all the leaves around the head
2. Boil in salt water for 20 minutes
3. Drain when tender
4. Serve with melted butter

title: Keitetty Kurkku (Boiled Cucumber)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumbers
- salted water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- milk sauce
- cream

## Directions

1. Use ripe cucumbers.
2. Peel, cut lengthwise in big pieces.
3. Cook in salted water until tender.
4. Drain carefully.
5. Mix with butter, salt and pepper.
6. Or make a sauce of the liquor of the cucumbers.
7. Serve in a vegetable dish or on toast.
8. May be served in milk sauce.
9. Or mash the cucumbers, mix with butter, salt and pepper.
10. Add cream, if preferred.

title: Keitetty Kurkku (Boiled Cucumber)
body:
## Ingredients

- older cucumbers
- salt
- water
- cream sauce or minced parsley
- butter and pepper (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel cucumbers
2. Cut lengthwise into four parts and then across the middle
3. Boil in salted water until tender
4. Strain water carefully
5. Mix with cream sauce and minced parsley
6. Alternatively, mince the boiled cucumbers and season with salt, pepper and butter

title: Keitetty Lampaanpaisti (Boiled Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton
- water
- vegetables
- salt
- pepper
- caper sauce

## Directions

1. Wipe the meat and place in a pot.
2. Pour boiling water over it and let it come to a boil quickly.
3. Add vegetables, salt and pepper.
4. Let simmer slowly under cover until tender.
5. Serve with caper sauce and vegetables.

title: Keitetty lampaanpaisti (Boiled Mutton)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mutton
- Vegetables
- Salt
- Pepper
- Caper sauce

## Directions

1. Wipe the meat and place in a kettle.
2. Cover with boiling water.
3. Bring quickly to boiling.
4. Cook with vegetables, salt and pepper.
5. Let simmer slowly covered until tender.
6. Serve with caper sauce, placing the vegetables around the meat on the platter.

title: Keitetty lampaanpaisti (Boiled Mutton) - with gravy
body:
## Ingredients

- Roasted mutton
- Fat from drippings
- 3 tablespoons flour
- Boiling water
- 1 tablespoon tomato
- Mustard

## Directions

1. Remove some of the fat from the roasted meat.
2. Mix the drippings with 3 tablespoons of flour.
3. Add boiling water until the gravy becomes moderately thick.
4. Flavor with a tablespoon of tomato and some mustard.
5. Strain.

title: Keitetty laserrus (Boiled Icing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup water
- 1/2 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 1 egg white
- lemon juice or vanilla for flavoring

## Directions

1. Put 1 cup sugar, 3/4 cup water and 1/2 teaspoon cream of tartar in a saucepan over the fire
2. Cook until the syrup will thread when dropped from the tip of a spoon
3. Beat until frothy the white of an egg
4. Pour gradually the syrup in stirring well
5. Flavor with lemon juice or vanilla
6. By using 2 egg whites with 1 cup of sugar, the icing will be made thinner

title: Keitetty lehti-artisokka (Boiled Artichoke)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichoke
- lemon
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Remove the bottom leaves and stem of the artichoke and trim the tips of the upper leaves slightly.
2. Rub the bottom with a little lemon.
3. Place the artichoke stem side up in boiling water with a little salt.
4. Cook until tender.
5. Transfer immediately to a serving platter and serve with Hollandaise sauce or melted butter.

title: Keitetty Lehti-Artisokka (Boiled Artichoke)
body:
## Ingredients

- artichokes
- lemon
- salted water
- Hollandaise sauce or melted butter

## Directions

1. Strip off outer leaves, remove stalks and trim off tops of inner leaves.
2. Rub the bottom with lemon.
3. Immerse into boiling salted water, having the stalk ends uppermost.
4. Cook until tender.
5. Remove from the water quickly.
6. Serve with Hollandaise sauce or melted butter.
7. Artichokes may be served cold with salad using French dressing or tartare sauce.

title: Keitetty Maha (Boiled Tripe)
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe
- water
- 2 stalks of celery

## Directions

1. Soak the tripe for a few hours in water with two stalks of celery.
2. Put in boiling water on the fire and cook until tender.
3. Serve hot.

title: Keitetty Muna Hyydyke (Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/4 cupful of sugar
- 1/2 teaspoonful of vanilla
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Put milk on heat and let boil.
2. Beat egg yolks with sugar, pour boiling milk gradually into eggs, beating well.
3. Beat still over water until it thickens, flavor and let cool.
4. If desired, two stiffly beaten egg whites may be added just before serving.
5. Boiled custard may also be flavored with wine and almonds.

title: Keitetty munahyydyke (Boiled Custard)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint milk
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla
- Salt

## Directions

1. Heat the milk and bring to a boil
2. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar
3. Pour gradually the boiling milk into the yolks stirring well
4. Stir over the fire in a pan of water until thick
5. Season and let cool
6. Two egg-whites beaten until firm may be added shortly before serving if desired

title: Keitetty parsa (Boiled Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salt
- water
- baking soda
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Scrape asparagus and rinse in cold water.
2. Tie in bunches so that they will not break while boiling.
3. Put on the fire in boiling salted water.
4. Cook for 15 or 20 minutes.
5. When done, drain quickly and untie the bunches.
6. Arrange on a serving platter or on toast.
7. Serve with melted butter mixed with lemon juice or baste with melted butter and garnish with lemon.

title: Keitetty Parsa (Boiled Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 asparagus stalks
- 1 oz. of salt
- 1½ quarts of water
- 4 ozs. of butter
- lemon juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Scrape asparagus
2. Wash in cold water
3. Tie up in bunches
4. Put in boiling water
5. Cook 15–20 minutes
6. When done, take them out from water
7. Unbind the bundles
8. Place on a serving dish or on toast
9. Serve with melted butter mixed with a little lemon juice or with Hollandaise sauce

title: Keitetty savustettu kieli (Boiled Smoked Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- Smoked tongue
- Cold water
- Pepper
- Onion
- Bay leaves
- Sweet or tart sauce

## Directions

1. Wash the tongue.
2. If too salty, soak in cold water over night.
3. Put on the fire in cold water with pepper, onion and bay leaves.
4. Cook slowly until tender.
5. Skin immediately, remove the bone.
6. Serve hot or cold with sweet or tart sauce.

title: Keitetty suolaliha (Boiled Corned Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- Corned beef
- Cold water
- Seasonings and vegetables according to taste
- Cabbage

## Directions

1. Put the meat on fire in plenty of cold water.
2. Bring slowly to boiling, simmer on steady fire allowing 30 minutes to a pound.
3. Cabbage and other kinds of vegetables may be boiled with the meat or separately and served with the meat.

title: Keitetty Suolanen Kinkku (Boiled Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Salt
- Mustard
- Bread crumbs
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the ham in cold water over night.
2. Rinse carefully and place on the fire in cold water.
3. When it has come to boil, lower the heat and let simmer, allowing 20 minutes to a pound.
4. When done, lift it out of the liquor and remove the skin.
5. Spread the ham with mustard.
6. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and sugar.
7. Bake in the oven until brown.

title: Keitetty suolanen makrelli (Boiled Salt Mackerel)
body:
## Ingredients

- salt mackerel
- warm water
- vinegar
- onion
- milk
- eggs
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Soak the fish in warm water skin side up.
2. Boil until tender with some vinegar and onion.
3. Place on a platter, cover with milk sauce mixed with hard boiled chopped eggs or two raw egg yolks according to taste.
4. The fish may be cut into fine pieces, mixed with milk sauce and served on toast.
5. The fish will taste better if boiled in milk instead of water.

title: Keitetty suolanen makrelli (Boiled Salt Mackerel)
body:
## Ingredients

- salt mackerel
- warm water
- a little vinegar
- onion
- milk
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Soak the fish in warm water.
2. Cook with a little vinegar and onion until tender.
3. Place on a serving-dish, pour milksauce, hard-boiled chopped eggs or two yolks upon it.
4. Or flake the fish in pieces, mix with sauce, serve on toast.
5. When milk is used for cooking instead of water, the fish will taste better.

title: Keitetty talvikyssäkaali (Boiled Winter Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- winter squash
- salted water
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut squash in pieces
2. Cook in salted water
3. Chop
4. Season with salt, pepper and butter

title: Keitetty uunissa paistettu kana (Roast Boiled Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- Butter, lard or chicken fat

## Directions

1. Place the chicken on the fire in boiling water, add salt, pepper and a tablespoon of vinegar.
2. When half done, lift into the roasting pan, spread with butter, lard or chicken fat and brown.
3. If becoming too brown, cover and add water to the drippings when necessary.
4. Baste often.
5. Make a gravy of the drippings in the pan.

title: Keitetty Vasikankieltä (Boiled Calf's Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf tongues
- salt
- peppercorns
- vegetables
- butter
- flour
- broth
- horseradish
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Wash the tongues and scrape.
2. Place on the fire in boiling water with salt, peppercorns and vegetables.
3. Cook on a slow fire until tender, about 1 hour.
4. Remove the skin.
5. If the tongues are small, split them lengthwise in the middle.
6. Prepare a sauce of butter, flour and liquor of the tongue.
7. Add grated horseradish or tomato.

title: Keitetty Vasikanpää (Boiled Calf's Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's head, split and cleaned
- cold water
- lemon
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- 10 peppercorns
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 onion
- 1 carrot
- parsley
- egg
- bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Rinse the calf's head well and remove the tongue and brains.
2. Boil the brains quickly.
3. Soak the head in cold water and place in boiling cold water.
4. When it comes to a boil, drain the water and cool in cold water.
5. Rub the head with lemon and place in hot water to simmer slowly.
6. Add vinegar, peppercorns, bay leaf, onion, carrot and parsley sprigs.
7. Cover and simmer until the meat is tender but not falling apart.
8. Remove the bones carefully and place the meat in a pan shaped to the original form.
9. Brush with egg, sprinkle with bread crumbs and dot with small pieces of butter.
10. Brown in the oven.
11. Serve with roulade or allemande sauce.

title: Keitetty viheriä sipuli (Boiled Leek)
body:
## Ingredients

- leek
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare the leek
2. Cook in salted water

title: Keitetty Viheriä Sipuli (Boiled Leek)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leeks
- Salted water
- Toast
- Melted butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Rinse and clean the leeks.
2. Cook in salted water until tender.
3. Lay on toast.
4. Cover with melted butter.
5. Season with salt and pepper.

title: Keitettyjen kylmien mahapalasten (Fried Tripe)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold boiled tripe
- Melted butter
- Lemon juice
- Egg
- Bread crumbs or flour
- Butter or lard

## Directions

1. Pour melted butter mixed with some lemon juice over pieces of cold boiled tripe
2. Let stand for an hour
3. Dip in egg and roll in bread crumbs or flour
4. Fry in butter or lard until light brown
5. Serve with Béarnaise or hot mayonnaise sauce

title: Keitettyjä maissia (Boiled Green Corn)
body:
## Ingredients

- green corn
- salted water or milk

## Directions

1. Remove the leaves and the stalk, boil for 10-20 minutes in salted water or in milk.
2. Serve on a folded napkin.

title: Keitettyjä makeita perunoita (Boiled Sweet Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet potatoes
- Salt
- Water
- Syrup (optional)

## Directions

1. Rinse the potatoes well
2. Place on the fire in boiling water slightly salted
3. Boil until tender
4. Peel and place into a hot oven for a few minutes if too watery
5. Cover with syrup and brown quickly in oven if desired

title: Keitettyjä mansikoita (Boiled Strawberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup water
- Strawberries

## Directions

1. Cook 2 cups of sugar with 1 cup of water for 15 minutes.
2. Add strawberries, bring to boil.
3. Pour into a dish, let cool.
4. Serve in a glass dish with some of their own liquid.

title: Keitettyjä Mansikoita (Boiled Strawberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of sugar
- 1 cupful of water
- strawberries

## Directions

1. Cook 2 cupfuls of sugar with 1 cupful of water 15 minutes
2. Put strawberries in and let boil up
3. Pour in a bowl and let cool down
4. Serve in a glass dish, with a small quantity of their own liquor

title: Keitettyjä palkopapuja (Boiled String Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- string beans
- salt
- baking soda
- pepper
- melted butter
- parsley (optional)
- apples (optional)
- flour (optional)
- sugar (optional)
- lemon juice or vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove all the strings from the beans
2. Cut the beans in pieces
3. Put on the fire in boiling salt water with baking soda
4. Cook until tender
5. Drain
6. Season with salt and pepper and add melted butter
7. A tiny bunch of parsley added to the beans while boiling will give good flavor
8. Alternatively, cook the beans with two pared and sliced apples, make a sauce of butter flour and liquor of the beans, season with salt, sugar, lemon juice or vinegar and pour over the beans
9. Or serve without sauce seasoned with salt and sugar and mixed with melted butter

title: Keitettyjä perunoita (Boiled Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- water

## Directions

1. Pare potatoes and cut into balls of equal size, or use a potato ball cutter, and rinse well.
2. Place on the fire in boiling water with a dash of salt and let simmer gently until tender.
3. Drain off the water and let stand near the fire uncovered for a while so the water vapor evaporates.
4. For old potatoes, place on the fire in cold salted water to boil.

title: Keitettyjä porkkanoita (Boiled Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- Small carrots of equal size or larger carrots
- Salt
- Water
- 1 tablespoon butter
- Chopped parsley (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel equal-sized carrots, cut the bigger carrots in pieces.
2. Place on the fire in boiling water with some salt, cook slowly until tender.
3. Drain.
4. Add a tablespoon of butter to the carrots, shake over the fire until the butter is mixed with the carrots.
5. Sprinkle with parsley, if desired.

title: Keitettyjä porkkanoita (Boiled Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound small carrots of uniform size
- 1 tablespoonful salt
- 1 tablespoonful sugar
- butter
- 1 tablespoonful minced parsley (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel the carrots and place in boiling water to cook.
2. Add salt and sugar and let simmer until tender.
3. Pour off the water and place butter in a saucepan.
4. Return the saucepan to the stove to heat it up for a few minutes.
5. Add minced parsley if desired.

title: Keitettyjä porkkanoita (Boiled Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of small uniform carrots
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar
- butter
- minced parsley (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel carrots and put in boiling water.
2. Add 1 tablespoonful of salt and 1 tablespoonful of sugar.
3. Let simmer gently until tender.
4. Drain water and put butter in the saucepan and place by the fire for a few minutes.
5. Large carrots may also be used, but should be cut into dice or strips.
6. May add 1 tablespoonful of minced parsley.

title: Keitettyjä punajuuria (Boiled Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beets
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the beets.
2. Place on the fire in boiling salted water and cook until tender.
3. Young beets will be done in an hour, old ones require about 3 hours.
4. Put into cold water so as to remove the peels easier.
5. Cut the young beets in quarters, the old ones in slices.
6. Serve sprinkled with salt and pepper and butter, or with vinegar mixed in melted butter.

title: Keitettyjä punajuuria (Boiled Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- salt
- water
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Wash beets.
2. Put into a saucepan of salted boiling water and cook for an hour.
3. Drain, scrape, slice and serve in a deep dish with melted butter poured over them.

title: Keitettyjä sipulia (Boiled Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- Onions of uniform size
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Select onions of uniform size
2. Peel and rinse
3. Place in boiling water
4. Cook 5 minutes, drain off water and pour fresh boiling salted water over
5. Cook until tender
6. If placing onions in cold water no need to change the water
7. Serve either with milk sauce, or with a sauce made of butter, flour and water in which the onions have cooked
8. Season with salt

title: Keitettyjä sipulia (Boiled Onions)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions of equal size
- salt
- butter
- flour
- water
- pepper
- lemon juice
- egg yolks (optional)

## Directions

1. Select onions of equal size; peel and rinse
2. Place on the fire in boiling water; parboil for 5 minutes
3. Drain the water and pour boiling water over again; season with salt
4. Cook until tender. If put on the fire in cold water, you do not need to change the water
5. Serve either with milk sauce, or prepare a sauce of butter, flour and the water in which the onions cooked
6. Season with salt, pepper and lemon juice
7. If desired, add 2 egg yolks at the end; do not boil after that
8. Alternatively, serve boiled onions with butter, salt and pepper

title: Keitettyjä viheriäherneitä (Boiled Green Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- green peas
- salt
- 1/4 teaspoon soda
- pepper
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Put the peas on the fire in boiling water with a dash of salt and 1/4 teaspoon soda.
2. Cook until tender but not mushy.
3. Drain, season with salt and pepper.
4. Add butter and some sugar if desired.

title: Keitettyjä viheriäherneitä (Boiled Green Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- salt
- 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
- butter
- pepper
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Put the peas on the fire in boiling water with a dash of salt and 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
2. Cook until tender but not mushy
3. Drain the water
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Add butter and some sugar if desired
6. When using canned peas rinse them well in cold water before cooking

title: Keitettyä majoneesikastiketta (Boiled Mayonnaise Dressing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- 2 tablespoons salad oil or butter
- 1/2 teaspoon mustard
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- Some pepper and sugar

## Directions

1. Put spices into a small double boiler
2. Mix in the yolks, vinegar and lemon juice
3. Cook stirring well until thick
4. Remove from the fire, add the oil or butter, stirring thoroughly
5. Dilute with cream or lemon juice before serving

title: Keitettyä makaroonia (Boiled Macaroni)
body:
## Ingredients

- macaroni
- salted water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- cheese crumbs

## Directions

1. Break macaroni to pieces 1 inch long
2. Rinse
3. Put on the fire in plenty of salted water
4. Cook for 20 minutes or until soft
5. Drain and cover with cold water to loosen the starch
6. Reheat
7. Season with salt and pepper
8. Dilute with butter
9. Serve in a vegetable dish with the top sprinkled with cheese crumbs

title: Keitettyä nuorta kaalia (Boiled Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Salt
- 1/4 teaspoon baking soda
- Butter or milk sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the head of cabbage in four or eight parts.
2. Put on the fire in boiling salt water with 1/4 teaspoon baking soda.
3. Cook until tender, about 1/2 hour.
4. Drain carefully.
5. Serve with melted butter or rich milk sauce.

title: Keitettyä pinaattia (Boiled Spinach)
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean carefully.
2. Cook until tender.
3. Drain.
4. Mix with butter, season with salt.

title: Keitettyä virnaa (Boiled Lentils)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound lentils
- cold water
- butter
- sugar
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- salt
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Rinse 1 pound of lentils
2. Put on the fire in cold water
3. Let simmer with some butter and sugar until tender
4. Make a sauce of 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon flour mixed with liquor of lentils
5. Bring to boiling
6. Season with salt and lemon juice
7. Alternatively, drain the lentils and add salt, butter and lemon juice

title: Keitetyt Munat (Boiled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Wash eggs with lukewarm water
2. Put them carefully into boiling water and cook 3-4 minutes, uncovered
3. Alternatively, put the eggs into cold water and place on the range
4. When coming to boil, remove the eggs, put them in cold water and wipe
5. For hardboiled eggs, put the eggs into boiling water and cook 10 minutes uncovered
6. Put into cold water to remove the shells easier

title: Keltajuuria ja herneitä (Carrots with Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- small carrots
- salted water or beef stock
- boiled green peas in equal part to carrots
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- parsley

## Directions

1. Wash and scrape small carrots and cut into small dice.
2. Cook in salted water or beef stock until tender.
3. Strain and add equal part of boiled green peas.
4. Season with salt, pepper, butter and parsley.

title: Kerma Hyydyke (Blanc Mange)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cupfuls of cream
- 3 teaspoonfuls of powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of vanilla
- 4 leaves or 3 tablespoons of gelatine
- macaroons and fruit jelly for garnish

## Directions

1. Whip cream with sugar to a froth.
2. Dissolve gelatine in water and add carefully to the cream.
3. Add vanilla.
4. Pour in a serving dish to stiffen.
5. Garnish with macaroons and fruit jelly.

title: Kerma kastike (Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar
- 2 beaten yolks
- 3/4 cupful of cream or milk
- 3 tablespoonfuls of wine

## Directions

1. Rub butter and sugar to a cream
2. Add 2 beaten yolks and 3/4 cupful of cream or milk
3. Whip in double-boiler until thick
4. Remove from the fire
5. Mix with 3 tablespoonfuls of wine

title: Kerma-pannukakkuja (Cream Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of sour cream
- 2 cupfuls of sweet cream
- 3 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Whip 4 yolks and 1 whole egg with sugar to a froth.
2. Pour cream into it, add flour and salt.
3. Lastly stir in whites, beaten stiff.
4. Fry thin pancakes in fryingpan or bake in oven.

title: Kermaa appelsiinikorissa (Orange Cream in Baskets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 1 cup thin cream
- Yellow rind of 1 lemon
- Juice of 5 oranges
- 3/4 package of gelatine
- 1 1/2 cups whipping cream

## Directions

1. Bring to boil the thin cream with the lemon rind
2. Beat the yolks with the sugar
3. Add gradually the scalded cream
4. Beat over the fire until thick
5. Let cool
6. Mix with the orange juice and the gelatine
7. Fold in the whipped cream
8. Fill the baskets with this mixture
9. Have oranges of equal size
10. Remove the peel of half an orange leaving 1/4 inch wide strip in the middle for the handle of the basket
11. Remove the pulp
12. Notch in points the edges of the baskets

title: Kermahyydyke (Blanc Mange)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cups cream
- 3 tablespoons powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1/4 package gelatine, dissolved

## Directions

1. Whip the cream.
2. Add the sugar, vanilla and the dissolved gelatine.
3. Pour into a serving dish to set.
4. Garnish with macaroons and fruit jelly.

title: Kermahyytelö (Cream Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups whipping cream
- 1/2 cup powdered sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla or lemon juice
- 1 tablespoon gelatine dissolved in 1/4 cup water

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1 tablespoon gelatine in 1/4 cup water.
2. Add vanilla and one part of the sugar.
3. Heat until gelatine is dissolved.
4. Whip the cream with the remaining sugar.
5. Add cream mixture gradually to the gelatine mixture when it has cooled somewhat.
6. Pour into a mold to set.
7. Unmold and garnish with preserved fruit and macaroons.

title: Kermahyytelö (Cream Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of rich cream
- 3/4 cupful of powdered sugar
- 5-6 leaves of gelatine
- 1/4 teaspoonful of vanilla or lemon juice
- 1/2 cupful of water

## Directions

1. Dissolve gelatine in 1/2 cupful of water
2. Whip cream and sugar to a stiff froth and mix with seasonings
3. Stir the dissolved gelatine into the whipped cream, when it is cool, but not firm
4. Pour in a mold to get quite firm
5. Garnish the cream jelly with stewed fruit and macaroons

title: Kermakakku-jälkiruoka (Cream Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups whipping cream
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs to a froth.
2. Add the whipped cream and flour.
3. Bake in a buttered mold; sprinkle the top with sugar.
4. Serve with jam.

title: Kermakakku-jälkiruoka (Dessert of Cream Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of rich cream
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Whip eggs to a froth
2. Add beaten cream and flour
3. Bake in a buttered mold sprinkled with sugar
4. Serve as dessert with jam

title: Kermakastike (Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 3/4 cup cream or milk
- lemon juice or wine

## Directions

1. Rub until creamy 2 tablespoons butter with 1 tablespoon sugar
2. Add 2 egg yolks beaten until frothy and 3/4 cup cream or milk
3. Beat in a double boiler until thick
4. Remove from the fire, add lemon juice or wine

title: Kermamunkkia (Cream Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 level tablespoon butter
- 1/2 cup flour
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 white beaten stiff
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 square chocolate
- 2 tablespoons milk
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla
- 2 egg yolks
- Fondant icing

## Directions

1. Boil the milk with the butter, add the flour, cook stirring well until the bottom begins to brown.
2. When cool, add the yolks and at the last the whites beaten stiff.
3. Shape through a pastry bag or drop by the spoonful on the baking sheet.
4. Brush with egg; bake.
5. When done fill with the chocolate mixture: Dissolve the chocolate, add half of the sugar and the milk. Whip the rest of the sugar with the yolks, stir on the fire until it thickens. Flavor and let cool.
6. When filled, coat the puffs with fondant icing.

title: Kermapannukakkuja (Cream Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1 1/2 cups sour cream
- 2 cups fresh cream
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 1/2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg yolks and 1 whole egg with the sugar until frothy
2. Pour in the cream, add salt and flour
3. Last of all beat the egg whites until firm
4. Fry into cakes or bake in a buttered pan

title: Kermasalaatin kastike No. 1 (Cream Salad Dressing No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 teaspoon mustard
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup cream or milk
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 2 tablespoons butter
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Mix together all the dry ingredients
2. Add the beaten eggs, lemon juice and cream
3. Beat in a double boiler until thick
4. Remove from the fire, add butter, beat well
5. Let cool

title: Kermasalaatin kastike No. 2 (Cream Salad Dressing No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg yolks (boiled)
- 1½ teaspoons mustard (prepared)
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- 1 cup cream
- Salt, pepper and sugar

## Directions

1. Rub well the chopped yolks with mustard and seasonings
2. Add slowly vinegar and lemon juice, rub until a smooth paste
3. Last of all add gradually the whipped cream
4. Or mix ½ cup of oil with scant ½ cupful of lemon juice
5. Add salt and sugar according to taste
6. Mix last with 1 cup of cream

title: Kermavohveleita (Cream Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups whipping cream
- 1 cup flour
- 3 eggs
- 2 tablespoons melted butter

## Directions

1. Whip the cream
2. Add the beaten eggs, flour and butter
3. Fry in hot greased waffle-iron until brown on both sides
4. Serve with jam, maple syrup or honey

title: Kerroskakku No. 1 (Layer Cake No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ cup butter
- 1½ cups sugar
- 2½ cups flour
- 4 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Rub the butter with the sugar until creamy.
2. Add the yolks one by one, beating the mixture well.
3. Mix in milk, vanilla and flour sifted together with baking powder.
4. Last of all fold in the egg whites beaten until firm.
5. Bake in three layer cake tins.
6. Have filling between layers.
7. Coat with icing.

title: Kerroskakku No. 2 (Layer Cake No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1/2 cup cornstarch
- 5 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Rub the butter with the sugar until creamy
2. Add the egg yolks beaten to a froth
3. Mix in cornstarch and baking powder sifted with flour and vanilla
4. Last of all fold in the firmly beaten egg whites
5. Bake in two layer cake tins

title: Keuhkomuhennus (Stewed Lungs)
body:
## Ingredients

- Calf heart and lungs
- Salt
- Pepper
- 1 onion
- Butter
- 2 tablespoons wheat flour or bread crumbs
- 2 cups milk or bouillon
- Lemon juice
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Split the heart and lungs of calf
2. Soak in cold water to remove the blood
3. Put on the fire in cold water with salt and pepper
4. Cook for 1 hour
5. Remove the skin and the throat, chop the meat
6. Brown the onion in butter, mix with the meat and fry until brown
7. Add 2 tablespoons of flour or bread crumbs and 2 cups of milk or bouillon
8. Season with salt and pepper, flavor with lemon juice
9. Serve with potatoes

title: Keveal-kakku (Light Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 1/2 cups sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- Yellow rind and juice of 1 lemon
- 1/2 cup milk
- 3 cups flour
- 1/2 teaspoon soda
- 1 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Rub butter and sugar together until creamy.
2. Beat the yolks until frothy.
3. Add cream of tartar and beat until stiff froth.
4. Combine this well with the butter mixture.
5. Add salt, lemon rind, juice, milk and flour sifted with soda gradually.
6. Last of all fold in the egg whites beaten until firm.
7. Bake for 1 hour.

title: Kieli Mahasylkirauhasen Kanssa (Tongue with Sweetbreads)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tongue, sliced thin
- Sweetbreads, fried and diced
- Egg white
- Butter
- Spinach
- Truffled sauce

## Directions

1. Butter individual molds
2. Line the edges with thin slices of tongue
3. Fill the molds with small pieces of fried sweetbreads
4. Fasten the tongue and sweetbread together with white of egg
5. Bake in oven in a water bath covered with buttered paper
6. Serve on a foundation of spinach with truffled sauce

title: Kieli Palkopapujen Kanssa (Tongue with String Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cold cooked tongue
- Boiled string beans
- Egg yolk
- Strained tomato
- Cornstarch
- Hollandaise sauce

## Directions

1. Cut cold tongue in slices
2. Place a few boiled string beans on top of each slice
3. Roll together and fasten the edges with egg yolk
4. Place on a platter, cover, and heat in oven
5. Make a sauce of strained tomato and cornstarch to baste the rolls
6. Pour Hollandaise sauce on the platter and place the rolls on top

title: Kieli-timboli (Tongue Timbale)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup ground tongue
- 1/2 cup whipped cream
- 3 tablespoons dissolved gelatine or 4 leaves of gelatine
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- liquid jelly
- red pepper
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Dissolve the gelatine in scanty water.
2. Add the lemon juice and pour gradually into the meat.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Add the whipped cream at the last.
5. Moisten the timbales with liquid jelly.
6. Garnish with red pepper and vegetables and fill with the mousse.
7. Put aside to set.
8. Serve with salad.

title: Kieli-Timboli (Tongue Timbales)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kupillista jauhettua kieltä (1 cupful of ground tongue)
- ½ kupillista vispattua kermaa (½ cupful of whipped cream)
- 3 ruokalusikallista tai 4 lehteä liivakkoa (3 tablesp. or 4 leaves of gelatine)
- 2 ruokalusikallista sherry viiniä (2 tablespoonfuls of sherry)
- pippuria ja suolaa (pepper and salt)

## Directions

1. Dissolve the gelatine in a little water.
2. Add the sherry and pour this mixture gradually into the tongue.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Finally add the whipped cream.
5. Moisten the small timbales with the mixture of gelatine.
6. Garnish with red pepper and fill with the mixture of tongue.
7. Let it get firm.
8. Serve with salad.

title: Kielihyytelö (Tongue à la Daube)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup ground tongue
- 1/2 cup whipped cream
- 3 tablespoons gelatine
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Mix the tongue with salt, pepper, lemon juice, gelatine and cream.
2. Line the mold with aspic.
3. Garnish with red pepper and slices of hard boiled egg.
4. Fill the mold with the tongue mixture.
5. Let set.
6. Serve unmolded with lettuce leaves.

title: Kinkku-muussi (Ham Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound ham
- 1/2 pound chicken breast
- 2 egg whites
- 1/2 pint cream

## Directions

1. Force ham with chicken breast through meat chopper several times.
2. Add egg whites and pound well.
3. Press through a sieve.
4. Dilute gradually with 1/2 pint of cream or until soft enough.
5. Test consistency by dropping 1/2 spoonful of mixture into boiling salted water.
6. If too thick, dilute with cream; if too soft, add an egg white.

title: Kinkku-timboli (Ham Timbale)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups ground ham
- 3 eggs
- 1 cup bread soaked in milk
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- 1/2 tablespoon chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- mushroom sauce

## Directions

1. Soak the bread crumbs in milk.
2. Add butter and cook for 5 minutes.
3. Stir into the bread crumbs the ground ham, parsley and the beaten eggs.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Put into buttered molds.
6. Bake in oven in a pan of water for 25 minutes.
7. Serve with mushroom sauce.

title: Kinkku-Timboli (Ham Timbale)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kupillista hienoksi jauhettua kinkkua (2 cupfuls of ground ham)
- 3 munaa (3 eggs)
- 1 kupillista maidossa lioitettua leipää (1 cupful of bread, soaked in milk)
- 2 ruokalusikallista sherry viiniä (2 tablespoonfuls of sherry)
- ½ ruokalusikallista hienonnettua persiljaa (½ tablespoonful of minced parsley)
- suolaa ja pippuria (salt and pepper)

## Directions

1. Soak the bread crumbs in milk.
2. Add the butter and cook 5 minutes.
3. Stir into it the ground ham, parsley, and eggs beaten well.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Pour into a buttered mold.
6. Bake in oven in a pan of water for 25 minutes.
7. Serve with mushroom sauce.

title: Kinkkuvoileipiä (Ham Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold ham
- mayonnaise
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Mix chopped cold ham with mayonnaise.
2. Use between slices of bread.

title: Kinushkles
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of cream
- 3/4 cupful of molasses
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Cook cream with sugar and vanilla to a thick mass.
2. Put aside to cool.
3. Boil molasses, mix gradually with the cold creamy mass.
4. Cook stirring well, until when tested in cold water mixture will become brittle.
5. Spread platter with salad oil.
6. Pour the mixture on.
7. Let cool.
8. Cut in pieces and wrap in paper.

title: Kirnupiimäkakku (Buttermilk Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 cup buttermilk or milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 2 tablespoons butter

## Directions

1. Mix in a bowl all the dry ingredients with the butter.
2. Add gradually the milk stirring well.
3. Turn onto the baking board, knead well.
4. Divide the dough in two parts, roll into 3/8 inch cakes, sprinkle with flour.
5. Divide both cakes in 6 parts, fry on both sides or bake for 10-15 minutes.
6. Serve hot.

title: Kirsikka hillo (Cherry Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- sugar equal in weight to the cherries
- 2 cupfuls of water per 5 pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Rinse cherries and remove the pits.
2. Weigh the cherries and take an equal weight of sugar and 2 cupfuls of water for every 5 pounds of sugar.
3. Cook sugar and water to a clear syrup that forms threads when dropped from a spoon, carefully skimming off the foam.
4. Add the berries and let them heat slowly.
5. Cook on low heat, shaking the saucepan.

title: Kirsikkahillo (Cherry Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- Cherries
- Sugar equal in weight to cherries
- 2 cupfuls of water to 5 lbs. of sugar

## Directions

1. Rinse cherries and stone.
2. Allow 2 cupfuls of water to 5 lbs. of sugar.
3. Simmer sugar with water until clear syrup threading when tested by dropping.
4. Skim carefully.
5. Place the cherries into the syrup.
6. Heat slowly.
7. Simmer on a slow fire, shaking the kettle and skimming until the scum ceases to rise.
8. When the cherries are clear and the scum no longer rises, pour the compote into jars.
9. If the syrup is not of right consistency, continue cooking for a while.
10. Pour over the berries when cooled.

title: Kirsikkahilloke (Cherry Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Wash cherries and remove the stones.
2. Weigh the berries.
3. Have an equal amount of sugar.
4. Lay in a kettle and let stand over night.
5. Bring slowly to boiling point.
6. Simmer until clear and thick.
7. Put into jars while hot.

title: Kirsikkahyytelö (Cherry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- cherries
- currants
- apples

## Directions

1. Have cherries not quite ripe.
2. Prepare plain or mixed with currants, apples etc. as given for jellies.

title: Kirsikkapiirakka (Cherry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups cherries
- ½ cup sugar
- cornstarch
- pastry for two crusts
- alternative: 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Wash the cherries, remove the stems and the stones.
2. Cover a pie plate with pastry, place the cherries over, sprinkle with sugar mixed with cornstarch or use 1 egg yolk diluted with liquor of cherries.
3. Pour the yolk over the cherries.
4. Cover with the crust.
5. Bake in a moderate oven.

title: Kisses
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of almonds
- 1 lb. of powdered sugar
- 2 whites of eggs

## Directions

1. Peel almonds, grate and pound with sugar in mortar or in wooden bowl.
2. Beat whites of eggs to a hard froth and stir almonds into it.
3. Drop almond-mixture with spoon on baking sheet.
4. Press lightly with finger in centre of each cookie, put jelly in.
5. Bake in a slow oven.

title: Kohokakku No. 1 (Sponge Cake No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup flour
- 4 tablespoons cornstarch
- 4 tablespoons water
- 1 tablespoon brandy or lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks with the sugar, add water, brandy, flour sifted with baking powder.
2. Last fold in the egg whites beaten to a stiff froth.

title: Kohokakku No. 2 (Sponge Cake No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- 1 1/2 cups powdered sugar
- 2 cups flour
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- 12 egg whites
- Some salt

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks with sugar, add lemon juice, egg whites beaten until firm and last of all mix in lightly the flour.
2. Bake for 1 hour in a buttered tin.

title: Kokkareita No. 1 (Dumplings No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1/4 cupful of milk
- 1 cupful of flour
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 egg whites
- Salt, pepper and nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook a porridge of butter, milk and flour.
2. Remove from the fire.
3. Add 3 yolks of egg one by one to the porridge stirring well.
4. Season with salt, pepper and nutmeg.
5. Beat 3 whites of egg until firm and add last of all.
6. Drop by the spoonful into boiling salt water.
7. Boil for 5 minutes or until done.

title: Kokkareita No. 2 (Dumplings No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- 1 beaten egg
- Flour
- Salt

## Directions

1. Mix melted butter with beaten egg.
2. Add enough flour to keep together.
3. Season with salt.
4. Drop by the spoonful into boiling bouillon.
5. The consistency should be moderately thin, about that of a thin porridge.

title: Konjakki kastike (Brandy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 3/4 cupful of brandy
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of water

## Directions

1. Cook sugar in 1/2 cupful of water to a light brown
2. Mix it with the remaining water
3. Let cool, not quite cold
4. Turn the caramel over dessert when serving it
5. Pour carefully brandy on and light it
6. Serve with plum and marrow puddings

title: Konjakkikastike (Brandy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup brandy
- 1 1/4 cup water

## Directions

1. Cook a light brown syrup of the sugar and 1/2 cup of water
2. Add the rest of the water
3. Let cool until not quite cold
4. Pour over the pudding shortly before serving
5. Add carefully the brandy and light it
6. Serve with plum and marrow puddings

title: Konjakkirinkeliä (Cognac Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 lbs. of butter
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 1 lb. of flour
- 1/4 cupful of cognac

## Directions

1. Beat butter to a froth
2. Add sugar, cognac and flour
3. Form the batter into small rings
4. Bake in a hot oven

title: Konjakkirinkilöitä (Brandy Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 4 cups flour
- 1/4 cup brandy

## Directions

1. Rub butter with sugar until creamy
2. Add brandy and flour
3. Form into tiny rings
4. Bake in a hot oven

title: Korintti Kakku (Currant Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- ½ lb. of butter
- ½ lb. of flour
- ½ lb. of sugar
- 5 eggs
- juice of ½ lemon
- ½ cupful of currants
- 1 teaspoonful of baking powder

## Directions

1. Work butter to a froth, add eggs, one at a time, alternately with 2 tablespoonfuls of flour and 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar.
2. Lastly add the rest of the flour and sugar, mixed with baking powder.
3. Stir lemon juice, a little grated lemon rind and currants into the mixture.
4. Pour in a buttered mold and bake in oven about ¾—1 hour.

title: Korinttikakku (Currant Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup butter
- 1 1/2 cup powdered sugar
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 5 eggs
- Juice of half a lemon
- 1/2 cup currants
- 1 teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Rub the butter until creamy
2. Add the eggs one by one
3. After each egg work into the mixture 2 tablespoons flour and 2 tablespoons sugar
4. Last mix in the rest of the flour and sugar with the flour sifted with the baking powder
5. Stir in the lemon juice and the currants rolled in flour
6. Pour into a buttered tin
7. Bake for about 1 hour

title: Kova kastike (Hard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 cupful of sugar
- lemon juice
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Rub butter and sugar to a cream
2. Flavor with lemon juice and vanilla

title: Kova-kastike (Hard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 cup sugar
- lemon juice
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Rub until creamy 2 tablespoons butter with 1 cup sugar
2. Flavor with lemon juice and vanilla

title: Krokettia makeista perunoista (Sweet Potato Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups mashed sweet potatoes
- 3 tablespoons butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- 1 or 2 yolks of egg
- Chopped parsley (optional)
- Bread crumbs
- Egg for dipping
- Lard for frying
- Syrup for basting (optional)

## Directions

1. Add butter, salt, pepper and yolks of egg to mashed sweet potatoes.
2. Beat well.
3. Chopped parsley may be mixed last of all, if preferred.
4. Form into balls or croquettes, roll in bread crumbs, dip in egg and roll in bread crumbs again.
5. Cook in deep lard.

title: Kuivattuja Lima papuja (Dried Lima Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- lima beans
- a piece of pork
- 2 tablespoons butter or lard
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 cup bean cooking liquid

## Directions

1. Rinse the beans, let stand in lukewarm water until they begin to crack.
2. Put on the fire in cold water with a piece of pork.
3. Cook slowly until tender.
4. Make a brown sauce of 2 tablespoons butter or lard browned with 2 tablespoons flour and diluted with 1 cup liquor of the beans.
5. Add the beans to the sauce.
6. Bring to boiling.

title: Kuivattuja papuja (Dried Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- pork
- bay leaf
- onion
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- cream or bouillon

## Directions

1. Rinse the beans, let stand in cold water over night.
2. Put on the fire in cold water, let boil slowly changing the water once when the beans are half done.
3. Add a few pieces of pork, 1 bay leaf and 1 small onion.
4. If the beans are to be baked, they have to be removed from the fire immediately when they begin to crack.
5. For puree, cook the beans until tender, rub through a sieve or force through a meat chopper.
6. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Add a lump of butter and dilute with cream or bouillon.

title: Kukkakaali au Gratin (Cauliflower au Gratin)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled cauliflower
- butter
- milk sauce
- grated cheese
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the boiled cauliflower in pieces
2. Lay the pieces into a buttered mold
3. Cover with milk sauce
4. Sprinkle with grated cheese
5. Dot with butter
6. Add a sprinkling of bread crumbs
7. Bake until the top is brown

title: Kukkakaali au Gratin (Cauliflower au Gratin)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled cauliflower
- butter
- milk sauce
- cheese crumbs
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut boiled cauliflower into small pieces
2. Put in a buttered form
3. Pour milk sauce over
4. Sprinkle with cheese crumbs and bread crumbs
5. Dot with bits of butter
6. Bake in oven until brown

title: Kukkakaali maitosoosin kanssa (Creamed Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt water
- milk sauce
- egg yolks (optional)
- lemon juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil the cauliflower in salt water for 20 minutes
2. Drain and place in a serving dish
3. Cover with milk sauce
4. The sauce may be mixed with 2 yolks of egg and some lemon juice, if preferred

title: Kukkakaali à la Hollandaise (Cauliflower à la Hollandaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- Hollandaise sauce

## Directions

1. Cook the cauliflower as directed for boiled cauliflower
2. Serve with Hollandaise sauce

title: Kukkakaali-hyytelö (Jellied Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- tomato jelly

## Directions

1. Boil the cauliflower
2. Garnish individual molds
3. Put a piece of cauliflower into the molds
4. Fill with liquid tomato jelly

title: Kukkakaali-pyree (Cauliflower Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cauliflowers
- 3 quarts of vegetable stock
- 1 leek
- 1 parsnip
- 1 carrot
- salt and pepper
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cupful of cream

## Directions

1. Cut the thick stalks of cauliflower into thin slices
2. Peel the remaining vegetables and cut in pieces and cook in a small quantity of water until soft
3. Press through a strainer and mix with the stock
4. Use the nicest pieces of cauliflower in the soup and let cook until tender
5. Thicken with butter and flour and last of all add the yolks of eggs and cream

title: Kukkakaalia Tomaattikastikkeen Kanssa (Cauliflower with Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salted water
- tomatoes
- flour
- water
- butter
- salt
- sugar
- pepper
- cream
- baking soda

## Directions

1. Boil the cauliflower in salted water until tender
2. Cut tomatoes in pieces
3. Boil tomatoes in a saucepan until tender
4. Thicken with flour mixed with some water
5. Add butter, salt, sugar and pepper
6. May be mixed with cream and a pinch of baking soda

title: Kukkakaalisalaatti (Cauliflower Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salted water
- 1 quart of vinegar
- 1/2 cupful of sugar
- cloves, 1 teaspoonful
- white pepper, 1 teaspoonful
- ginger, a little
- mace

## Directions

1. Cut cauliflower in small pieces
2. Cook in salted water 10-15 minutes
3. Place carefully into jars
4. Cook 1 quart of vinegar with 1/2 cupful of sugar, season with ginger and mace
5. Have the spices tied in a bag
6. Drain the brine on the cauliflower into the jar
7. Cover closely and keep in a cool place

title: Kukkakaalisalaatti (Cauliflower Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salted water
- lettuce leaves
- mayonnaise
- hard cooked eggs
- minced parsley
- olives

## Directions

1. Cook the cauliflower in salted water until tender
2. Place whole or in parts on a bed of lettuce
3. Cover with mayonnaise
4. Garnish with hard cooked eggs, minced parsley and olives

title: Kukkakaalisalaattia (Pickled Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt water
- 1 quart vinegar
- 1/2 cup sugar
- cloves
- white pepper, 1 teaspoon each
- ginger
- mace

## Directions

1. Cut cauliflower in small parts
2. Cook in salt water for 10-15 minutes
3. Lay carefully into jars
4. Boil 1 quart vinegar with 1/2 cup sugar
5. Add cloves and white pepper, 1 teaspoon each, some ginger and mace, having the spices tied in a bag
6. Strain the liquid over the cauliflower
7. Cover

title: Kultakakku (Gold Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup milk
- 5 or 7 egg yolks
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 lemon juice and rind

## Directions

1. Rub the butter with the sugar until creamy
2. Add the well beaten egg yolks
3. Add the juice and the grated yellow rind of a lemon
4. Add milk, baking powder and flour sifted together
5. Rub the mixture well

title: Kuningas Kana (Royal Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon chopped onion
- Fillets of roast or boiled chicken, diced
- 1/2 cup tomato
- 1 tablespoon chopped mushrooms
- 2 tablespoons mushroom liquor
- 1/4 cup Sherry wine or lemon juice
- Pepper and salt
- 1/2 teaspoon chopped green pepper
- Chopped truffles
- 1/3 teaspoon meat extract
- A few scallops

## Directions

1. Cook the onion in butter for a few minutes
2. Add the chicken cut in cubes, tomato, and mushrooms
3. Let boil for 10 minutes
4. Add the scallops, lemon juice, and spices
5. Bring to boiling and serve as an entree or substitute for fish
6. May be mixed with peas and asparagus tips if desired
7. Alternative: use meat stock instead of tomato, thicken with butter and flour, add 2 egg yolks diluted with cream, heat but do not boil

title: Kuningas Kana (Royal Chicken) - Alternative Preparation
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon chopped onion
- Fillets of roast or boiled chicken
- Meat stock
- Butter and flour for thickening
- 2 egg yolks
- Cream

## Directions

1. Cook the onion in butter
2. Add diced chicken fillets and meat stock
3. Thicken with butter and flour
4. Add 2 egg yolks diluted with cream
5. Heat but do not boil

title: Kuppi Kakku (Cup Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cupful of butter
- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of flour
- 1/2 cupful of milk
- 1/2 teaspoonful of vanilla
- 1 1/2 teaspoonfuls of baking powder

## Directions

1. Rub butter and sugar to a froth, add yolks of eggs, milk, vanilla, baking powder with flour.
2. Lastly add whites beaten stiff to a froth.
3. Bake in a buttered mold in a moderate oven.

title: Kuppikakku (Cup Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup milk
- 2 eggs
- Vanilla or lemon rind
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 1/2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Rub butter with sugar until creamy
2. Add eggs one at a time rubbing the mixture well
3. Mix with vanilla or lemon rind, milk and baking powder sifted with flour
4. Bake in two tins
5. For a plainer cake, use less butter, 1 egg and water instead of milk
6. For a quick cake, beat all the ingredients together in a bowl for 5 minutes

title: Kupu Kaali-Vanukas (Cabbage Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cabbage head
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 1 cupful of chopped mushrooms
- 2 slices of onion
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut and boil cabbage
2. Drain the water
3. Mix with butter, salt and sugar
4. Bake covered until tender
5. Cook chopped onion and mushrooms with 1 tablespoonful of butter in a pan
6. Butter a mould
7. Lay alternately the cabbage and mushrooms in it
8. Leave small dots of butter on the surface
9. Sprinkle with bread crumbs
10. Bake the pudding in a slow oven for ½ hour

title: Kupukaalivanukas (Cabbage Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- 2 tablespoons butter
- salt
- sugar
- onion
- mushrooms
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage in strips and parboil, then drain.
2. Fry with 2 tablespoons of butter, some salt and sugar until tender.
3. Fry in butter a chopped onion and mushrooms.
4. Fill a buttered mold with alternating layers of cabbage and mushrooms, dot with butter, and sprinkle with bread crumbs.
5. Bake for 1.5 hours or until brown.

title: Kurkku Hyydyke (Cucumber Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 middlesized cucumbers
- 2 tablespoonfuls of tomato
- 5 leaves of gelatine
- 1 cupful of chicken stock

## Directions

1. Put chicken stock, cucumbers and tomato on the fire.
2. Let boil until cucumber is soft.
3. Strain and stir gelatine leaves into it.
4. Clarify with two whites of eggs.
5. Line a mold with pieces of cucumber.
6. Pour the jelly in.

title: Kurkku-keitto (Cucumber Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large cucumbers
- 3 quarts of vegetable stock or water
- 2 leeks
- 1 celery
- 1 sprig of parsley
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cupful of cream
- salt and pepper
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, split, remove the seeds and cut in pieces
2. Clean the remaining vegetables and brown with onion in butter, adding a little sugar and salt to it
3. Pour the boiling stock on the vegetables and cook until stew
4. Press through a strainer, thicken with butter and flour
5. Beat the eggs, add the cream and soup into it slowly, stirring on the fire until hot
6. Cucumber cut in dice, peas, spinach, salad and carrots may be served in soup

title: Kurkku-soppa (Cucumber Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large cucumbers
- 1 1/2 quarts of white stock
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 onion or 1 slice of onion
- 2 egg yolks
- 1/2 cupful of cream
- salt, pepper, a little nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, cut into slices and remove the seeds.
2. Simmer the onion and cucumber slices in butter, add the flour and meat stock.
3. Let boil.

title: Kurkku-soppa (Cucumber Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large cucumbers
- 1 1/2 quarts of white stock
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 red onion or 1 slice of onion
- 2 yolks of eggs
- 1/2 cupful of cream
- salt, pepper and a little mace

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, cut into slices and remove the seeds.
2. Cook the onion with the slices of cucumbers in butter, add flour and white stock into it.
3. Let boil for a few minutes; pass through a strainer and season.
4. Whip the yolks and cream, pour the soup slowly into it beating well.
5. Add a spoonful of white wine to flavor the soup.

title: Kurkkuhyydyke (Cucumber Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 medium-sized cucumbers
- 2 tablespoons tomato
- 1 tablespoon gelatine or 6 leaves
- 1 cup chicken or veal stock
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Place on the fire the stock with the cucumber and the tomato. Cook until the cucumber is tender.
2. Strain, add the gelatine, clarify with the whites of 2 eggs.
3. Pour into a mold to set; mix with pieces of fresh cucumber, if desired.

title: Kurkkukastike (Cucumber Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 cups bouillon
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 pickled cucumber
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Make a gravy of butter, flour and bouillon, add lemon juice, chopped cucumber, sugar, pepper and salt.
2. Serve with fillet of beef, duck or veal.

title: Kurkkukeitto (Cucumber Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large cucumbers
- 1½ quarts white stock
- 1 red onion or a slice of onion
- 2 stalks celery
- 1 sprig parsley
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cup cream
- butter
- flour
- salt and pepper
- lemon or Sherry

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumbers, cut in slices, remove the seeds.
2. Boil quickly in butter the onion and the slices of cucumber, add flour and stock.
3. Continue to boil for a few minutes.
4. Force through a sieve and season.
5. Whip the yolks with the cream, add the soup slowly and beat well.
6. Some lemon or Sherry will improve the taste of the soup.
7. May be made by using vegetable stock or water and some tomato.

title: Kurkkusalaatti (Cucumber Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cucumber
- cold water
- lettuce
- French dressing
- tomato

## Directions

1. Peel the cucumber, place in cold water.
2. Cut in thin slices, arrange on a bed of lettuce.
3. Pour French dressing over.
4. Serve very cold with fish.
5. Cucumber and tomato together make a nice salad.

title: Kuuma ananasputinki (Hot Pineapple Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup flour
- 4 tablespoons cornstarch
- 2 1/2 cups cream
- 1/4 cup butter
- 1 pound pineapple

## Directions

1. Cook a paste of the cream and the flour.
2. Add the butter and the yolks beaten with sugar.
3. Cut the pineapple in cubes and add to the paste.
4. Last of all add the egg whites beaten firm.
5. Bake in a buttered mold in a pan of water for 1 hour.
6. Prepare the sauce from the liquid of pineapple by adding lemon juice, sugar and cornstarch.

title: Kuuma majoneesi-kastike (Hot Mayonnaise Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 yolks of egg
- 3 tablespoons olive oil
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- 1/4 cup boiling water
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Mix the oil drop by drop with the yolks, stirring well
2. Add gradually the vinegar and the water
3. Beat in a pan of boiling water until thick
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Add chopped parsley, if desired

title: Kylmä ankka (Cold Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- lemon juice or sherry wine
- 4 tablespoons gelatine dissolved in water
- 1 cup cream
- 1 cup chicken stock
- 3 tablespoons gelatine dissolved in water
- cornstarch
- Kitchen Bouquet
- whipped cream
- truffles
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Clean and roast the duck as directed.
2. When cold, remove the fillets and grind several times.
3. Mix with some lemon juice or sherry wine, 4 tablespoons of gelatine dissolved in water and 1 cup of cream.
4. Fill the breast space with this mixture.
5. Baste the duck with liquid jelly prepared of 1 cup of chicken stock, 3 tablespoons of gelatine dissolved in water and some cornstarch.
6. Color dark with Kitchen Bouquet.
7. Garnish the top with whipped cream, truffles and hard boiled eggs.

title: Kylmä Kana (Cold Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg yolks
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon gelatine
- cold water
- 1 cup chicken breast
- salt
- pepper
- 2 cups whipped cream

## Directions

1. Beat 2 yolks of egg to a froth, add 1/2 cup milk and 1 tablespoon gelatine dissolved in cold water.
2. Heat, beating well until thick, not allowing to boil.
3. Mix with 1 cup of chicken breast ground or cut in tiny pieces, season with salt and pepper.
4. Last of all add 2 cups of whipped cream.
5. Put into mold to set.
6. Serve with fruit salad. Instead of cream mayonnaise may be used.

title: Kylmä kieli hyytelön kanssa (Jellied Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- hot salted water
- 1 quart liquor
- 8 tablespoons dissolved gelatine, or 10 leaves of gelatine
- 1 1/2 cup tomato
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. Soak the tongue in cold water.
2. Scrape carefully in hot water.
3. Place in hot salted water to simmer until tender.
4. Allow 2 or 3 hours for boiling.
5. Skin the tongue.
6. Strain the liquor and skim.
7. Mix 8 tablespoons of dissolved gelatine with 1 quart of liquor (or 10 leaves of gelatine), add 1 1/2 cup of tomato and 2 egg whites.
8. Boil the mixture well while stirring, then let simmer gently for 5 minutes.
9. Strain through a fine cloth.
10. Pour cleared liquor on the bottom of a mold.
11. Cut the tongue into slices and arrange in the mold in the whole form.
12. Gradually pour the jelly on top and let set.

title: Kylmä Luumu Putinkki (Cold Plum Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of plums
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of cream
- 5 leaves or 4 tablesp. of gelatine
- 1/4 lb. of sugar

## Directions

1. Cook plums until tender in a small quantity of water
2. Stone and press through a sieve
3. Add sugar and gelatine dissolved in 1/2 cupful of plum liquor
4. Add lastly cream whipped to a froth
5. Set to firm in a mold
6. Turn on a serving dish and garnish with whipped cream

title: Kylmä luumuputinki (Cold Plum Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound plums
- 1 1/2 cups cream
- 5 leaves or 3/4 package gelatine
- 3/4 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the plums in scanty water until tender.
2. Remove the stones, force the plums through a sieve or a meat grinder.
3. Add sugar, gelatine and last the whipped cream.
4. Mold.
5. Serve unmolded garnished with whipped cream.
6. Prune pudding may be prepared in the same way.

title: Kylmä Suklaati Putinkki (Cold Chocolate Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 ozs. of chocolate
- 4 tablesp. of sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- 7 leaves or 5 tablesp. of gelatine
- 2 cupfuls of milk
- 2 cupfuls of cream

## Directions

1. Heat milk and chocolate together
2. Whip egg yolks with sugar
3. Pour the hot milk gradually into the egg yolks and sugar
4. Whip on the fire until it reaches a sabayon consistency
5. Add gelatine to the mixture and let cool
6. When the mixture begins to thicken (before it sets into lumps), add whipped cream
7. Put in a mold to set firm
8. Serve turned out of the mold with whipped cream

title: Kylmää kanaa hyytelön kera (Jellied Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 chicken
- salt
- pepper
- onion
- 2 stalks of celery
- 1 quart chicken stock
- 1/2 cup tomato
- 2 teaspoons meat extract
- 7 tablespoons gelatine dissolved in water, or 10 leaves of gelatine
- 2 egg whites
- hard boiled eggs
- beets
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the chicken and place on the fire to cook with salt, pepper, onion and 2 stalks of celery.
2. Let simmer until tender.
3. Remove the skin and bones and cut into nice pieces.
4. Take 1 quart of chicken stock, 1/2 cup of tomato, 2 teaspoons of meat extract, 7 tablespoons of gelatine dissolved in water or 10 leaves of gelatine and 2 whites of egg.
5. Whip over the fire until boiling.
6. Let simmer for 5 minutes.
7. Strain through a fine cloth.
8. Cover the bottom of a jelly mold with cleared liquid jelly.
9. Let thicken.
10. Garnish the mold with hard boiled eggs, beets and parsley.
11. Lay the pieces of chicken into mold and fill the mold with liquid jelly.
12. Put in a cold place to set.

title: Kylmää kuhaa (Cold Bass)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish fillets
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemon juice
- Butter
- Fish stock
- Tomato slices
- Hard boiled eggs
- Mayonnaise
- Lettuce leaves
- Crab tails
- Lemon slices
- Fish aspic

## Directions

1. Raise the fillets of the fish, remove the skin and bones and cut in pieces of equal size.
2. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and lemon juice, let stand for 10 minutes.
3. Place the pieces of fish in a pan with a dot of butter on each, add some fish stock.
4. Bake covered with buttered paper 15 minutes.
5. Remove from oven, let cool.
6. Arrange the fish on slices of tomato on a platter having a slice of egg on each piece.
7. Cover with rich mayonnaise.
8. Garnish around with leaves of lettuce, crab-tails, slices of lemon and aspic made of liquor of the fish.

title: Kylmää lohta (Cold Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- Salmon
- Mayonnaise
- Lettuce leaves
- Boiled vegetables: potatoes, artichokes, beets, carrots, cauliflower, string beans, green peas, tomatoes and mushrooms
- Lemon halves or hard-boiled egg white halves
- Hard-boiled egg yolks, chopped
- French dressing

## Directions

1. Cook the salmon as directed.
2. Skin while it is hot and place on a platter to cool down.
3. Cover with rich mayonnaise.
4. Garnish the platter with lettuce leaves.
5. Arrange neatly on the leaves boiled vegetables cut in cubes.
6. Fill cups made of halves of lemon or halves of whites of hard-boiled eggs with mayonnaise or salmon jelly and place around the fish.
7. Sprinkle the fish with hard boiled yolks of egg chopped.
8. Moisten the lettuce leaves and vegetables with French dressing.

title: Kylmää suklaatiputinkia (Cold Chocolate Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce chocolate
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 7 leaves or 1/2 package of gelatine
- 1 cup milk
- 2 cups cream

## Directions

1. Scald the milk with the grated chocolate.
2. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar, add gradually the scalded milk.
3. Beat over the fire until thick.
4. Add the dissolved gelatine, let cool.
5. When the mixture begins to harden (it must be free from lumps) add the whipped cream.
6. Mold.
7. Serve unmolded with whipped cream.

title: Kylmää Vasikanliha-mureketta (Cold Veal à la Daube)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds veal with bones
- 1 pound pork
- salt
- whole peppercorns
- onion
- carrot
- celery
- parsley
- hard boiled eggs
- mayonnaise or tart sauces

## Directions

1. Place veal and pork on fire in cold water with salt, pepper, onion, carrot, celery and parsley
2. Cook until done
3. Remove from bouillon and force through the meat chopper
4. Garnish the bottom of a mold with hard boiled eggs
5. Fill the mold with meat
6. Pour some bouillon over
7. Place in a cold place to set
8. Unmold and cut into slices
9. Serve with mayonnaise or any kind of tart sauces

title: Kyssäkaali (Summer Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- summer squash
- salted water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- milk sauce
- cream

## Directions

1. Peel and remove the seeds
2. Cut in cubes
3. Put on fire in boiling salted water
4. Cook until tender
5. Drain carefully
6. Add butter, salt and pepper, or prepare a thin milk sauce and pour over the squash
7. May be served mashed, seasoned with salt and pepper, diluted with butter and cream

title: Kyssäkaali (Summer Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- summer squash
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut squash into dice
2. Boil until soft
3. Put into a clean cloth
4. Press the water out
5. Season with butter, salt and pepper

title: Kääre-paisti (Roll Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 pounds of steak
- 4 ounces of veal
- 3 ounces of pork
- 5 tablespoonfuls of breadcrumbs
- 1 egg
- 1 cupful of meat stock
- 1/2 onion
- 1 tablespoonful of chopped parsley
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter

## Directions

1. Take a 12-inch long, 7-inch wide, and 1-inch thick piece of steak and pound it well.
2. Sprinkle a little salt and pepper on top.
3. Grind the veal and pork finely.
4. Mix the breadcrumbs with the meat stock and add the onion fried, egg, parsley, salt and pepper.
5. Mix this mixture with the ground meat and knead it well.
6. Spread the filling on the meat, roll it up and tie it with cord.
7. Put butter in a pan, place the meat in it and roast in the oven until brown.
8. Add meat stock or water and roast for 3/4 to 1 hour, basting often.
9. Take the meat on a platter and remove the cord.
10. Make the sauce from the same stock by adding flour, meat stock and cream.

title: Käärepaistia (Boiled Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2.5 pounds steak, 12 inches long, 7 inches wide, 1 inch thick
- 4 ounces veal
- 3 ounces pork
- 5 tablespoons bread crumbs
- 1 egg
- 1 cup meat stock
- 1/2 onion
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- Salt and pepper
- Butter

## Directions

1. Pound the steak well and sprinkle with salt and pepper
2. Grind the veal and pork fine
3. Mix the bread crumbs with the meat stock, add the onion fried, egg, parsley, salt and pepper
4. Add this mixture to the ground meat and beat well
5. Spread the mixture over the steak, roll together and tie with string
6. Melt butter in a dripping pan and place the steak in it
7. Bake in oven until brown
8. Add meat stock or water and continue baking for 3/4 or 1 hour, basting often
