- 13 quarts of water
- 1 yeast cake

## Directions

1. Grate yellow rind of lemons
2. Slice the lemons and put in a jar with sugar, wine acid, ginger and grated yellow lemon rind
3. Pour 1½ quarts of water over it and let stand covered over night
4. Cook the rest of the water and stir it into the above mixture
5. Mix with yeast when cooled and let stand over night
6. Strain and bottle up carefully leaving the yeast on the bottom of the jar
7. Cork the bottles
8. The drink is ready to be served after 8—10 days

title: Scrambled Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of milk or water
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Whip the eggs, add milk, salt and pepper.
2. Melt the butter in a frying pan, not allowing it to brown.
3. Slip the whipped eggs into the pan, stir constantly over a slow fire.
4. When the mixture is a creamy mass remove carefully with a spoon.
5. By using water instead of milk the eggs get a delicate brown color.

title: Seka pai (Mince Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lbs. of lean beef, boiled and chopped
- 1 lb. of suet, chopped
- 2 quarts of apples, chopped
- 1 lb. of cleaned currants
- 2 lbs. of raisins, stones removed
- 1/2 lb. of lemon peel, cut in pieces
- 1 cupful of molasses
- 1 1/2 cups of brown sugar
- 2 lemon juice
- 2 orange juice
- 1/2 teaspoonful of nutmeg
- 1 teaspoonful each of cinnamon, cloves and mixed spices
- 1 nutmeg, chopped
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of brandy
- 1 cupful of sherry wine
- 1 cupful of cider vinegar

## Directions

1. Mix meat and suet
2. Mix dry ingredients and then liquids
3. Let simmer gently
4. Add spices after boiling
5. Pack in a jar for a few days before use
6. If mixture looks too firm, add broth in which the meat was boiled
7. Bake between two crusts in a moderate oven 1 hour

title: Sekalaisia pikkelsiä (Chow-chow)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 quarts cucumbers
- 1 quart green tomatoes
- 1 red pepper
- 1 cauliflower
- 1 pint small onions
- 1 quart string beans
- 1 quart peas
- 1 pint carrots cut in pieces
- 2 quarts vinegar
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon pepper
- a few bay leaves
- mace
- 2 ounces mustard seed
- salt
- 1/2 ounce allspice
- different kinds of mushrooms
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and cut in pieces the vegetables
2. Let stand in salt water for 24 hours
3. Boil vinegar with sugar and spices
4. Add vegetables and cook until tender
5. Arrange the vegetables nicely into jars
6. Add white pepper, mace and bay leaves
7. Strain the liquid, pour over the vegetables
8. Sprinkle the top with mustard seeds
9. Cover as directed

title: Sekapiirakka (Mince Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of lean beef boiled and chopped
- 1 pound suet chopped
- 2 quarts apples cut in pieces
- 1 pound currants
- 2 pounds seeded raisins
- 1/2 pound lemon rind cut in pieces
- 1 cup molasses
- 1 1/2 cups brown sugar
- Juice of 2 lemons
- Juice of 2 oranges
- 1/2 teaspoon mace
- 1 teaspoon each cinnamon, cloves, and allspice
- 1 nutmeg grated
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 1/4 cups brandy
- 1 cup Sherry wine
- 1 cup old cider

## Directions

1. Mix beef with suet.
2. Add the dry ingredients, then the liquid ones.
3. Simmer until the ingredients become tender.
4. Add the spices.
5. Pack into a jar to stand for a few days before using.
6. If too dry, dilute the mixture with beef stock.
7. Bake in a double crust in a moderate oven.

title: Selkä-Paisti (Pork Tenderloin)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tenderloin
- lemon juice
- salt
- ginger
- pepper
- water
- bread crumbs
- apple slices

## Directions

1. Crack the ribs
2. Rub the pork with lemon juice, salt, ginger and pepper
3. Place it in the roaster having the skin side downward
4. Lay slices of apple on the ribs
5. Pour a little water into the roaster and roast in a hot oven
6. Turn over, sprinkle with bread crumbs and continue roasting until brown
7. When the pork is tender, place it on a platter, remove from the bone and cut into thin slices
8. Strain over it the sauce

title: Selkäliha-paisti (Fillet of Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- fillet of beef
- butter
- salt
- slices of lard
- onion slices
- carrots
- turnips
- celery
- 1/2 cup beef bouillon
- 1 bay leaf
- parsley
- mushrooms, 1/2 cup
- cream
- sugar coloring (Kitchen Bouquet) - 1 teaspoon (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the fat carefully so the meat does not tear, along with the surface skin and tendons.
2. If the fillet is thin and flat, tie it with string folded in half.
3. Place thin slices of lard in a buttered pan.
4. Arrange a few slices of onion, carrots, turnips and celery on top of the lard.
5. Place the fillet on top, and arrange a few slices of lard over the meat.
6. Put into a hot oven.
7. When browned, sprinkle salt on top and pour 1/2 cup of beef bouillon into the pan.
8. Add 1 bay leaf and some parsley.
9. Cook 10 minutes per pound and baste often with the drippings in the pan.
10. Place the meat on a platter.
11. Prepare gravy from the drippings by carefully skimming off fat.
12. Add 1/2 cup of mushrooms and simmer 5 minutes.
13. If not sufficiently colored, add 1 teaspoon of sugar coloring (Kitchen Bouquet) and some cream.
14. Pour the gravy over the meat.

title: Selkäliha-paisti (Fillet of Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- Fillet of beef
- Pork slices
- Onion slices
- Carrots
- Turnip
- Celery
- Bouillon
- Bay leaf
- Parsley
- Salt
- Mushrooms
- Kitchen Bouquet
- Cream
- Sherry or Madeira

## Directions

1. Remove the fat carefully and trim off skin and fibres, taking care not to make the meat ragged.
2. If the fillet is thin, fold it once and tie with cord.
3. Lay thin slices of pork in a buttered dripping-pan and put a few slices of onion, carrots, turnip and celery on the pork.
4. Place the fillet on them and a few slices of pork on the fillet.
5. Bake in a hot oven.
6. When browned, sprinkle with salt and pour 1/2 cupful of bouillon into the pan.
7. Add 1 bay leaf and a little parsley.
8. Allow 10 minutes to each pound.
9. Baste often with drippings.
10. Place the meat on a platter and make the sauce of drippings by skimming the fat off carefully.
11. Add 1/2 cupful of mushrooms to the pan and cook 5 minutes.
12. If not brown enough, mix with 1 teaspoonful of Kitchen Bouquet and a little cream.
13. Pour the sauce over the meat and garnish with mushrooms.
14. Instead of mushrooms sherry or madeira may be used.

title: Selkäpaisti (Pork Tenderloin)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork tenderloin with ribs
- lemon juice
- salt
- ginger
- pepper
- apples
- water
- bread crumbs
- egg

## Directions

1. Crack the ribs
2. Rub the meat with lemon juice, salt, ginger and pepper
3. Place in a pan with the skin side down having a few slices of apple on the ribs
4. Roast in a hot oven adding a little water when needed
5. Turn the roast over, sprinkle with bread crumbs, brown in a hot oven
6. When tender, remove from the pan and remove the meat from the backbone
7. Cut in slices and make a gravy of the drippings
8. Strain the gravy over the meat
9. Alternatively, remove the backbone before roasting, spread meat with egg, roll in bread crumbs and roast
10. Or open one side lengthwise, fill with slices of apples, sew up and roast

title: Selleri maitosoosin kanssa (Creamed Celery)
body:
## Ingredients

- celery
- salt
- water
- milk sauce

## Directions

1. Scrape and wash celery.
2. Cut in pieces 1 inch long.
3. Cook in salted water until tender.
4. Drain and mix with milk sauce.

title: Sellerisalaatti (Celery Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- celery stalks
- cold water
- French dressing
- mayonnaise dressing
- lettuce

## Directions

1. Clean and scrape celery stalks, cut in 1/4 inch long pieces.
2. Keep in cold water for a while.
3. Wipe, mix with French or mayonnaise dressing.
4. Serve on a bed of lettuce.

title: Selleriä maitosoosin kanssa (Creamed Celery)
body:
## Ingredients

- Celery stalks
- Salted water
- Milk sauce

## Directions

1. Scrape and rinse stalks of celery
2. Cut in pieces 1 inch long or smaller
3. Cook in salted water until tender
4. Drain and mix with milk sauce

title: Sherbet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of half-frozen lemon ice
- wine to taste
- 1 white of egg

## Directions

1. Mix half-frozen lemon ice with wine to taste
2. Add white of egg
3. Freeze until the mixture is hard
4. Serve as entree

title: Shirred Eggs
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- salt
- cream (optional)
- pepper (optional)
- cheese or chopped tongue (optional)

## Directions

1. Butter the eggshirrers.
2. Break the eggs carefully into them, one in each, sprinkle with a little salt and bake in oven until firm.
3. Before breaking the eggs into the cups a little cream may be put in them.
4. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and when half firm, sprinkle with cheese or chopped tongue.

title: Shrimp-salaatti (Shrimp Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled shrimps
- cucumber
- salt
- pepper
- mayonnaise
- lettuce leaves
- French dressing

## Directions

1. Take equal parts of boiled shrimps cut in pieces and cucumber
2. Season with salt and pepper
3. Mix with mayonnaise
4. Serve on a bed of lettuce
5. Garnish with whole shrimps and cucumber
6. Pour French dressing over

title: Sian Fillet (Fillet of Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork fillet
- Butter
- Onion
- Salt
- Pepper
- Water or cream

## Directions

1. Place the fillet in a pan, fry in butter with an onion.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper.
3. Fry until brown.
4. Add some water or cream to the drippings, pour over the meat.
5. The fillet may be cut in pieces 2 inches thick, pound and fry.

title: Sian Munuais-Paisti eli Satula (Roast Chine of Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4—5 pounds of chine of pork
- lemon
- salt
- pepper
- apples
- boiled prunes
- butter
- bread crumbs
- hot water

## Directions

1. Wipe with a cloth wrung out from hot water
2. Rub with lemon, salt and pepper
3. Fill with pared apples cut in pieces and with boiled stoned prunes
4. Tie with cord
5. Place on a hot buttered pan, sprinkle with bread crumbs and put in a hot oven to brown
6. Pour hot water over it and baste often
7. When done, place back upward on a platter, remove the meat from the backbone, cut lengthwise into slices
8. Replace the slices around the bone
9. Prepare a sauce of the drippings by removing the grease first

title: Sian munuaispaisti eli satula (Roast Chine of Pork)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4-5 pound chine of pork
- lemon
- salt
- pepper
- apples
- prunes
- cord
- butter
- bread crumbs
- hot water
- flour
- Kitchen Bouquet

## Directions

1. Wipe the 4 or 5 pound chine of pork with a cloth wrung out of hot water
2. Rub with lemon, salt and pepper
3. Fill with pared apples cut in pieces and with stewed stoned prunes
4. Roll together and tie with cord
5. Place in a hot buttered pan and sprinkle with bread crumbs
6. Roast in a hot oven
7. Add hot water to the drippings when necessary and baste often
8. When done, place on a platter skin-side up
9. Remove the meat from the backbone and slice crosswise
10. Replace the slices and lay the apples and prunes around
11. Skim off the fat and prepare a gravy
12. Thicken the gravy with flour and color with Kitchen Bouquet

title: Sianpää (Pig's Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig's head
- Pork from various parts
- 4 eggs
- Pepper
- Salt
- Lemon juice
- Slices of carrots
- Truffles
- Cucumbers
- Cloth for wrapping

## Directions

1. Split the head and remove the tongue.
2. Saw the bone leaving the nose whole.
3. Grind all kinds of pork and pound well.
4. Mix with 4 eggs, pepper, salt and lemon juice.
5. Put one half of this mixture into the head.
6. Lay slices of carrots, truffles, cucumbers, etc. on the meat.
7. Fill the head with the remaining mixture.
8. Sew up the head and the eyes.
9. Put a slice of carrot in the ears and fasten them down.
10. Tie the nose with string.
11. Wrap the head in a cloth and cook.
12. Place under a heavy weight.
13. Serve cold with Cumberland sauce.

title: Sieni "muussi" (Mushroom Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- 1 cup of finely chopped mushrooms
- 1 cup of chicken mousse
- lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- butter for molds

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms, put on fire in boiling water.
2. Cook for 10 minutes.
3. Press through a sieve.
4. Mix 1 cup of mushrooms with 1 cup of chicken mousse, some lemon juice, salt and pepper.
5. Butter and garnish small timbale molds, fill with the mixture.
6. Bake in oven in a pan of water covered with buttered paper for 15 minutes or until firm.
7. Serve with mushroom sauce.

title: Sieni Kastike (Mushroom Catsup)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms
- salt
- pepper
- mace
- allspice
- 1 teaspoonful each of pepper, mace and allspice to 2 cupfuls of liquor
- bay leaf
- onion

## Directions

1. Clean fresh mushrooms and cut in pieces
2. Lay in jar and sprinkle with salt between layers
3. Cover and let stand in a cold place 3-4 days, stirring now and then with spoon
4. Cook mushrooms, chop and press the liquor through a sieve
5. Put the liquor on fire
6. Season with pepper, mace and allspice, allowing 1 teaspoonful of each to two cupfuls of liquor
7. Add one bay leaf and a little onion
8. Let boil until it gets thick
9. Strain, let cool down and bottle

title: Sieni Kvenellejä (Mushroom Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled chopped mushrooms
- 2 tablespoonfuls of bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of milk or meat stock
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare like meat quenelles, but instead of meat use boiled chopped mushrooms
2. Serve in mushroom and meat soups

title: Sieni-kvenelliä (Mushroom Quenelles)
body:
## Ingredients

- Boiled chopped mushrooms
- 2 tablespoons melted butter
- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoons milk or bouillon
- 1/2 onion
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare as stated for meat quenelles.
2. Instead of meat use boiled chopped mushrooms.
3. Use in mushroom and meat soups.

title: Sieni-mousi (Mushroom Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- water
- 1 cupful of mushrooms
- 1 cupful of chicken mousse
- sherry wine
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare mushrooms
2. Place on heat in a little boiling water
3. Let simmer 10 minutes
4. Press through a sieve
5. Mix 1 cupful of strained mushrooms, 1 cupful of chicken mousse, a little sherry wine, salt and pepper
6. Butter small timbale molds, decorate and fill with this mixture
7. Bake in oven in a water bath with buttered paper on top for about 15 minutes, or until firm
8. Serve with mushroom sauce

title: Sieni-muhennus (Stewed Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- mushrooms
- salt and pepper
- rich milk
- flour

## Directions

1. Put 2 tablespoonfuls of butter in a frying pan
2. Peel the mushrooms, cut into pieces and fry in butter 5 minutes
3. Take out the mushrooms
4. Prepare a sauce of butter, flour and rich milk in the pan
5. Season with salt and pepper
6. Stir the mushrooms into the sauce, heat thoroughly

title: Sieni-palleroita (Mushroom Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- butter
- milk sauce
- bread crumbs
- egg

## Directions

1. Fry and chop mushrooms
2. Mix fried chopped mushrooms with milk sauce to a moderately thick mixture
3. Form into croquettes
4. Roll in bread crumbs, dip in egg, roll again in bread crumbs
5. Fry in butter

title: Sieni-poletti (Mushroom à la Roulette)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- butter
- beef stock
- salt and pepper
- cream
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms
2. Cook in butter for 5 minutes
3. Add a little beef stock, salt and pepper
4. When ready to serve, add cream and egg yolks

title: Sieni-soppa (Mushroom Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. or 1 small jar of mushrooms
- 2 quarts of veal stock
- 1 small onion
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of tomatoes
- 1 cupful of cream
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the onion in butter for 5 minutes; add the flour, the stock, the mushrooms cleaned and chopped, and the tomatoes.
2. Cook for 10 minutes and force through a sieve.
3. Season with salt and pepper and at last add the cream.
4. Finely chopped split peas, cooked first in water until soft, may be used in the soup.

title: Sienikastike (Mushroom Catsup)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms
- salt
- pepper, 1 teaspoon per 2 cups liquid
- mace, 1 teaspoon per 2 cups liquid
- allspice, 1 teaspoon per 2 cups liquid
- 1 bay leaf
- some onion

## Directions

1. Clean and cut in pieces fresh mushrooms
2. Lay into a bowl, having a sprinkling of salt between the layers
3. Cover and let stand in a cold place for 3 or 4 days, stirring now and then with a spoon
4. Bring to boil the mushrooms, force the liquid through a sieve
5. Put the liquid on the fire, allow to 2 cups liquid, pepper, mace and allspice, 1 teaspoon each, 1 bay leaf and some onion
6. Simmer until thick, strain, cool and bottle up

title: Sienikastike No. 1 (Mushroom Sauce No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1/2 can mushrooms with the liquor
- 2 cups bouillon or fish stock
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Make a gravy of butter, flour, bouillon and the liquor of mushrooms
2. Add small mushrooms whole or cut in pieces
3. Cook the gravy for 10 minutes
4. Season with salt, pepper and lemon juice
5. Some cream and yolk of an egg may be added to the sauce shortly before serving

title: Sienikastike No. 2 (Mushroom Sauce No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart fresh mushrooms
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- 1½ cups cream or milk
- 2 egg yolks
- ½ cup cream

## Directions

1. Clean 1 quart of fresh mushrooms and cut in pieces.
2. Place on the fire to simmer with butter, salt and pepper for 15-20 minutes.
3. Add 1½ cups of cream or milk and bring to boiling.
4. Mix 2 yolks of egg with ½ cup of cream.
5. Add the egg yolk mixture to the sauce and heat.

title: Sienileipiä (Mushroom Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled mushrooms
- hard-boiled egg yolks
- butter
- lemon juice
- parsley
- paprika
- caper
- grated cheese
- buttered toast
- caviare
- pickled cucumbers
- lobster tails

## Directions

1. Mix boiled chopped mushrooms with yolks of hard-boiled eggs, creamed butter, a dash of lemon juice, chopped parsley, paprika, caper and grated cheese.
2. Force through a sieve.
3. Spread on buttered toast.
4. Decorate with caviare, pickled cucumbers and lobster tails.

title: Sienimuhennus (Stewed Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms
- 2 tablespoons butter
- Flour
- Rich milk
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms and cut into pieces
2. Fry with 2 tablespoons butter for 5 minutes
3. Remove the mushrooms from the pan
4. Make a sauce by adding flour and rich milk to the drippings
5. Season with salt and pepper
6. Stir the mushrooms into the sauce and heat thoroughly

title: Sienimureke (Mushroom Farce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 pounds mushrooms
- 10 ounces bread crumbs
- 1/4 pound butter
- 2 small onions
- 2 cups rich milk
- 3 eggs
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms and force through a meat grinder.
2. Add the onions chopped and fried in butter.
3. Add the bread crumbs soaked in milk, seasonings and eggs.
4. Rub the mixture well.
5. Bake in a buttered mold for 1 hour.
6. Serve with melted butter or make a sauce of vegetable stock.

title: Sieniomeletti (Mushroom Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fresh mushrooms or 1 can
- 4 eggs
- 1 onion
- 4 tablespoons cream or milk
- Salt and pepper
- Butter for the pan

## Directions

1. Cut mushrooms fine, fry with onion.
2. Beat the yolks and whites of eggs separately.
3. Mix the yolks with the milk or cream and the mushrooms.
4. Add the whites beaten stiff.
5. Pour in a hot buttered omelet pan.
6. Bake until firm.

title: Sienipalleroita (Mushroom Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Milk sauce
- Bread crumbs
- Egg
- Butter or lard for frying

## Directions

1. Mix fried chopped mushrooms with milk sauce until a moderately thick batter forms
2. Form into croquettes
3. Turn in bread crumbs, dip in egg and turn in bread crumbs again
4. Fry in butter or lard

title: Sienipoletti (Mushrooms à la Poulette)
body:
## Ingredients

- Mushrooms
- Butter
- Bouillon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cream
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms
2. Cook with a lump of butter for 5 minutes
3. Add some bouillon, salt and pepper
4. Shortly before serving, add some cream and 2 yolks of egg

title: Sienisalaatti (Mushroom Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms
- salted water
- 1 quart of vinegar
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- white pepper, 1 teaspoonful
- cinnamon, 1 teaspoonful
- cloves, 1 teaspoonful
- ginger, 1 teaspoonful

## Directions

1. Clean mushrooms and let boil up in salted water
2. Place into jar
3. Boil up vinegar with sugar and spices
4. Drain over the mushrooms when it gets cool
5. Seal the jars and keep in a cold place

title: Sienisalaatti (Pickled Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms
- salt water
- 1 quart vinegar
- 3/4 pound sugar
- white pepper, 1 teaspoon
- cinnamon, 1 teaspoon
- cloves, 1 teaspoon
- ginger, 1 teaspoon

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms
2. Parboil in salt water
3. Lay into jars
4. Bring to boil vinegar with sugar and spices
5. Cool
6. Strain over the mushrooms
7. Cover

title: Sienisoppa (Mushroom Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds fresh or 1 can mushrooms
- 2 quarts white stock
- 1 small red onion
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 tablespoon tomato
- salt and pepper
- 1 cup cream
- green beans (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook the onion in butter for 5 minutes.
2. Add flour, white stock, mushrooms cleaned and chopped, and tomato.
3. Cook for 10 minutes.
4. Force through a colander.
5. Season with salt and pepper.
6. Last of all add the cream.
7. Finely cut green beans, cooked first in salted water until tender, may be used in the soup.

title: Sienivoileipiä (Mushroom Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled chopped mushrooms
- 3 hard boiled eggs
- butter
- lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 1 teaspoon anchovy paste
- paprika
- capers
- grated cheese
- buttered toasted bread slices
- caviar
- cucumber

## Directions

1. Make a mixture of boiled chopped mushrooms, yolks of hard boiled eggs, butter worked to a froth, lemon juice, chopped parsley, paprika, caper and grated cheese.
2. Force the mixture through a sieve.
3. Spread on buttered toast.
4. Garnish with caviar and cucumber.

title: Sieniä munien kanssa (Mushrooms with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound mushrooms
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 onion
- salt and pepper
- 5 eggs
- 5 cups milk

## Directions

1. Clean and chop the mushrooms
2. Brown the chopped onion with butter
3. Add the mushrooms, salt and pepper and fry until brown
4. Whip 5 eggs and add 5 cups of milk and the mushrooms
5. Pour into a buttered mold
6. Bake in oven in a pan of water until firm, about 20 minutes

title: Sieniä perunain kanssa (Mushrooms with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- mashed potatoes
- milk
- 2 eggs
- mushrooms
- butter
- onion
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare mashed potatoes with milk and add 2 eggs
2. Chop the mushrooms
3. Brown mushrooms in butter with onion and season with salt and pepper
4. Put into a buttered mold in alternate layers of potato and mushrooms
5. Bake in oven to a light brown
6. Serve with melted butter or brown sauce

title: Sieniä perunain kanssa (Mushrooms with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- perunoita
- maitoa
- 2 munaa
- sieniä
- sipulia
- voita
- suolaa
- pippuria

## Directions

1. Valmistetaan perunasosetta maidoin, johon lisätään 2 munaa.
2. Siemet hienonnetaan ja paistetaan voissa sipulin kanssa, maustetaan suolalla ja pippurilla.
3. Voideltuun vormuun pannaan kerroksittain perunasosetta ja sieniä ja paistetaan uunissa vaaleanruskeaksi.
4. Syödään voisulan kera.

title: Siirappi Kakku (Molasses Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of brown sugar
- 1 cupful of molasses
- 1 cupful of boiling water
- 1/4 cupful of butter
- 1/4 cupful of lard
- 1 heaping cupful of flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 heaping teaspoonful of soda
- 1/4 teaspoonful of cinnamon
- 1/4 teaspoonful of ginger
- 1/4 teaspoonful of mace

## Directions

1. Beat well together molasses, sugar and water
2. Add melted butter and lard
3. Add beaten eggs, spices, soda and flour
4. Bake in a moderately hot oven

title: Siirappi Kakkuja (Molasses Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 1/2 cupfuls of molasses
- 3/4 cupful of cream
- 9 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1/2 lb. of fine sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of ground cinnamon
- 1/2 tablespoonful of cardamom
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 tablespoonful of brandy
- 4 eggs
- 5 1/2 lbs. of flour

## Directions

1. Molasses, cream, butter, sugar and spices are brought to a boil.
2. Flour is sifted.
3. Half of the flour is placed in a bowl and the cooled molasses mixture is gradually mixed into it.
4. Eggs are beaten, brandy and soda with the remaining flour are added and well mixed.
5. Batter is allowed to stand until the next day.
6. Roll thin and shape into small cookies with a cutter.
7. Bake in a moderate oven.

title: Siirappi Leipiä (Molasses Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of molasses
- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1/2 cupful of butter
- 1 cupful of lard
- 1 cupful of cold coffee
- 3 teaspoonfuls of ginger
- 3 teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- 10 cupfuls of flour
- a dash of salt

## Directions

1. Warm molasses, add lard, butter and sugar, rubbing well.
2. Mix with coffee, spices and flour.
3. Roll into a thin sheet and cut with a round cutter into small cakes.
4. Bake in a quick oven.

title: Silli keitettynä munien kanssa (Herring Cooked with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 salted herrings
- water
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Soak 4 salted herrings, split, skin and bone.
2. Pour boiling water over and let simmer for a little while.
3. Chop 3 hard-boiled eggs.
4. Arrange the fish in layers with the eggs on a serving-dish and pour browned butter over.

title: Silli keitettynä munien kanssa (Herring with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 salt herrings
- 3 hard boiled eggs
- Butter sauce
- Potatoes

## Directions

1. Soak the herrings, skin and bone.
2. Cover with boiling water, let boil slowly for a few minutes.
3. If very salty, place the fish on a fire in cold water.
4. Chop the hard boiled eggs.
5. Arrange the fish on a platter alternately with layers of egg.
6. Serve with hot potatoes.
7. The fish may be covered with thin milk sauce flavored with onion juice.

title: Silli pallerot (Herring Patties)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 herrings
- 1 cup of finely chopped potatoes
- 2 eggs
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of breadcrumbs
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cream

## Directions

1. Soak the herrings, skin and bone.
2. Grind in a meat grinder.
3. Chop the onion and fry in butter.
4. Soak the breadcrumbs in cream, to which add beaten eggs.
5. Mix the fried onion, and egg and bread mixture with the herring.
6. Shape into cakes or patties, dip in egg and breadcrumbs and fry in lard.
7. Serve with a sharp sauce.

title: Sillilaatikko (Herring Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 or 3 salted herrings
- 3 cups bread crumbs
- 2 cups milk
- 1/2 cup melted butter
- pepper
- sugar if desired
- 3 egg yolks beaten to a froth
- 3 egg whites beaten until firm

## Directions

1. Soak over night 2 or 3 salted herrings
2. Remove the skin and the bones
3. Force through a meat grinder
4. Soak 3 cups bread crumbs in 2 cups milk or use stale bread instead of crumbs
5. Mix in the fish
6. Add to the mixture 1/2 cup melted butter, a dash of pepper and sugar if desired and 3 egg yolks beaten to a froth
7. Last of all fold in 3 egg whites beaten until firm
8. Steam in a buttered mold in oven for 3/4 or 1 hour
9. Serve with egg sauce

title: Sillilaatikko (Molded Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 herrings
- 1 cup bread crumbs
- 2 cups rich milk
- ½ cup butter
- ½ onion
- 3 eggs
- Some pepper and sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the fish, remove the bones and skin, force through a meat chopper
2. Cook the chopped onion in butter, add bread crumbs, milk and eggs beaten
3. Stir gradually into the fish, season
4. Put in a buttered mold, bake in oven in a pan of water

title: Sillilaatikko perunain kanssa (Molded Herring with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 herrings
- 4 cups boiled or raw potatoes
- ½ onion
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 3 cups milk
- 3 eggs
- Chopped parsley and pepper

## Directions

1. Soak the fish, skin and bone
2. Spread a mold with butter
3. Fill alternately with layers of potatoes and herring having potatoes on the bottom and on the top of the dish
4. Place the chopped onion on the layers of fish and pour melted butter over the top
5. Beat the eggs, add milk, pepper and parsley, pour into the mold
6. Sprinkle with bread crumbs
7. Bake

title: Sillipalleroita (Herring Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups boiled mashed potatoes
- 2 salted, skinned and boned herrings
- 1 onion, chopped and fried in butter
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1/2 cup bread crumbs
- pepper
- 2 or 3 beaten eggs
- cream if necessary

## Directions

1. Have 3 cups boiled mashed potatoes
2. Chop 2 salted, skinned and boned herrings and add to the potatoes
3. Mix in 1 onion chopped and fried in butter, 3 tablespoons butter, 1/2 cup bread crumbs and a dash of pepper
4. Last of all add to the mixture 2 or 3 beaten eggs
5. Dilute with cream if necessary
6. Form into croquettes
7. Fry or cook in deep fat

title: Sillipallerot (Herring Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 salt herring or 2 fresh ones
- 1 cup chopped potato
- 2 eggs
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 tablespoons bread crumbs
- 2 tablespoons cream

## Directions

1. Soak the fish, skin and bone, force through a meat chopper.
2. Chop the onion, fry in butter.
3. Soak the bread crumbs in cream, add the beaten eggs.
4. Mix the herring with onion, eggs and bread crumbs.
5. Form into cakes or balls, dip in egg and bread crumbs, or roll in flour only.
6. Fry in butter or lard.
7. Serve with any tart sauce.

title: Sillisalaatti (Finnish Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 to 1½ salt herrings
- 2 cupfuls of boiled or roast meat
- 2 cupfuls of boiled potatoes
- 2 cupfuls of boiled carrots
- 2½ cupfuls of boiled beets
- 2 pickled cucumbers
- White pepper
- French dressing
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Lettuce leaves
- 2 apples (optional)

## Directions

1. Soak fish in water for a few hours
2. Bone, skin and chop the fish
3. Cut vegetables, pickles and meat in small pieces
4. Mix all well together and pour French dressing on
5. Place in bowl
6. Garnish the surface with hard-boiled eggs and beets
7. Arrange lettuce leaves around
8. The salad may be mixed also with two apples, cut in small pieces

title: Sillisalaatti (Herring Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ salted herrings
- 2 cups boiled potatoes
- 2 cups boiled carrots
- 2½ cups boiled beets
- 2 dill pickles
- salt
- white pepper
- 2 hard boiled eggs
- 2 cups boiled or fried meat
- lettuce leaves
- French dressing or cream dressing

## Directions

1. Soak the herrings in water for a few hours
2. Cut herrings into small pieces
3. Cut vegetables and pickle into small pieces
4. Mix all ingredients together well
5. Put on a platter
6. Garnish the top with hard boiled eggs and beets
7. Garnish around with lettuce leaves
8. Serve with French or cream dressing

title: Sillisosetta (Herring Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 herrings
- 2 hard boiled eggs
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- 4 tablespoons cream
- 1 tablespoon butter
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Soak the fish, skin and bone.
2. Chop the fish.
3. Chop the onion, fry in butter.
4. Add the fish, lemon juice, cream and seasonings.
5. Let boil quickly.
6. Add the chopped eggs.
7. Place on a platter.
8. Serve hot or cold covered with mayonnaise.

title: Sillisosetta (Herring Mousse)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 herrings
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon juice
- 4 tablespoonfuls of cream
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Soak the herring, skin and bone.
2. Chop the herring.
3. Cook the chopped onion in butter, add the herring into it.
4. Mix with lemon juice, cream and seasonings.
5. Let quickly boil.
6. At the last mix with chopped eggs.
7. Arrange on a serving-dish.
8. Serve hot or cold covered with Mayonnaise sauce.

title: Silliä munien kanssa (Salted Herring with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 salted herrings
- 1 onion
- pepper
- 2 hard boiled eggs
- cream
- beets
- chives

## Directions

1. Soak 2 salted herrings over night
2. Clean and remove the skin and bones
3. Chop fine with a knife
4. Add 1 chopped onion, some pepper and 2 hard boiled eggs chopped
5. Dilute with cream
6. Place on a serving dish
7. Garnish with hard boiled eggs, beets and chives

title: Simpukka Bouillon (Clam Bouillon)
body:
## Ingredients

- clams
- hot water
- cream

## Directions

1. Wash the clams well with brush, changing water.
2. Put into the kettle with a small quantity of hot water.
3. Cook on a slow fire, covered, until the shells open.
4. Strain the bouillon through a sieve-cloth and put aside to cool and clear.
5. Serve in cups as it is or mix with a little scalded cream.
6. It may be served also mixed with clarified rich meat-stock or chicken stock, 2 cupfuls of clam bouillon allowed to 1 cupful of meat stock.

title: Simpukka cocktaili (Clam Cocktail)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- ½ teaspoon vinegar
- 1 teaspoon tomato catsup
- 1 teaspoon Worcestershire sauce
- 1 teaspoon grated horseradish
- Salt
- A few drops of tobasco
- Clams

## Directions

1. Make a dressing of lemon juice, vinegar, tomato catsup, Worcestershire sauce, grated horseradish, salt and a few drops of tobasco.
2. Wash the clams.
3. Mix with the dressing.
4. Keep on ice until served.

title: Simpukka Cocktaili (Clam Cocktail)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoonful of lemon juice
- 1/2 teaspoonful of vinegar
- 1 teaspoonful of tomato catsup
- 1 teaspoonful of Worcestershire sauce
- 1 teaspoonful of grated horseradish
- salt
- Tabasco sauce
- clams
- ice

## Directions

1. Prepare sauce with lemon juice, vinegar, tomato catsup, Worcestershire sauce, grated horseradish, salt and a few drops of Tabasco
2. Wash clams
3. Mix clams into the sauce
4. Set on ice until serving

title: Simpukka Muhennos (Stewed Clams)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint clams
- 1 large tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 3/4 cup clam liquor
- 1 cup heated cream
- Pinch of baking soda

## Directions

1. Take 1 pint clams and separate the soft part from the hard part
2. Chop the hard part finely
3. Heat a large tablespoon of butter with the chopped clams
4. Add 1 tablespoon flour
5. Add 3/4 cup clam liquor and boil for a few minutes
6. Add the soft part of the clams
7. Add 1 cup heated cream with a pinch of baking soda stirred in
8. Let boil for a couple of minutes
9. Just before serving, gradually stir in two beaten egg yolks

title: Simpukka muhennos (Stewed Clams)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint clams
- 1 heaping tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1/2 cup clam liquor
- 1 cup scalded cream
- a pinch of soda
- 2 egg yolks
- some cream
- salt
- pepper
- buttered toast

## Directions

1. Remove the hard parts of the clams and chop fine
2. Cook the heaping tablespoon of butter with the chopped clams
3. Add the tablespoon of flour and 1/2 cup of clam liquor
4. Continue cooking for a few minutes
5. Mix the cup of scalded cream with the soft parts of clams
6. Add a pinch of soda to the cream mixture
7. Boil for 2 minutes
8. Shortly before serving, beat 2 egg yolks with some cream
9. Add the beaten egg yolks gradually to the mixture
10. Season with salt and pepper
11. Serve on buttered toast

title: Simpukka mykyjä (Clam Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cup milk
- 1 1/2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- salt
- lemon juice
- 1 pint clams
- pepper
- hot lard
- lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Make a batter of 2 eggs, 1/2 cup of milk, 1 1/2 cups of flour, 1 teaspoon of baking powder, salt and lemon juice
2. Chop the 1 pint of clams fine
3. Season the clams with salt and pepper
4. Add the chopped clams to the batter
5. Drop the batter by spoonfuls into boiling lard, cooking 3 or 4 at a time
6. When brown, drain on gray paper
7. Serve on a folded napkin garnished with lemon and parsley

title: Simpukka-buljonki (Clam Bouillon)
body:
## Ingredients

- Clams
- Water
- Cream

## Directions

1. Wash the clams well with a brush changing water.
2. Put into a kettle with a small quantity of hot water.
3. Cook on a slow fire covered until the shells open.
4. Strain the bouillon through a cheese cloth, put aside to cool and clarify.
5. Serve in cups as it is or mixed with some scalded cream.
6. May be served mixed with clarified rich meat stock or chicken stock, allowing 2 cups of clam bouillon to 1 cup of meat stock.

title: Simpukka-muhennus (Stewed Clams)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of clams
- butter
- flour
- clam liquor
- scalded cream
- soda
- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- buttered toast

## Directions

1. Have 1 pint of clams, remove the hard parts and chop them fine
2. Cook one heaping tablespoonful of butter with the chopped clams
3. Add one tablespoonful of flour and 1/2 cupful of clam liquor
4. Cook for a few minutes
5. Mix with soft parts of clams and 1 cupful of scalded cream mixed with a pinch of soda
6. Let cook for 2 minutes
7. Pour gradually in it beaten yolks of 2 eggs mixed with a little cream
8. Season with salt and pepper
9. Serve immediately on buttered toast

title: Simpukka-mykyjä (Clam Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 cupful of milk
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of flour
- 1 teaspoonful of baking powder
- lemon juice
- 1 pint of clams
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Make batter of 2 eggs, 1/2 cupful of milk, 1 1/2 cupfuls of flour, 1 teaspoonful of baking powder and a little lemon juice
2. Have one pint of clams and chop them fine
3. Season with salt and pepper and add the batter into the mixture
4. Drop by spoonfuls into boiling lard, cooking 3 or 4 at a time
5. When browned drain on brown paper
6. Serve on a folded napkin

title: Simpukka-soppa (Clam Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of clams
- 1 cupful of milk
- 1 cupful of cream
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 2 egg yolks
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the clams finely and put over gentle heat in their own liquid and a little water or fish stock added.
2. Simmer gently for about an hour.
3. Thicken with butter and flour.
4. Beat the egg yolks and gradually add the scalded milk, to which a little soda has been added.
5. Remove the soup from heat and gradually pour in the egg and milk mixture.

title: Simpukka-vatkuli (Clam Chowder)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints of fish stock or water
- 1 quart of clams
- 1 middle-sized onion
- 6 ozs. of salted pork
- 1 potato
- 1 carrot
- 1 tomato
- 1 teaspoonful of chopped parsley
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of pepper
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 2 cupfuls of milk or cream
- mace
- crackers

## Directions

1. Place clams on fire, in their own liquor, let boil for a few minutes.
2. Cut pork into cubes, and fry with chopped onion until brown.
3. Mix with flour and pour clam liquor and fish stock gently in it.
4. Put potatoes, carrot and tomato cut in cubes in it.
5. Boil until the vegetables are soft.
6. Add clams cut in pieces, parsley, crackers and milk into it.
7. Bring to the boiling point and send to the table.

title: Simpukkakeitto (Clam Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart clams
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup cream
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 egg yolks
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the clams fine, put on the fire in their own liquor diluted with some water or fish stock.
2. Simmer for 1 hour.
3. Thicken with butter and flour.
4. Beat the yolks, add slowly the scalded milk.
5. Remove the soup from the fire, add some soda and pour slowly the milk into the soup.

title: Simpukkakeitto (Clam Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart clams
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup cream
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 egg yolks
- Salt and pepper
- Water or fish stock

## Directions

1. Chop the clams finely and put on a gentle fire in their own liquid, with a small amount of water or fish stock added.
2. Simmer gently for about one hour.
3. Thicken with butter and flour.
4. Beat the egg yolks, add the gradually heated milk.

title: Simpukkamuhennus (Scallop Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- Scallops
- 1 cup milk
- 1 cup cream
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons flour

## Directions

1. Prepare same as oyster stew by using scallops instead of oysters.
2. Boil the scallops for 2 minutes.

title: Simpukkavatkiili (Clam Chowder)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pints fish stock or water
- 1 quart clams
- 1 middle sized onion
- 6 ozs. salt pork
- 1 potato
- 1 carrot
- 1 tomato
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon pepper
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 cups milk or cream
- Mace
- Crackers

## Directions

1. Place clams on fire in their own liquor, let boil a few minutes.
2. Cut the pork in cubes, fry with chopped onion.
3. Stir flour in it and add the liquor and the fish stock slowly in the pan.
4. Put potato, carrot and tomato cut in cubes in the mixture, boil until they are tender.
5. Add the clams cut in pieces, seasonings, parsley, crackers and the milk.
6. Boil quickly and serve.

title: Simpukoita sienien kanssa (Clams with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 onion
- butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 pint clams
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- 3 egg yolks
- water
- 10 medium-sized mushrooms

## Directions

1. Fry 1 chopped onion in butter
2. Add 1 tablespoon of flour and 1 pint of clams chopped
3. Season with salt, pepper and some mustard
4. Cook for 20 or 30 minutes
5. Remove from fire and add 3 beaten egg yolks
6. Add some water if desired
7. Chop and fry 10 medium-sized mushrooms and add to the mixture
8. Shake over the fire until firm
9. Serve, optionally garnished with whole mushrooms around the edges

title: Sinappia Pöytää Varten (Mustard for Table Use)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons fine mustard
- 4 tablespoons vinegar
- 2 teaspoons sugar
- a pinch of salt
- 1 tablespoon olive oil
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Mix well the mustard and vinegar
2. Add the sugar, salt and olive oil
3. Mix until smooth
4. If too thick, dilute with some boiling water

title: Sipuli au Gratin (Onions au Gratin)
body:
## Ingredients

- onions
- salt
- water
- butter
- milk sauce
- bread crumbs
- cream
- milk
- tomato slices (optional)

## Directions

1. Peel and cook onions in salted water until tender
2. Drain carefully and lay in a buttered mold
3. Cover with milk sauce
4. Sprinkle with bread crumbs mixed with butter; bake until brown on top
5. Alternatively, lay onions in a mold alternately with bread crumbs, having a layer of bread crumbs on top
6. Sprinkle the layers with salt and dot with butter
7. Fill the mold with rich milk
8. Bake for 1/2 hour
9. Slices of tomato may be laid on top of the bread crumbs, but no milk should be used then

title: Sipuli-keitto (Onion Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 lb. of onions
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 2 quarts of meat stock
- 3 potatoes
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cupful of cream or milk
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Peel the onions and cook them in water about 10 minutes. Drain well.
2. Cut in pieces and fry in butter on a slow fire until tender; add the flour stirring thoroughly.
3. Mix with the boiling meat stock and cooked and mashed potatoes. Let boil 10 minutes.
4. Pass through a sieve; season with salt and pepper.
5. Whip the yolks and cream, pour it slowly into the soup whipping well; sprinkle with finely chopped parsley.
6. Instead of potatoes tapioca may be used. Before using the tapioca has to be cooked in a small quantity of water until tender.

title: Sipuli-keitto (Onion Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of onions
- 3 quarts of vegetable stock or water
- 2 stalks or 1 root of celery
- 1 small parsnip
- 4 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 3 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 cupfuls of cream
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cut vegetables and onions into pieces and brown in butter
2. Add flour and scalded vegetable stock and let cook for an hour
3. Force through a sieve and season with salt and pepper
4. Whip egg yolks, add cream and pour the soup gradually into it
5. Stir on the fire until hot
6. Thicken with fried bread dice boiled in soup

title: Sipulikeitto (Onion Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- more onions than in potato soup
- less potatoes than in potato soup
- vegetable stock or meat stock
- several kinds of vegetables

## Directions

1. Prepare as stated for potato soup by using more onions and less potatoes.
2. May be made of vegetable stock or having several kinds of vegetables cooked with the onion.

title: Sitruuna täyte (Lemon Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 4 eggs
- 1 cupful of sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 2 tablespoonfuls of corn starch
- a little water

## Directions

1. Rub yolks with sugar in a kettle
2. Add lemon juice, water and corn starch
3. Thicken on the fire

title: Sitruuna-kermaa (Lemon Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 3/4 cup sugar
- Grated rind of 1 orange
- Juice of 2 lemons
- 1/4 package of gelatine
- 2 cups cream

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks with the sugar in a saucepan
2. Add all the ingredients except the cream
3. Place on the fire and beat until thick
4. Let cool beating continuously
5. Add the whipped cream
6. Put into a mold to set
7. Serve with fruit sauce or cream

title: Sitruuna-laserrus (Lemon Icing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup confectioner's sugar
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- 1 tablespoon water
- grated lemon rind

## Directions

1. Have 1 cup confectioner's sugar
2. Mix in slowly 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 tablespoon water and some grated lemon rind
3. More water and less lemon juice may be used, or other flavoring may be substituted for lemon

title: Sitruuna-Vesijäätelö (Lemon Ice)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups water
- 2 cups sugar
- 4-5 lemons

## Directions

1. Cook sugar and water for 15 minutes
2. Let cool
3. Press juice from lemons into sugar water
4. Strain and freeze

title: Sitruunahyydyke (Lemon Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 tablespoons lemon juice
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 1/2 cups boiling water
- 1 1/2 tablespoons gelatine
- 1/2 cup cold water
- Lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Dissolve the gelatine in 1/2 cup of cold water.
2. Add 1 1/2 cups of boiling water, sugar and lemon juice.
3. Stir until the sugar is dissolved.
4. Strain through a cloth.
5. Pour into a mold to set.
6. Serve with lettuce.

title: Sitruunahyydyke (Lemon Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart water
- Juice of 3 lemons
- Grated yellow rind of 3 lemons
- 3 tablespoons cornstarch
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 cup sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the water with lemon rind and juice for a few minutes
2. Thicken with cornstarch
3. Beat egg yolks with sugar
4. Add the beaten yolks to the mixture
5. Stir over fire, not allowing to boil
6. Optional: fold in 2 egg whites beaten stiff after removing from fire

title: Sitruunahyytelö (Lemon Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups water
- 1 cup sugar
- 4 lemons
- 2 tablespoons or 1/2 package gelatine
- Whites and shells of 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan water, the grated yellow rind and the juice of the lemons, sugar, gelatine, egg whites beaten frothy and the shells.
2. Place on the fire, stir until coming to boil.
3. Simmer for 5 minutes.
4. Strain through a cloth wrung out of hot water.
5. Bring the unclear first liquor back into the straining cloth.
6. If not clarified jelly desired, prepare as follows: Dissolve gelatine in 1/2 cup water, add the remaining water, juice, sugar and the yellow rind of 1 lemon.
7. Stir until the gelatine is dissolved.
8. Remove from the fire, add lemon juice, strain and mold.

title: Sitruunahyytelö (Lemon Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cupfuls of water
- 1 cupful of sugar
- 4 lemons
- 1½ ozs. of gelatine (½ box)
- 2 whites of eggs and shells

## Directions

1. Put water, grated yellow lemon rind, lemon juice, sugar, gelatine, half beaten whites of eggs and shells in a saucepan.
2. Place the saucepan on the fire and stir until it begins to boil.
3. Simmer 5 minutes.
4. Strain through a cloth, wrung out of hot water.
5. Bring the unclear first juice back into the draining-cloth.
6. If you do not want to clarify the jelly, dissolve gelatine in ½ cupful of water.
7. Put the rest of the water with sugar and few thin slices of lemon rind on the fire.
8. Stir until the sugar has dissolved.
9. Mix with gelatine stirring until the gelatine has dissolved.
10. Remove from fire, add lemon juice, drain, pour into a mold to get firm.

title: Sitruunakastike (Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 yolks of egg
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 2 cups milk or cream
- salt and pepper
- lemon juice
- chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Beat 3 yolks of egg in a saucepan
2. Add 3 tablespoons of butter and 2 cups of milk or cream
3. Continue to beat until thick
4. Season with salt, pepper, lemon juice and chopped parsley
5. Serve with fish

title: Sitruunakastike No. 1 (Lemon Sauce No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1/4 cup lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1 cup water
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch

## Directions

1. Mix the sugar with the cornstarch
2. Add gradually the boiling water, stirring well
3. Cook until clear
4. Remove from the fire, add the lemon juice and butter

title: Sitruunakastike No. 2 (Lemon Sauce No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- juice and yellow rind of 1 lemon
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup water
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 cups cream
- Sherry wine

## Directions

1. Rub the butter with the sugar until creamy
2. Add the beaten egg yolks and the water
3. Stir in a double boiler over the fire until thick
4. Remove from the fire, add the juice and grated yellow rind of a lemon and some Sherry
5. When cool, mix with whipped cream

title: Sitruunakohokas (Lemon Souffle)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 1 lemon
- 1 tablespoon bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Whip the yolks with the sugar, add the yellow rind and the juice of the lemon and the bread crumbs.
2. Beat the egg whites until firm, add to the yolks.
3. Bake in a well-buttered mold for 20-30 minutes, having the mold in a pan of water.

title: Sitruunakohokas (Lemon Souffle)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lemon
- 1 tablespoonful of bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Beat yolks and sugar to a froth.
2. Stir grated yellow lemon rind, juice and bread crumbs into it.
3. Lastly add the stiff beaten whites.
4. Pour the mixture in a well buttered individual mold.
5. Steam in oven about 20-30 minutes.
6. Serve hot in the mold.

title: Sitruunakohokas (Lemon Souffle)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 1 lemon
- 1 tablespoonful of bread flour

## Directions

1. Whip the yolks and sugar to froth.
2. Add the yellow rind and juice of lemon and bread flour.
3. Whip whites to froth and mix in lastly.
4. Pour the mixture in a well buttered souffle mold.
5. Bake in oven in a water bath nearly 20—30 min.
6. Serve hot in its own dish.

title: Sitruunapiirakka No. 1 (Lemon Pie No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1½ lemon juice and yellow rind
- 4 tablespoons water
- ¾ cup sugar
- pastry for one open crust
- meringue made from 3 egg whites, 6 tablespoons sugar, ½ teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat the yolks with sugar, add lemon juice, rind and water.
2. Stir on the fire in a double boiler until thick; let cool.
3. Add the beaten whites.
4. Bake on an open crust.
5. When done, coat with meringue, if desired; brown the meringue in oven.

title: Sitruunapiirakka No. 2 (Lemon Pie No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup water
- 1 cup sugar
- 3 eggs
- ¼ cup lemon juice and the yellow rind of 1 lemon grated
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 3 or 4 tablespoons cornstarch or flour
- pastry for one open crust
- meringue

## Directions

1. Put on the fire the water with lemon juice, rind and butter.
2. Mix the sugar with the flour, pour gradually the boiling water into the flour, cook for a few minutes.
3. Add the yolks, turn the mixture on an open pie crust.
4. Bake until firm.
5. Cover with meringue, continue baking until brown.

title: Sitruunapulveri-limonaati (Lemonade Powder)
body:
## Ingredients

- Yellow rind of 3 lemons
- 2.5 pounds sugar
- lemon juice
- 1 teaspoon wine acid powder

## Directions

1. Grate the yellow rind of 3 lemons
2. Rub together with 2.5 pounds sugar
3. Press the juice of the lemons and strain it
4. Add the strained juice and 1 teaspoon wine acid powder to the sugar
5. Mix thoroughly together
6. Spread on paper to dry
7. Keep in a covered glass jar
8. Allow 2 teaspoons powder to 1 glass of ice water when serving

title: Sitruunaputinki (Lemon Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 cups cream
- 6 eggs
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 1 lemon
- 2 tablespoons potato flour
- 1/2 pound sugar for caramel
- 3/4 cup hot water
- Lemon juice
- Lemon or fruit sauce

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar in a saucepan.
2. Add the yellow rind and the juice of a lemon, cream and flour.
3. Place the saucepan on the fire and cook beating well until thick.
4. Let cool, stirring now and then.
5. Beat egg whites until frothy.
6. Add to the mixture.
7. Prepare caramel by dissolving 1/2 pound sugar in 3/4 cup hot water with lemon juice.
8. Coat a mold with caramel and fill with the mixture.
9. Bake in oven in a pan of water for 3/4 hour.
10. Serve hot with lemon or fruit sauce.

title: Sitruunarinkilöitä (Lemon Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 5 tablespoons sugar
- 3 tablespoons melted butter
- 2 tablespoons cream
- Grated yellow rind of 1 lemon
- Flour enough to make a paste

## Directions

1. Beat yolks with sugar
2. Add cream, butter, lemon rind and flour to make a paste thick enough to handle
3. Form into small rings
4. Bake on a buttered baking sheet until light brown

title: Sitruunarinkilöitä (Lemon Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 3 ozs. of sugar
- 2 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of cream
- grated lemon rind
- wheat flour as needed

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks and sugar to a froth
2. Add cream, butter, and lemon rind
3. Add flour enough to make a dough easy to handle
4. Form into small rings
5. Bake on a buttered baking sheet until light brown

title: Skotlantilainen Inkiväärijuoma (Scotch Ginger Ale)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds granulated sugar
- 2 lemons
- 1 ounce wine acid
- 1.5 ounces powdered ginger
- 10 quarts water
- 1 yeast cake

## Directions

1. Grate the yellow rind of the lemons
2. Cut the lemons in slices
3. Put into a covered bowl with the sugar, wine acid, ginger and the yellow rind
4. Pour 1.5 quarts boiling water over
5. Cover and let stand over night
6. Bring to boil the remaining water and add to the mixture in the bowl
7. When cool, mix in the yeast
8. Cover and let stand over night
9. Strain carefully leaving the yeast on the bottom of the bowl
10. Bottle up and seal
11. The drink is ready to be used after 8 or 10 days

title: Small Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of sugar
- 6 eggs
- 1 1/2 lbs. of flour

## Directions

1. Wash butter and work to a cream with sugar
2. Add slowly eggs into it, whipping well
3. Lastly mix with flour
4. Force through a pastry bag on baking sheet cookies in shape of S
5. Bake about 10 minutes in a moderate oven

title: Snow Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of lemon or wine jelly
- 3 whites of eggs
- whipped cream or custard for serving

## Directions

1. Prepare lemon or wine jelly and let cool.
2. Before it is firm, whip it to a froth.
3. Beat whites of eggs until stiff and add to the jelly.
4. Continue beating until it is stiff enough to hold its shape.
5. Put in a mold or drop with spoon into a glass dish.
6. Serve with whipped cream or custard.

title: Sokerikakkuja (Sugar Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup butter
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 2 eggs
- 4 tablespoons milk or cream
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 cups flour

## Directions

1. Rub until creamy the butter with the sugar
2. Add beaten eggs, milk, flavoring, flour mixed with baking powder
3. Use flour to make thick enough batter
4. Roll out
5. Cut in small cookies with a cutter
6. Sprinkle with sugar and chopped almonds
7. Bake in a hot oven

title: Sokerilankaa (Spun Sugar)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 1 cup water
- 1½ teaspoons cream of tartar
- a smooth stick

## Directions

1. Put into a saucepan 2 cups sugar, 1 cup water and 1½ teaspoons cream of tartar.
2. Place over the fire and stir until the sugar is dissolved.
3. Boil without stirring until the sugar becomes firm when dipped in cold water and cracks when broken.
4. Place the pan in cold water to stop the boiling.
5. Dip two forks into the syrup and spin the sugar from them by shaking them rapidly over a smooth stick.
6. Remove the spun sugar carefully from the stick and use to garnish desserts.
7. Keeps best when covered with a bowl.

title: Sokerileipä (Sugar Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 cups flour
- 1 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat until firm egg whites
2. Beat yolks with sugar
3. Add to the yolks vanilla and flour
4. Fold in the whites last of all
5. Drop by the spoonful on a buttered baking sheet
6. Sprinkle lightly with sugar
7. Bake in a slow oven until light brown

title: Sokerimunkkia (Sugar Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cups water
- 1/2 pound butter
- 3/4 pound flour
- 6 eggs
- sugar for sprinkling
- whipped cream
- vanilla
- jam

## Directions

1. Boil 4 cups water
2. Add 1/2 pound butter
3. Reheat to boiling point
4. Add 3/4 pound flour
5. Cook beating the mixture until it leaves the edges of the saucepan
6. Pour into a bowl to cool
7. Mix in 6 eggs, 2 at a time, beating well
8. Form into small round cakes on a baking sheet
9. Bake until light brown
10. Sprinkle with sugar when done
11. May be filled when cool with whipped cream mixed with sugar, vanilla and jam

title: Sokeripytkyjä (Lady Fingers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1/2 cup flour
- 1/4 teaspoon vanilla
- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- Salt

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks to a firm froth
2. Add sifted sugar and flavoring, continue to beat for a while
3. Beat egg whites until firm
4. Add gradually sifted flour to whites
5. Combine the mixtures
6. Force through a pastry bag on a baking sheet lined with paper, forming cookies 1 inch wide and 4 inches long
7. Sprinkle with powdered sugar
8. Bake in a slow oven so as not to brown the top
9. May spread with egg white and put two together if preferred, or drop by spoonful on baking sheet

title: Sokeriväiriä (Kitchen Bouquet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping tablespoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon water

## Directions

1. Cook sugar and water together on a quick fire until dark brown
2. Test it by cooling some of it
3. If it becomes firm, add some water, continue to boil
4. Bottle up
5. Use to color soups and sauces with

title: Sokeriväriä (Kitchen Bouquet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping tablespoonful sugar
- 1 tablespoonful water

## Directions

1. Have a heaping tablespoonful of sugar and 1 tablespoonful of water
2. Boil on a quick fire until dark brown syrup
3. Test it by cooling a little of it
4. If it gets firm, add a small quantity of water and return over the fire
5. Bottle and use to color soups and sauces

title: Sokeroidut appelsiininkuoret (Candied Orange Peel)
body:
## Ingredients

- orange peels
- equal weight of sugar
- water, 1 cupful per pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Soak orange peels in cold water for 6 or 7 days, changing the water every day
2. Cook until tender
3. Drain
4. Have equal weights of orange peels and sugar, and allow 1 cupful of water to 1 pound of sugar
5. Cook the sugar in water to a thick syrup
6. Add to the syrup the orange peels and cook until the liquid has evaporated
7. Use for puddings and to garnish cakes with

title: Sokeroittuja Karpaloita (Sugared Cranberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- powdered sugar
- white of an egg
- cleaned dried cranberries

## Directions

1. Warm sifted powdered sugar on paper or in a pan
2. Beat the white of an egg until firm
3. Dip cleaned wiped cranberries in the egg white
4. Drain in a colander
5. Roll in heated sugar until all are white and coated with sugar
6. Keep in a warm place until the sugar has stuck to the cranberries

title: Sokeroituja makeita perunoita (Candied Sweet Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- lemon juice or vinegar (optional)

## Directions

1. Boil potatoes, peel, cut in halves lengthwise.
2. Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a frying pan.
3. Brown the potatoes sprinkled with 2 tablespoons of sugar.
4. Sprinkle with lemon juice or vinegar if preferred.

title: Sokeroitut Appelsiinin Kuoret (Candied Orange Peels)
body:
## Ingredients

- orange peels
- sugar equal in weight to orange peels
- 1 cupful water to 1 pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Soak peels 6-7 days in cold water changing the water every day
2. Cook until tender, place to drain
3. Have equal weights of orange peels and sugar, and allow 1 cupful of water to 1 pound of sugar
4. Cook to a thick syrup
5. Mix with orange peels and continue cooking until the peels are clear and the liquid is cooked away
6. Use for puddings and to garnish cakes

title: Sokuri lankaa (Spun Sugar)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of sugar
- 1 cupful of water
- 1/4 teaspoonful of cream of tartar

## Directions

1. Put 2 cupfuls of sugar in a saucepan with 1 cupful of water and 1/4 teaspoonful of cream of tartar.
2. Place on fire stirring until the sugar has dissolved.
3. Boil without stirring until the sugar gets firm when dipped in cold water, and cracks when broken.
4. Put the saucepan in cold water to stop boiling.
5. Put dipper or two forks in syrup and spin sugar from them over a stick, shaking them quickly back and forth.
6. Remove the spun sugar carefully from the stick and use to garnish desserts.
7. Keep well when covered with a bowl.

title: Sokuri Lastuja (Sugar Wafers)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lb. of butter
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1/2 lb. of eggs

## Directions

1. Melt butter and mix with sugar.
2. Rub in a bowl until white.
3. Stir slowly eggs alternately with flour into it, whipping well.
4. Place a thin layer of batter on a well greased sheet and bake in a moderate oven.
5. Cut into pieces 3 inches long and 1 inch wide and roll them hot around a stick.
6. Alternatively, form the batter into round thin cookies and roll together while hot.

title: Sokuri Leipiä (Sugar Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 3/4 teaspoonful of vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat whites of eggs to a froth.
2. Whip yolks with sugar until light.
3. Mix yolks with vanilla and flour.
4. Lastly stir the beaten whites into it.
5. Drop with spoon on greased baking sheet and sprinkle thinly with sugar.
6. Bake in a slow oven to a light yellow.

title: Sokuri munkkia (Sugar Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cupfuls of water
- 1/2 pound of butter
- 1/2 pound of wheat flour

## Directions

1. Boil up 4 cupfuls of water
2. Add 1/2 pound of butter into it
3. Reheat to boiling
4. Add 1/2 pound of wheat flour
5. Beat the mixture well until it leaves the sides of the pot

title: Sorsa tomaatin kanssa (Wild Duck with Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 wild duck
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 small onion sliced
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 cup water
- 3 cups tomato
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the duck in nice pieces, dip in flour and brown with butter and onion.
2. Add the water and the tomato, continue cooking for half an hour covered.
3. Remove the duck from the pan, strain the drippings, season.
4. Arrange on a platter, pour the gravy over.
5. Serve with rice.

title: Sorsa tomaatin kera (Wild Duck with Tomato)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 wild duck
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 small onion sliced
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 cupful of water
- 3 cupfuls of tomatoes
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the wild duck in nice pieces, dip in flour and fry in butter with onion.
2. Add water and tomatoes, cook tightly covered about 1/2 hour.
3. Remove the duck, strain the gravy, season with salt and pepper.
4. Arrange the duck on a serving dish, pour over it the gravy and serve with rice.

title: Sorsan-paisti (Roast Wild Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 wild ducks
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 teaspoonfuls of salt
- 1/2 quart of meat stock
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the ducks, let stand in water mixed with vinegar.
2. Boil quickly.
3. Wipe dry, rub with salt, brown in a pan with a lump of butter.
4. Pour over it little by little the boiling meat stock.
5. Roast about an hour basting often.
6. Serve very hot.
7. Strain the gravy, skim off the fat and season with lemon.
8. Thicken with flour if wanted.

title: Sorsapaisti (Roast Wild Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 wild ducks
- 3 tablespoons butter
- 2 teaspoons salt
- 1½ quarts bouillon or water
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Clean the wild ducks, let stand in water mixed with vinegar, or parboil.
2. Wipe and rub with salt, brown in butter in a dripping-pan.
3. Add boiling bouillon to it when necessary.
4. Roast for an hour basting often.
5. Serve very hot.
6. Strain the drippings, skim off the fat and flavor with lemon.

title: Sosetta makeista perunoista (Mashed Sweet Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet potatoes
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Scalded milk or cream

## Directions

1. Boil or bake the potatoes
2. Scoop out the insides and mash
3. Add pepper, salt, butter and scalded milk or cream until of right consistency
4. Rub to a smooth paste

title: Sosetta Talvi-Kyssäkaalista (Mashed Winter Squash)
body:
## Ingredients

- winter squash
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- milk or cream

## Directions

1. Rinse the squash, cut in large pieces and remove the seeds
2. Put in oven in a frying-pan covered for 2 hours, or if uncovered add a dot of butter to prevent from drying
3. Remove the inside with a spoon, mash
4. Add butter, salt, pepper, milk or cream
5. Rub well
6. Alternatively: peel, remove the seeds, cut in pieces, put in boiling salt water, cook until tender, mash and prepare as stated above

title: Sour Milk Griddle Cakes
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of flour
- 1½ cupfuls of sour milk
- ½ teaspoonful of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Prepare and bake as griddle cakes.

title: Sour Milk Griddle Cakes (Hapanmaito pannukakkuja)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of flour
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of sour milk
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Prepare and bake in the same way as the preceding griddle cakes.

title: Spaghetti
body:
## Ingredients

- spaghetti
- salted water
- cheese crumbs or various condiments

## Directions

1. Cook spaghetti until soft in salted water.
2. Serve with cheese crumbs or the same condiments as macaroni.

title: Spaghetti Timbale
body:
## Ingredients

- long pieces of fine macaroni or spaghetti
- salted water
- butter
- cooked chicken or veal, chopped
- milk sauce
- 3 or 4 beaten eggs
- buttered paper

## Directions

1. Cook long pieces of fine macaroni or spaghetti in salted water.
2. Put in cold water to keep them whole.
3. Place on a straight cloth to cool.
4. Butter well a dome shaped mold.
5. Put the macaroni lengthwise around the inside of the mold.
6. Fill the mold with cooked chicken or veal chopped and mixed with milk sauce.
7. Add 3 or 4 beaten eggs just before putting into the oven.
8. Bake in a pan of water, covered with a buttered paper, about 30 minutes.

title: Spaghetti-timboli (Spaghetti Timbale)
body:
## Ingredients

- long pieces of macaroni or spaghetti
- salt
- cooked chicken or veal
- milk sauce
- 3 or 4 eggs
- butter for molds

## Directions

1. Parboil in salted water long pieces of macaroni or spaghetti.
2. Put into cold water to keep them whole.
3. Place on a straight cloth to cool.
4. Butter a ring-shaped mold and line with the macaroni having the pieces lengthwise in the mold.
5. Fill the mold with cooked chicken or veal chopped and mixed with milk sauce.
6. Add 3 or 4 well beaten eggs shortly before putting the mold into the oven.
7. Bake for 30 minutes in oven in a pan of water covered with buttered paper.

title: Spaghetti-Timboli (Spaghetti Timbale)
body:
## Ingredients

- lunghi pezzi di fini maccheroni o spaghetti (long pieces of fine macaroni or spaghetti)
- brodo salato (salted water)
- carne di pollo cotta e finemente tritata oppure carne di vitello (cooked chicken or veal, finely minced)
- salsa di latte (milk sauce)
- 3 o 4 uova ben montate (3 or 4 eggs, well beaten)

## Directions

1. Cook long pieces of fine macaroni or spaghetti in salted water.
2. Place in cold water so they remain whole.
3. Set on a clean cloth to cool.
4. Butter a mold with a hole in the center well.
5. Place macaroni lengthwise around the mold.
6. Fill the mold with cooked and minced chicken or veal mixed with milk sauce.
7. Just before putting in the oven, add 3 or 4 well beaten eggs.
8. Bake in oven in a pan of water, covered with buttered paper, about 30 minutes.

title: Spanish Omelet
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of chopped onion
- 1 tablespoonful of skinned and cut red and green pepper
- 1½ cupfuls of tomatoes
- 2 tablespoonfuls of chopped mushrooms
- 1 tablespoonful of caper
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook onion and pepper in butter, add tomato, and let simmer gently, covered, 15 minutes
2. Mix with mushrooms, caper and salt
3. Prepare the plain omelet, pour the mixture on it, and serve immediately

title: Spinach Pudding
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of spinach leaves
- 3/4 cupful of rice
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- 1 pint of water
- 1 quart of milk
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Clean and cook spinach in salted water with a little soda
2. Drain the water and chop the spinach
3. Cook rice, water and milk until a thick paste
4. Stir the chopped spinach into it and mix with butter, sugar, salt and eggs
5. Pour in a buttered form and bake in oven about 30-40 minutes
6. Serve with meat

title: Spinach Soufflé
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of spinach leaves
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 3 cupfuls of milk
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of bread crumbs
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 3 eggs
- a pinch of nutmeg

## Directions

1. Clean and cook spinach as directed above and chop them
2. Mash with butter, flour, milk and spinach stock
3. Add bread crumbs soaked in milk, sugar, salt and nutmeg
4. Lastly add eggs well beaten
5. Butter a mould and pour the mixture in
6. Bake in oven about 1 hour in a pan of boiling water
7. Serve with meat and fish

title: Spinach Timbale
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of boiled spinach, rubbed through a sieve
- 2 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- 1 tablespoonful of salt
- 1 teaspoonful of sugar
- whites of 2 hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Add melted butter, salt, sugar and last of all whites of 2 hard boiled eggs to the spinach
2. Bake in a buttered mould in a pan of water

title: Sponge Cake I
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1 cupful of flour
- 4 tablespoonfuls of corn starch
- 4 tablespoonfuls of water
- 1 tablespoonful of brandy
- 1 teaspoonful of baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with sugar until light
2. Add water, brandy, baking powder mixed and sifted with flour and corn starch
3. At the last add the stiff beaten whites of eggs

title: Sponge Cake II
body:
## Ingredients

- yolks of 12 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon juice
- whites of 12 eggs
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Beat yolks of eggs with sugar until light
2. Add lemon juice and stiffly beaten whites of eggs
3. Lastly stir flour slightly into it
4. Put in a buttered mold and bake about one hour

title: Sponge Cake with Sour Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1/4 lb. of sugar
- 1/2 cupful of finely ground sour bread
- 1/2 cupful of flour
- 1/4 teaspoonful of vanilla

## Directions

1. Rub the yolks of eggs and sugar until light
2. Add seasonings, ground bread, flour and whites of eggs beaten to a stiff froth
3. Pour the mixture in a buttered mold and bake in a moderate oven 3/4-1 hour

title: Squab à la Florence
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 squabs
- 3 ozs. of marrow
- 4 ozs. of butter
- 2 ozs. of bread crumbs
- 1 egg
- salt and pepper
- chopped parsley
- fried slices of artichokes
- cream

## Directions

1. Clean squabs.
2. Mince the marrow, mixed with butter and rub well.
3. Add the other ingredients.
4. Stuff the squabs, and bake 30 minutes, basting often.
5. Arrange on fried slices of artichokes.
6. Skim the gravy and thicken with a little cream.

title: Stewed Beans
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- cold water
- beef stock
- 3 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar
- onion juice
- juice of half a lemon
- ½ cupful of beef stock

## Directions

1. Soak the beans in cold water over night
2. Put cold water on the fire, strain off the water after an hour
3. Pour beef stock over the beans, cook until tender
4. Prepare a sauce of 3 tablespoonfuls of butter, 2 tablespoonfuls of flour, 2 tablespoonfuls of sugar, a little onion juice and juice of half a lemon, ½ cupful of beef stock
5. Pour the sauce over the beans
6. Let still rest on the fire 10 minutes

title: Stewed Lobster
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of boiled lobster
- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 slice of onion, chopped
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 cupful of milk
- salt
- paprika
- sherry
- 2 egg yolks
- 1/2 cupful of cream

## Directions

1. Cook chopped onion in butter.
2. Add flour.
3. Add lobster.
4. Season with salt and paprika and bring slowly to boiling point.
5. Before serving add a little sherry and yolks of 2 eggs mixed with 1/2 cupful of cream to it.
6. Shake on the fire for a little while.

title: Stewed Macaroni
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound of macaroni
- 1/2 onion
- 1/2 green pepper
- 1 tiny carrot
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 quart of fresh cut tomatoes
- 1/2 cupful of water
- salt
- 4 tablespoonfuls of chopped mushrooms
- 2 tablespoonfuls of grated cheese

## Directions

1. Cook 1/2 pound of macaroni cut in pieces in salted water and drain.
2. Fry 1/2 onion, 1/2 green pepper and 1 tiny carrot with 2 tablespoonfuls of butter until brown.
3. Add 2 tablespoonfuls of flour and stir thoroughly.
4. Add 1 quart of fresh cut tomatoes and 1/2 cupful of water.
5. Season with salt and let simmer 30-40 minutes.
6. Strain and mix 4 tablespoonfuls of chopped mushrooms and let simmer 5 minutes.
7. Add 2 tablespoonfuls of grated cheese.
8. Pour the mixture over the boiled macaroni.

title: Stewed Oysters (Muhennettu oisteri)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint of oysters
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1/2 cupful of cream
- yolks of 2 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- lemon
- toast or patty shells
- mushrooms (optional)

## Directions

1. Put 1 pint of oysters into kettle and parboil in their own liquor
2. Cook 2 tablespoonfuls of butter with 1 tablespoonful of flour
3. Add 1/2 cupful of oyster liquor and 1/2 cupful of cream
4. Put the oysters into it and place to heat
5. Add 2 beaten yolks of eggs
6. Season with salt, pepper and lemon
7. Serve on toast or in patty shells
8. Stewed oysters may be mixed with chopped mushrooms

title: Stewed Spinach
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of spinach leaves
- 4 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 2 tablespoonfuls of flour
- 1 cupful of cream or milk
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- salt
- 1/2 teaspoonful of soda
- sugar

## Directions

1. Remove the roots and spoiled leaves
2. Cook in water mixed with salt and 1/2 teaspoonful of soda
3. Drain off the water and chop the spinach fine
4. Put the butter and flour in kettle and add the chopped spinach
5. Add spinach stock, milk, salt and sugar
6. Let the stew boil 10 minutes
7. Pour in the dish and garnish with hard boiled eggs
8. Serve with meat or fish

title: Stewed Tripe
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoonful of butter
- 1 tablespoonful of flour
- 1 cupful of milk
- salt
- pepper
- 1 teaspoonful of onion juice
- 1 cupful of boiled tripe

## Directions

1. Make a sauce of 1 tablespoonful of butter, 1 tablespoonful of flour and 1 cupful of milk
2. Season with salt, pepper and 1 teaspoonful of onion juice
3. Cut boiled tripe in dice and mix with sauce
4. Stir over the fire until the pieces of tripe are thoroughly done

title: Strawberry Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of strawberries
- 5 yolks of eggs
- 1/4 pound of sugar
- 1 cupful of bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Mash 1 quart of strawberries
2. Beat 5 yolks of eggs with sugar
3. mix with the mashed strawberries
4. Add 1 cupful of bread crumbs
5. At the last mix with the beaten whites of eggs
6. Bake in an individual mold
7. Serve cold with cream

title: Strawberry Cake (Kylmänä)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cupful of butter
- 1 1/2 cupfuls of sugar
- 3 beaten yolks
- 1/2 cupful of milk
- 2 cupfuls of flour
- 1/2 teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- 1/4 teaspoonful of soda
- 3 hard beaten whites

## Directions

1. Work 1/2 cupful of butter, mixed with 1 1/2 cupfuls of sugar, to a cream
2. add 3 beaten yolks, 1/2 cupful of milk, 2 cupfuls of flour, 1/2 teaspoonful of cream of tartar, 1/4 teaspoonful of soda, and lastly 3 hard beaten whites
3. Butter 3 low pans, pour the batter into them and bake in oven
4. When cold, cover cakes with whipped cream, mixed with a little sugar and vanilla
5. place strawberries, split in two, on and sprinkle with sugar
6. Pile the layers on a dish and serve immediately

title: Strawberry Ice
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of water
- 2 1/2 cupfuls of sugar
- 1 quart of strawberries
- 2 tablespoonfuls of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cook water and sugar
2. Press strawberries through a cloth
3. Mix with syrup, flavor with lemon juice and freeze

title: Strawberry Ice Cream I
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of cream
- 1 basket of strawberries
- 1½ cupfuls of sugar
- 1 tablespoonful of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Wash strawberries and let stand in sugar 1 hour.
2. Mash and force through a cloth.
3. Add cream and lemon juice.
4. Freeze.

title: Strawberry Ice Cream II
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of cream
- 1 basket of strawberries
- 2 cupfuls of sugar
- 1 teaspoonful of vanilla
- juice of ½ lemon
- 4 yolks of eggs
- a little salt

## Directions

1. Put ½ pint of cream or milk on the fire.
2. Beat ½ cupful of sugar with yolks.
3. Pour the boiling milk slowly on and whip the mixture in a double-boiler on the fire until it is thick.
4. When cool, add strawberries, washed and mixed with the rest of the sugar, pressed through a sieve.
5. Add vanilla and lemon juice into the mixture.
6. Then freeze.

title: Strawberry Pie
body:
## Ingredients

- pie crust
- split strawberries
- sugar
- juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Cover the piedish with crust
2. Fill with split strawberries, soaked an hour in sugar and juice of half a lemon
3. Place the upper crust on
4. Bake in oven

title: Strawberry Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1/2 cupful of water
- 2 cupfuls of strawberry puree pressed through a sieve
- a little vanilla

## Directions

1. Boil sugar and water 10 minutes.
2. When it has cooled, add strawberry puree pressed through a sieve and a little vanilla.

title: Strawberry Short Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of flour
- 3 teaspoonfuls of baking powder
- 1/4 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/4 cupful of sugar
- 3/8 cupful of milk
- 1/2 cupful of butter
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Mix all dry ingredients in a bowl
