- 2 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Sift flour with baking powder and salt.
2. Beat yolks of eggs, add milk and pour flour slowly into it.
3. Mix with melted butter.
4. Lastly mix with hard-beaten whites of eggs.
5. Fry on hot greased waffle iron to a nice brown on both sides.

title: Vohveleita II (Waffles II)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cupfuls of cream
- 2 eggs
- 6 heaping tablespoonfuls of flour
- 4 tablespoonfuls of sugar

## Directions

1. Beat cream to a stiff froth.
2. Whip eggs with sugar to a froth, mix with cream.
3. Add flour and let stand for 1 hour.
4. Heat waffle-iron, butter it and fry waffles to a delicate brown on both sides.
5. Sprinkle with sugar and serve with jam.

title: Voi Rinkeliä (Butter Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 yolks of eggs
- 3/4 cupful of sugar
- 3/4 cupful of rich cream
- 10 cardamom seeds
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 3 cupfuls of flour
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Whip yolks of eggs, sugar and cream to a thick froth.
2. Add cardamom, soda and flour enough to be rolled on board.
3. Roll into a thin sheet, cut into strips.
4. Form them into small flat rings.
5. Bake in oven or cook in fat.
6. Sprinkle with sugar.

title: Voi-laserrus (Butter Frosting)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 cup confectioner's sugar
- cream, milk or water
- lemon, orange, chocolate or vanilla for flavoring

## Directions

1. Rub 2 tablespoons butter with 1 cup confectioner's sugar until creamy
2. Add cream, milk or water
3. Flavor with lemon, orange, chocolate or vanilla

title: Voikakku (Butter Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 glassful eggs
- 1 glassful powdered sugar
- 1/2 glassful melted butter
- 1 glassful flour
- Vanilla or lemon flavor

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with the sugar for about 1/2 hour
2. Add gradually butter and flour
3. Pour the mixture into a buttered mold
4. Bake 3/4 hour

title: Voikakku (Butter Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 glassful of eggs
- 1 glassful of sugar
- 3/4 glassful of melted butter
- 1 1/2 glassfuls of flour

## Directions

1. Whip eggs and sugar to a froth, mix with butter and flour.
2. Pour the mixture into a buttered mold and bake 1/2 - 3/4 hour.
3. May be used for fruit layer cakes.

title: Voikakkuja (Butter Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 cup sugar
- 2 tablespoons lemon juice
- 2 egg yolks or 1 whole egg
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- 6 or 7 cups flour

## Directions

1. Rub until creamy butter with sugar
2. Work gradually into the butter eggs, lemon juice and flour mixed with baking powder
3. Roll out
4. Cut in small cookies
5. Brush with egg white
6. Add a sprinkling of sugar and almonds
7. Bake until light brown

title: Voikastike (Butter Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1 tablespoonful of minced parsley

## Directions

1. Cream well the butter
2. Add juice of 1/2 lemon and minced parsley
3. Pour on broiled meat or fish
4. The sauce may be served without parsley with boiled asparagus and artichokes

title: Voikastike (Maitre d'Hotel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons butter
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cream well 2 tablespoons of butter
2. Add the juice of half a lemon and 1 tablespoon of chopped parsley
3. Serve with broiled meat or fish having the sauce on top
4. The sauce may be made without parsley and served with asparagus and artichokes

title: Voileipiä Kuivista Hedelmistä (Sandwiches Made of Dried Fruit)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal parts of dates
- plums
- figs
- raisins
- chopped walnuts
- lemon juice
- sugar
- water
- brown bread

## Directions

1. Stone and chop all dried fruit
2. Mix with chopped walnuts and lemon juice
3. Serve with brown bread
4. Alternatively, cook chopped fruit with sugar in small quantity of water into a thick marmalade, boiling slowly to prevent it from burning
5. Use approximately 1 pound of fruit to 1 cup of water and 1 cup of sugar

title: Voileipiä kuivista hedelmistä (Sandwiches made of Dried Fruit)
body:
## Ingredients

- dates
- prunes
- figs
- raisins
- lemon juice
- nuts
- brown bread

## Directions

1. Take equal amounts of dates, prunes, figs and raisins.
2. Remove the stones and chop the fruit.
3. Dilute with lemon juice.
4. Chop nuts and mix in if desired.
5. Serve with brown bread.
6. Alternatively, the chopped fruit may be cooked in a little water with sugar to make marmalade, using about 1 pound of fruit to 1 cupful of water and 1 cupful of sugar, cooking gently.

title: Voileipiä täytettyjen munien kanssa (Sandwiches with Filled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- anchovy paste
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- parsley
- toasted bread slices
- mayonnaise

## Directions

1. Cut a slice off both ends of the eggs.
2. Cut the eggs in two, take the yolks.
3. Mix yolks with anchovy paste and butter, season with salt and pepper.
4. Fill the whites to a heap with this mixture.
5. Sprinkle with parsley.
6. Place on toast.
7. Serve having mayonnaise around, if desired.

title: Voileipiä ytimen kanssa (Marrow on Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- marrow bone
- flour
- water
- salt
- pepper
- preserved onions
- parsley
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cover the holes of the marrow-bone with hard dough made of flour and water so as to prevent the marrow running out while boiling.
2. Put the bone into boiling water and let boil 1 or 2 hours.
3. Spread marrow on toast and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
4. Keep the toast in oven for 2 or 3 minutes and serve very hot.
5. Alternatively, mix the marrow with preserved onions and chopped parsley, season with salt and pepper, and flavor with lemon juice before spreading on toast.

title: Voirinkeliä (Butter Rings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg yolks
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 3/4 cup whipping cream
- 1 teaspoon soda
- 10 cardamom seeds
- 3 cups flour

## Directions

1. Beat to a thick froth the yolks with sugar and cream
2. Add cardamom, soda and flour enough to make a paste thick enough to be rolled on the board
3. Roll thin
4. Cut into strips
5. Form them into tiny flat rings
6. Bake in an oven or cook in fat
7. Sprinkle with sugar

title: Voissa Paistettua Ankeriasta (Eel Baked in Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- butter or lard
- lemon juice
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the eel and cut in pieces two inches long.
2. If small use the fishes whole.
3. Boil with salt and pepper.
4. Dry and roll in flour.
5. Fry in butter or lard until nicely browned.
6. Arrange on a platter and pour over the butter in which they were cooked, mixed with a little lemon juice.
7. Garnish with fine parsley.

title: Voissa paistettua ankeriasta (Fried Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- Eel
- Salt
- Pepper
- Flour
- Butter or lard
- Lemon juice
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the fish, cut in pieces two inches thick (if small, use them whole).
2. Parboil with salt and pepper.
3. Wipe, roll in flour.
4. Fry in butter or lard until nice and brown.
5. Arrange on a platter, pour the drippings over, add some lemon juice.
6. Strew with chopped parsley.
7. Or: Dip the fish in egg, roll in bread crumbs, cook in lard.
8. Serve with tomato or tartar sauce.

title: Voitaikina (Puff Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound flour
- 1 egg
- 1 cup water
- 1 teaspoon gin
- juice of lemon
- 1/2 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Wash the butter in ice water and force the water out by wrapping the butter tightly into a towel.
2. Whip the egg, add water, lemon juice, gin and flour mixed with 1 teaspoon of butter.
3. Knead with fingers to an even paste.
4. Roll, place on top of the paste the butter flattened to a cake.
5. Fold up the sheet of paste so as to enclose the butter.
6. Place on ice in a dish for 1 hour.
7. Roll the paste into an oblong square and fold in three.
8. Turn over, reroll and fold in three again.
9. Repeat this operation three times rolling out twice and folding up three times each time.
10. Place the paste on ice for 15 minutes between each operation.
11. Rerolling must be done in the opposite direction.
12. Bake in a hot oven.

title: Voitaikina (Puff Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 lb. of flour
- 1 egg
- 1 cupful of water
- 1 teaspoonful of alcohol
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 teaspoonful of salt

## Directions

1. Wash butter in icewater and force the water out by wrapping tightly the butter into a towel.
2. Whip egg, mix with water, lemon juice, alcohol and sifted flour, mixed with 1 teaspoonful of butter.
3. Knead with fingers to an even paste.
4. Roll, place butter, pressed into a flat cake, on the paste.
5. Fold up the sheet of paste to inclose the butter.
6. Put on ice for 3/4 hour.
7. Roll into an oblong square and triple.
8. Turn over and reroll.
9. Triple again.
10. Repeat this operation three times, rolling out twice and folding up three times each time.
11. Set the paste on ice for 15 minutes between each operation.
12. Rerolling has to be made in the opposite direction.
13. Bake in hot oven.

title: Voitaikina avonaisille paille (Pastry for Open Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of flour
- 1 cupful of butter
- yolk of 1 egg
- 1/2 cupful of water
- a pinch of salt and sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare as puff paste
2. Roll the paste into a crust 1/8 inch thick
3. Lay over the bottom of a pie-plate
4. Perforate and moisten the edges
5. Cut a string of the paste to fasten the crust around the wet pie-plate
6. Spread with egg and bake, placing the plate on a dish, to a light brown
7. Remove the plate carefully and put the pastry into the plate to get cold

title: Voitaikina avonaisille piirakoille (Pastry for Open Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups flour
- 1 cup butter
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/2 cup water
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare the same way as voitaikina (puff paste).
2. Roll the paste thin into a round cake.
3. Place outside the pie mold, perforate with holes around and moisten the edges with water.
4. Cut a narrow long strip from the paste, which you place along the moistened edge.
5. Brush with egg.
6. Place the mold on some vessel in the oven and bake until light brown.
7. Remove the crust neatly from the mold and place inside the mold to cool.
8. Alternatively: place the rolled paste in the mold and let the paste hang over the edge about one inch wide.
9. Turn the paste inside the edge, perforate a few holes in the bottom, place thin paper on the crust and fill the mold with rice or dry peas so that the paste cannot rise unevenly.
10. Brush the edge with egg and bake at regular oven heat.
11. When the crust is done remove the rice and paper.

title: Voitaikina piirakoihin (Pastry for Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/4 cups flour
- 1/2 cup butter or lard
- 1/4 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/2 cup cold water
- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- sugar and salt

## Directions

1. Mix the flour with butter, sugar, salt and baking powder. Work thoroughly with fingers.
2. Dilute the egg with the water, add to the flour stirring well.
3. Keep in a cold place.

title: Voitaikina päihin (Pastry for Pies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of flour
- 1/2 cupful of butter
- yolk of 1 egg
- 3/4 cupful of water
- 1 teaspoonful of lemon juice
- a little sugar and salt

## Directions

1. Mix salt and sugar with flour
2. Add butter to the flour same as directed in last recipe
3. Mix egg yolk with water
4. Work with fingers into the flour
5. Put in a cold place for a little while

title: Voitaikina-kuoriaiset (Patty Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- puff paste
- egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Roll puff paste to a 1/2 inch thick sheet.
2. Cut in round cakes with a patty cutter.
3. Remove the center of half of the cakes with a smaller cutter.
4. Moisten the edges of the whole cakes, place the hollowed cakes on top, press together.
5. Keep in a cold place until they become hard.
6. Brush with egg (not the edges).
7. Bake in a hot oven for 20 minutes.
8. Bake the small circles cut out of the center of the cakes to be used as covers for the filled shells.
9. Remove the soft paste from the inside of the shells.

title: Volkakkuja (Butter Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lb. of butter
- 1 glassful of sugar
- 1 egg
- 2 lbs. of flour

## Directions

1. Wash butter and work it to a cream
2. Mix with sugar and continue rubbing a little while
3. Stir slowly egg and flour into the butter
4. Roll paste and shape into small cookies
5. Spread with egg, sprinkle with sugar and chopped almonds
6. Bake in oven to a light yellow

title: Väskynähyytelö (Prune Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup prunes
- water
- 1/2 package gelatine
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 lemon juice
- sherry wine or orange juice
- cream for serving
- ground almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Soak 1 cup prunes in cold water
2. Cook until tender
3. Drain, pass through a sieve and grind finely or through a meat grinder
4. Add to the prune liquid 1/2 package dissolved gelatine, 1 cup sugar, 1 lemon juice and a little sherry wine or orange juice
5. Stir over the fire until the gelatine is dissolved, then strain
6. Add the prunes and let set, stirring occasionally until it begins to jelly so that the fruit does not settle to the bottom
7. Serve with cream
8. Ground almonds used in the mixture give a good flavor

title: Väskynähyytelö (Prune Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of prunes
- cold water
- ½ box of dissolved gelatine
- 1 cupful of sugar
- 1 lemon juice
- sherry wine

## Directions

1. Soak 1 cupful of prunes in cold water.
2. Cook until soft, strain and chop fine.
3. Add to the prune liquor ½ box of dissolved gelatine, 1 cupful of sugar, 1 lemon juice and a little sherry wine.

title: Väskynähyytelö (Whipped Cranberry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of cranberries
- 1/2 box of gelatine
- 1 cupful of sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- a little sherry wine

## Directions

1. Soak 1 cupful of cranberries in cold water
2. Cook until tender
3. Strain and chop fine
4. Add into the liquor of cranberries 1/2 box of gelatine, soaked in water
5. Add 1 cupful of sugar, juice of 1 lemon and a little sherry wine
6. Stir over the fire until the gelatine is thoroughly dissolved
7. Strain and mix with the cranberries
8. Let set, stirring now and then until it begins to thicken so as to mix the fruit evenly with the liquid jelly
9. Serve with cream
10. Chopped almonds make a fine flavor to it

title: Väskynät-hyytelö (Prune Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup prunes
- 1/2 package gelatine, dissolved
- 1 cup sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- some sherry or orange juice
- chopped almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Soak 1 cup prunes in cold water.
2. Cook until tender.
3. Drain, chop or grind.
4. Add to the liquid of the prunes, 1/2 package of dissolved gelatine, 1 cup sugar, the juice of 1 lemon, some sherry or orange juice.
5. Stir over the fire until the gelatine is dissolved.
6. Strain; add the prunes.
7. Let set, stirring now and then until it begins to thicken, so the fruit does not settle to the bottom.
8. Chopped almonds will greatly improve the taste.
9. Serve with cream.

title: Walnut Cake
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 eggs
- 6 ozs. of sugar
- ½ cupful of butter
- ¾ cupful of milk
- 1 cupful of walnut meat, chopped with the thin brown skin
- 2 cupfuls of flour
- 2 teaspoonfuls of baking powder

## Directions

1. Work butter and sugar to a froth
2. Add beaten yolks
3. Add milk, walnuts, baking powder, flour and then whites of eggs, beaten to a stiff froth

title: Water Bread
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of water
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 1/2 cake of yeast
- 6-7 cupfuls of flour
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve yeast in lukewarm water, add flour enough to make a very soft paste. Let rise.
2. Warm the rest of the water, pour in a bowl, mix with salt, butter and flour to prepare a soft paste.
3. Work the risen sponge into it and add the rest of the flour gradually, kneading until the dough is even.
4. Throw a towel over it and set in a warm place to rise.
5. When risen until light, toss the dough on a board, knead briskly and long, divide into loaves and put in buttered molds.
6. Cover with towel and let rise again.
7. Bake in a moderate oven 1 hour. If preferred, the sugar may be left out.

title: Water Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 5 cupfuls of water
- 5 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil water with butter.
2. Mix quickly with flour.
3. Stir over the fire until thick enough to easily remove from the pan.
4. Let cool.
5. Mix with eggs one by one.
6. Drop the mixture by heaping spoonfuls on a baking sheet.
7. Sprinkle thinly with sugar.
8. Bake in a moderate oven.
9. Serve when cold filled with whipped cream.

title: Water Puffs
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lb. of butter
- 1/2 lb. of flour
- 5 cupfuls of water
- 5 eggs
- sugar
- whipped cream
- vanilla
- jam

## Directions

1. Boil water with butter.
2. Mix quickly with flour.
3. Stir over the fire until thick enough to easily remove from the pan.
4. Let cool.
5. Mix with eggs one by one.
6. Drop the mixture by heaping spoonfuls on a baking sheet.
7. Sprinkle thinly with sugar.
8. Bake in a moderate oven.
9. Serve when cold filled with whipped cream mixed with sugar, vanilla and jam.

title: Whipped Cream Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup whipped cream
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla

## Directions

1. Add 1 tablespoon sugar and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla to a cup of whipped cream

title: White Cookies
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1/2 lb. of sugar
- 3/4 cupful of cream
- 1 teaspoonful of cream of tartar
- 1/2 lb. of melted butter
- 1 teaspoonful of lemon juice
- a little cinnamon and cardamom
- 1/2 lb. of corn starch
- flour

## Directions

1. Whip eggs with sugar to a froth
2. Mix with cream, cream of tartar, butter, spices, corn starch and lastly flour enough to make a dough tolerable to handle
3. Roll and shape small cookies
4. Bake in a slow oven

title: White Stock (Valkea Liemi)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fowl
- 3 lbs. of knuckles of veal
- 1 quart of water to each pound of meat and bones
- vegetables and seasonings

## Directions

1. Wash a fowl and knuckles of veal.
2. Allow 1 quart of water to each pound of meat and bones.
3. Place all in a kettle, cover and let it slowly come to the boiling point.
4. Add the vegetables and seasonings and let it slowly boil for 5 hours.
5. Strain and let cool uncovered.

title: Whole Wheat Muffins (Whole Wheat Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cups whole wheat flour
- ½ cup white flour
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- ½ teaspoon salt
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 tablespoons butter or shortening
- 1 cup milk
- 1 or 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix together the dry ingredients.
2. Beat the eggs with the sugar, add the milk, mix in the flour and the melted butter.
3. Stir well, bake in buttered muffin tins in a hot oven for 30 minutes.

title: Wiener Schnitzel
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal
- Salt and pepper
- Egg
- Bread crumbs
- Butter
- Lemon
- Anchovies
- Capers

## Directions

1. Cut veal crosswise into slices 1/2 inch thick
2. Pound lightly and trim into oval-shaped cakes
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, or dip in salted water
4. Dip in beaten egg and bread crumbs
5. Brown butter in a frying pan
6. Fry the slices of meat on both sides
7. Cover and leave on the range for a little while
8. Put a bit of butter under each slice
9. Place a slice of lemon on top
10. Spread boned anchovies with caper, roll together, and lay the rolls on the slices of lemon

title: Wiener Schnitzel (Wiener Schnitzel)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- salt
- pepper
- salted water
- egg
- bread crumbs
- butter
- lemon
- anchovy
- capers

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into 1/2 inch thick slices, pound and form into oval-shaped cakes.
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper or dip in salted water.
3. Dip in beaten egg and roll in bread crumbs.
4. Brown in butter on both sides.
5. Cover and leave on the heat for a while.
6. Arrange on a serving platter with a dot of butter under each piece and a slice of lemon on top.
7. Make rings of anchovies with capers inside and place on top of the lemon.

title: Windsor voileipiä (Windsor Canapes)
body:
## Ingredients

- slices of bread
- butter
- steak
- ham
- tongue
- grated cheese
- mustard
- pepper
- pickled cucumbers
- capers
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Brown slices of bread on both sides in butter.
2. Chop or grind steak, ham and tongue.
3. Mix with grated cheese, butter, a little mustard and pepper.
4. Stir until smooth.
5. Lay on the toast.
6. Garnish with chopped pickles, capers and hard-boiled egg.

title: Windsor-voileipiä (Windsor Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread slices
- butter
- steak
- ham
- tongue
- cheese
- mustard
- pepper
- mayonnaise
- capers
- hard boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Fry slices of bread in butter on both sides.
2. Force equal amounts of steak, ham and tongue through the meat chopper.
3. Mix with equal amount of cheese, add a little butter, season with mustard and pepper.
4. Rub until smooth.
5. Spread on the fried slices of bread.
6. Decorate the top with mayonnaise, caper and hard boiled egg.

title: Wine Sauce
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cupful of water
- 1/2 cupful of sherry
- 1/2 cupful of white wine or madeira
- 1 lemon's yellow rind
- 1-2 tablespoonfuls of arrowroot flour
- 1/2 cupful of sugar

## Directions

1. Mix sugar and arrowroot.
2. Pour boiling water slowly into it, stirring well.
3. Boil until the mixture is clear.
4. Add wines and lemon yellow rind.
5. The sauce can also be thickened with a few egg yolks.

title: Ydinkokkareita (Marrow Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of marrow
- 1/2 cupful of milk
- Wheat bread flour
- 2 eggs
- Salt and pepper
- Parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Melt 2 tablespoonfuls of marrow and strain through a cloth.
2. Let cool for a little while, then rub to a cream.
3. Scald 1/2 cupful of milk and add bread crumbs until absorbed.
4. Mix with 2 eggs thickened with bread crumbs.
5. Season with salt and pepper and sprinkle with parsley.
6. Add the bread mixture slowly to the marrow, rubbing well.
7. Test the mixture; if too thin, thicken with bread crumbs.
8. Form into tiny balls and roll in flour.
9. Cook in salt water.
10. Serve with meat soup.

title: Ydinkokkareita (Marrow Balls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoonfuls of marrow
- 1/2 cupful of milk
- bread crumbs
- 2 eggs
- salt, pepper and minced parsley

## Directions

1. Melt the marrow and strain it through a cloth
2. Let cool a little while, then cream it
3. Boil 1/2 cupful of milk and add bread crumbs until absorbed
4. Mix 2 eggs with bread crumbs and stir into the milk mixture
5. Season with salt, pepper and minced parsley
6. Add this mixture gradually to the creamed marrow, rubbing well
7. Test the mixture and add more bread crumbs if too soft
8. Form into round balls
9. Dip in flour
10. Cook in salted water
11. Serve with meat soup

title: Ydinluu (Marrow Bone)
body:
## Ingredients

- marrow bone
- salt water
- thick paste
- clean cloth
- bouillon
- toast
- parsley
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Saw the marrowbone into pieces 2-3 inches long.
2. Scrape the meat off carefully.
3. Leave the bones in cold water for 1/2 hour.
4. Close both ends with thick paste and tie a clean cloth around.
5. Place the bones in a saucepan in bouillon.
6. Cook slowly for 1 1/2 hours.
7. Remove the cloth and the paste.
8. Place the bones on toast.
9. Garnish around with parsley and lettuce leaves.
10. Serve very hot.

title: Ydinputinki (Marrow Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups bread crumbs
- 4 eggs
- 1 pint rich milk
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 6 ounces marrow
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1/4 pound almonds
- 1 cup raisins
- 1 cup cream

## Directions

1. Mix the eggs into the milk, add the bread crumbs and spices.
2. Chop the marrow fine, mix with flour.
3. Combine with the bread mixture, add raisins, almonds and sugar.
4. Pour into a buttered mold sprinkled with bread crumbs.
5. Bake for 1 hour.
6. Serve with lemon sauce.

title: Yksinkertainen kakku (Plain Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cups butter
- ¾ cup sugar
- ½ cup milk
- 2 eggs
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon vanilla
- 1½ cups flour

## Directions

1. Rub butter and sugar together until creamy.
2. Add egg yolks beaten to a froth.
3. Add milk, baking powder and flour sifted together.
4. Add vanilla.
5. Last, fold in stiffly beaten egg whites.
6. Beat the mixture well.
7. Bake for 30 minutes.
8. Dissolved chocolate mixed with milk or 2 tablespoons cocoa sifted with flour may be used in this cake.

title: Yksinkertainen omeletti (Plain Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons milk, cream or water
- 1 tablespoon butter
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs and add milk or water, salt and pepper.
2. Melt the butter in a hot omelet pan.
3. Turn the eggs into the pan.
4. Fry shaking the pan over the fire until firm.
5. Place the omelet on one side of the pan and brown quickly underneath.
6. Turn onto a hot platter and serve with or without sauce.

title: Yksinkertainen Riisiputinki (Plain Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups milk
- 1 tablespoon washed rice
- salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon vanilla
- stewed fruit or jam

## Directions

1. Pour 2 cups of milk into a mold, add 1 tablespoon of washed rice and a dash of salt
2. Bake in oven for 2 hours stirring often
3. Add 1 tablespoon sugar and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla when the pudding is half done
4. If the pudding becomes hard, add scalded milk
5. Serve hot or cold with stewed fruit or jam

title: Yksinkertainen sitruuna pai (Plain Lemon Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 eggs
- 1 1/2 lemon rind and juice
- 4 tablespoonfuls of water
- 3/4 cupful of sugar

## Directions

1. Beat yolks with sugar
2. Add lemon juice and rind, then water
3. Stir on the fire in double-boiler until thick
4. Let cool
5. Mix with stiff beaten whites
6. Bake on an open pie
7. When firm, cover with Meringue, if desired

title: Yksinkertainen Vanilja Kermajäätelö (Plain Vanilla Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of cream
- 3/4 cupful of sugar
- 1 vanilla bean or 1 teaspoonful of vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Scald cream and sugar on the fire.
2. Add flavoring when cool and freeze.
3. If rich cream is used, dilute it with a little milk.

title: Yksinkertainen Vanilja-Kermajäätelö (Plain Vanilla Ice Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart cream
- 3/4 cup sugar
- 1 vanilla bean or 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Scald the cream with the sugar.
2. When cool add vanilla and freeze.
3. If whipping cream is used, dilute it with some milk.

title: Yksinkertaisia Leivoksia (Plain Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 or 3/4 cup milk or water
- 2 cups flour
- 2 tablespoons butter or shortening
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 3 teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Sift the flour with salt and baking powder.
2. Work well with fingers the butter into the flour.
3. Add gradually milk enough to make a batter thick enough to handle.
4. Roll into 3/8 inch thick sheet, cut with a biscuit cutter into round cakes.
5. Place on a buttered baking sheet and bake in a hot oven for 15 minutes.
6. 1 egg may be added to the milk, if preferred.

title: Yksinkertaisia Leivoksia (Plain Biscuits)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1½ cupfuls of milk
- 4 cupfuls of flour
- 2 tablespoonfuls of butter or lard
- 1 teaspoonful of salt
- 3 teaspoonfuls of baking powder

## Directions

1. Sift flour mixed with salt and baking powder
2. Mix butter with flour carefully
3. Add milk slowly with knife or hand
4. Roll the dough into a sheet ½ inch thick
5. Shape into round cakes
6. Place on a buttered baking pan
7. Bake in a hot oven for 15 minutes

title: Yksinkertaista riisiputinkia (Plain Rice Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cupfuls of milk
- 1 tablespoonful of rice
- salt
- 1 tablespoonful of sugar
- 1/2 teaspoonful of vanilla

## Directions

1. Pour 2 cupfuls of milk in a mold, mix with 1 tablespoonful of rice and a little salt.
2. Bake in oven 2 hours, stirring often.
3. Add 1 tablespoonful of sugar and 1/2 teaspoonful of vanilla when pudding has been in oven one hour.
4. Add boiling milk if pudding appears stiff.

title: Äidin Piparkakkuja (Mother's Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- 3/4 cupful of molasses
- 6 tablespoonfuls of melted butter
- 1 teaspoonful of soda
- 1 teaspoonful of cinnamon
- 1 teaspoonful of pepper
- 1 teaspoonful of ginger
- flour to make a moderately thick batter

## Directions

1. Whip eggs add molasses, butter and spices, beating well, into it.
2. Mix soda with a little flour and stir it into the batter.
3. Add flour enough to make a moderately thick batter.
4. Roll thin, cut with cutter into small cookies.
5. Bake on greased sheet in a slow oven.

title: Äyriäis-omeletti (Shrimp Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup cooked shrimp meat
- butter
- 3 eggs
- 3 tablespoons milk or cream
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Heat 1/2 cup of cooked shrimps cut in pieces with butter in a saucepan.
2. Add 3 eggs beaten with 3 tablespoons of milk or cream.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Shake over the fire until firm.
5. Serve on a platter.

title: Äyriäis-omeletti Sieniä (Shrimp Omelet with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 cup mushrooms, chopped
- butter
- 1/2 cup cooked shrimps
- 1 to 3 eggs
- cream
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Fry 1/4 cup of chopped mushrooms in butter for 5 minutes.
2. Add shrimps and eggs mixed with cream.
3. Use 1 or 2 eggs more than in the basic recipe.
4. Shake over the fire until firm.
5. Serve on a platter.
